data.title: Executive Director, Corporate Banking Dubai & NE
data.jd_text: Join the UAE’s largest bank and one of the world’s largest and safest financial institutions. Our focus is to create value for our employees, customers, shareholders and communities to grow through differentiation, agility and innovation. We are looking for top talent and your success is our success. Accelerate your growth as you help us reach our goals and advance your career. Be ready to make your mark a top company, in an exciting & dynamic industry. Job Purpose To grow and manage large account portfolios and sensitive client relationships for assigned portfolio in the corporate banking segment; improve service quality levels and supervise the team’s day-to-day activities and client interactions to ensure that the team's objectives are constantly met and its revenue targets achieved, while maintaining client satisfaction and portfolio credit quality. Key Accountabilities Generic Accountability Client and Market Analysis Conduct financial analysis on clients and their respective geographies/ industries/ markets to identify potential opportunities and risks. Originate new business opportunities with target clients across banking products and in line with relevant policies. Obtain support from relevant stakeholders and governance forums to close deals (e.g., credit, products, GT). Develop Term Sheet, Information Memorandum, Mandate letter etc. Examine & resolve credit, market and operational risk aspects of the transactions in collaboration with products and enablement units. Leverage FAB’s network expansion through cross border deals. Anchor Strategic Alliances with UAE’s Top Developers & Authorities Expand Products Penetration Through Sector-Focused Solutions Ensure high returns with satisfactory yields Maintain the credit quality of the client's relationships within the Portfolio to an acceptable level set by the Bank's management. Pitch, review, negotiate and conclude deals. Develop and maintain strong relationships with key decision makers in other banks to originate business. Up to date with the latest market, competitors, regulatory and industry and amend accordingly the account plans as when applicable. Ability to handle sophisticated transactions and engage assigned team members. Recommend policy amendments as when required to match with the latest business trends. Business Origination Client Relationship Management To achieve assigned financial and non-financial objectives by meeting all borrowing & non borrowing needs of customers with target segments (E.g., borrowers, transactors, depositors). Prepare and Maintain Account Plans for all clients with support from relevant partners (e.g., products, international banking, Employee banking, Asset Management, etc.). Support on the conduct of annual/periodic credit facility reviews in the existing portfolio in a timely manner and support on the recommendation of appropriate credit facilities in line with the banks credit. policy to maximize earnings while containing risks to an acceptable level and identifying candidates for downgrades/ upgrades. Develop, Implement and maintain complete control aspects i.e., documentation, periodical client visits / call reports, stock inspections and formal credit checks / informal market feedback to monitor the assigned portfolio effectively. Organize client meetings with relevant partners/ seniority to deepen FAB relationship within the portfolio. Identify & develop cross selling opportunities and increase cross-sell ratio and returns with clients. Support client on their day-to-day banking requirements with support from the team (as applicable). Ensure customer satisfaction remains high and meets the targeted NPS score. Keep abreast of market trends and manage a disciplined marketing process by identifying steps/strategies necessary to effectively initiate relationships with prospects. Onboard new clients as and when required. Collaboration Align with Corporate team as well as other partners to identify and convert opportunities. Collaborate with other business divisions to drive cross-BU collaboration. Develop proposition strategy, competition analysis, focused group approach, service delivery model, customer experience, internal & external campaigns and alignment with other Products & proposition including Risk policies, Payment & Cash management, Trade products, collaboration with Retail banking including Private Banking, etc. Align with International banking to support client in FAB network locations. Support Unit/Divisional/Bank-wide initiatives (as required or assigned). Operational Follow all relevant policies, processes, standard operating procedures, and instructions so that work is carried out in a controlled and consistent manner. Conduct periodic reviews of risks (credit, operational, compliance etc.) within the portfolio on a regular basis. Drive day-to-day administrative and transactional activities for the team such as client calls, documentation follow up, pipeline updates, and any other activities

data.title: Executive Director, Head of Leverage Finance Syndicate & Distribution
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text: JOB PURPOSE: The role is to lead a newly established Leveraged Finance Syndicate & Distribution (LFSD) vertical under Loan Syndicate & Distribution (LSD). LSD provides critical market intelligence, loan underwriting and distribution skills in support of FAB’s GCF Loan Businesses. LSD work closely with the GCF Loan Origination (LO) teams to provide optimal financing solutions for FAB’s clients and delivering growth to the GCF’s loan product revenues. LFSD vertical covers leveraged and acquisition financing, Term Loan Bs, loan facilities linked to listings/delistings, share sales, NAV financings, fund finance, and Diversified Payment Rights (DPRs) With FAB’s increased efforts towards increasing wallet share in Leveraged Finance markets, this role carries the responsibility of using the reputation and market knowledge of the LSD team to support the growth ambitions. The purpose of the job is to - Identify loan underwriting and syndication opportunities in the leveraged finance space. Underwrite, bookrun and distribute syndiicatated loans Identify and execute secondary trade opportunities Manage execution and support business development leading to revenue growth and assist in origination of new loan facilities covered by LFSD through effective distribution Lead, review and supervise all market activity related to underwriting, primary syndication and secondary purchase and sales. KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES: Generic Accountability In coordination with the Head of LSD, develop the FAB’s LFSD capabilities while adapting to the competitive, regulatory and environmental factors impacting the industry and our clients Work with the Head of LSD to build and maintain relationships with the senior management of banks, financial institutions, private credit funds and CLO managers Provide quality inputs into pitches to win major leveraged financing mandates. Lead subsequent execution and distribution. Work with other LSD team members to support business plan, service the existing clients and establish new relationships Establish and foster strong working relationships with key internal stake holders (e.g. IB&M, WB and PCG Coverage, Credit, CAD, Legal and Compliance. etc.) Be responsible for the management of revenue, expenses and KPI targets for LFSD Assist in the implementation of new products as required Provide mentorship to junior members of the team, especially the UAENs under the Ethraa programme. Specific Accountability In coordination with Head of LSD, develop the overall strategy LFSD. Emphasis on the underwriting decisions and processes, primary syndication and secondary sale With guidance from Head of LSD and in coordination with other team members, development of an optimal coverage model for Loan Distribution – especially private credit funds and CLO managers, who are key investors to leveraged finance transaction - while, ensuring effectiveness in identifying business opportunities, addressing client needs, and sustaining profitable business growth for GCF Act as the go-to-person for leveraged finance transaction in FAB’s underwriting book and be responsible for taking and extinguishing underwriting risks within the bank’s approved framework and policies Lead negotiations of key underwriting and syndication / distribution terms with strategic clients, banks and investors, in conjunction with the relevant teams as necessary, ensuring the bank receives favourable terms in order to contribute to business growth QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE: Minimum Qualification Bachelor’s degree in finance / accounting / business or related discipline. MBA / CA and/or professional qualifications. Proficient in English oral and written communication. Good interpersonal, motivation, resource management and marketing-orientated skills Minimum Experience 10/12 years relevant experience in corporate and leveraged financing, in similar positions of progressively increasing managerial responsibilities in the senior function. Strategic thinker and agent for change Good under standstanding of US and European leveraged finance markets, structures (including Term Loan Bs) and distribution liquidity pools. Proven track record of delivery of critical projects Good coaching skills

data.title: Executive Director, API & Open Banking -Cash and Trade
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text: Join the UAE’s largest bank and one of the world’s largest and safest financial institutions. Our focus is to create value for our employees, customers, shareholders and communities to grow through differentiation, agility and innovation. We are looking for top talent and your success is our success. Accelerate your growth as you help us reach our goals and advance your career. Be ready to make your mark a top company, in an exciting & dynamic industry. Job Purpose The individual is responsible for defining, owning and scaling the bank’s API‑led propositions across Global Trade, Treasury Services and Tokenized / Digital Asset‑enabled offerings. The role owns the strategic product vision, commercialization, monetization and enterprise‑wide adoption of API‑based capabilities, positioning APIs and Open Banking as a core distribution and engagement layer that embeds the bank into client ecosystems, enables real‑time financial flows and supports emerging use cases across the current and future state of transaction banking. The role acts as a strategic bridge between business, technology, digital transformation and regulatory bodies, ensuring that all API‑led products are client‑centric, scalable, secure and fully compliant with UAE and international regulatory frameworks. Key Accountabilities Contribute to the development and execution of GTx and Wholesale Banking API and Open banking strategy across domestic and international locations. Define and own the API product vision and long‑term roadmap aligned with Global Transaction Banking, Treasury and Tokenization strategy. Position API and Open Banking capabilities as core differentiator for payments, liquidity management, escrow services and Next Gen propositions Identify collaborate-to- create opportunities with Fintech partners for innovative use cases for Cash and Trade propositions. Drive harmonization and consistency across product lines to create a coherent, scalable API and Open Banking stack for all cash and Trade Product verticals. Drive collaboration across business, technology and control functions. Ensure alignment with FAB policies, frameworks and operating standards. Represent the function effectively to senior management, external and internal stakeholders Job Context Product Vision & Strategic Ownership Define and own the API product vision and multi‑year roadmap aligned to Global Trade, Treasury and Tokenization strategy. Position APIs as core channels for payments, liquidity, escrow, trade, and digital asset‑enabled offerings. Identify and prioritize embedded finance, ecosystem‑led growth and tokenization‑driven opportunities through API and Open Banking infrastructure Drive consistency and scalability across product lines to deliver a cohesive enterprise API proposition. End‑to‑End Product Lifecycle Management Own the full API product lifecycle from ideation and design through launch, scale and ongoing optimization. Ensure API products are commercially viable, reusable, secure and client‑centric. Balance speed‑to‑market with long‑term resilience, operational robustness and regulatory expectations. Client, Platform & Ecosystem Enablement Enable and scale direct integration of bank capabilities into corporate ERP, TMS, digital platforms and partner ecosystems. Engage strategic clients and partners to co‑create and scale API‑led use cases Act as senior product representative in client, partnership and industry engagements. Commercialization & Value Realization Define pricing, packaging and usage‑based commercial models for API products. Track and demonstrate value across CASA growth, fee income, client acquisition and retention. Leverage adoption analytics and performance insights to refine investment priorities and product strategy. Governance, Risk & Regulatory Alignment Ensure API products are compliant with client money, safeguarding, operational resilience and data requirements. Maintain alignment with Central Bank, FSRA / ADGM and other applicable regulatory frameworks for developing fit-for-purpose propositions in these jurisdictions Embed risk controls, transparency and reporting by design within all API solutions. Leadership & Stakeholder Management Act as a trusted senior partner to GTB, Treasury, Digital Assets, Technology, Risk and Operations. Provide clear, commercially grounded updates to the Managing Director and senior management forums. General Responsibilities Promote API reuse and standardization to reduce duplication and accelerate delivery. Lead cross-functional groups to align API strategy with enterprise goals. Maintain high standards of API documentation. Contribute to budget planning and resource allocation for API engineering. Provide training and enablement on API best practices. Qualifications & Experience 10+ years of experience in transaction banking, treasury, digital platforms or product‑led financial services. Proven track record of building and scaling API‑driven or platform‑based products in regulated environmen

data.title: Head of Enterprise Data Platforms
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text: Job Description To lead the strategic design, development, simplification , modernization and governance of the bank’s enterprise data platforms, ensuring it serves as a robust, scalable, and secure foundation for data-driven decision-making, advanced analytics, AI & agentic initiatives. The role is pivotal in enabling the bank’s digital transformation by delivering a modern data architecture that supports real-time insights, multi disciplinary autonomous AI agents, regulatory compliance, and innovation across all business units. Key Functions Define and execute the enterprise data platform strategy in alignment with the bank’s digital, agentic and AI roadmap. Lead the design and implementation of a modern, cloud-based data architecture (e.g., data lakehouse, data fabric). Strategize, lead and execute the data estate simplification agenda and seamlessly decommission the redundant, obsolete and non scalable data platforms and duplicate tooling Oversee the integration of structured and unstructured data sources across the enterprise. Ensure high availability, scalability, and performance of the data platform. Establish and enforce data governance, metadata management, and data lineage practices along side Data Governance unit. Collaborate with data science, analytics, and business / OPS teams to enable self-service data access and advanced analytics. Drive the adoption of DevOps and DataOps practices for continuous delivery and automation. Manage vendor relationships and evaluate emerging technologies for platform enhancement. Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and internal audit standards Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing team of data engineers, architects, and platform specialists. Develop and manage the data platform budget, ensuring cost-effective operations. Report on platform performance, adoption/ usage metrics, and value delivery to executive leadership. Foster a culture of innovation, agility, and continuous improvement within the data function. Represent the bank in industry forums and contribute to thought leadership in data platform strategy. Qualifications Strategic Thinking & Vision Ability to align data platform strategy with business goals, platform-based architecture, data estate modernization and digital transformation initiatives for AI enablement. Technical Expertise Deep knowledge of cloud-native data platforms (e.g., Azure, AWS, GCP), data lake houses (Data Bricks), data mesh, and real-time data streaming (Confluent Kafka). Leadership & Influence Proven experience in leading cross-functional teams and influencing senior stakeholders. Data Governance & Security Strong understanding of data privacy regulations (e.g., UAE Central Bank, GDPR), data quality frameworks, and security best practices. Program & Change Management Expertise in managing large-scale data platform programs and driving organizational change. Collaboration & Communication Excellent interpersonal and communication skills to work across business, IT, and regulatory functions. TCO & Operational Efficiency Mindset Proven track record in optimizing TCO for data estate with FINOPS implementation and tooling simplification strategies and execution

data.title: Head of Project Finance & Corporate Development - Infrastructure
data.jd_text: Location: Dubai, UAE (with significant international mobility) Sector: Energy Infrastructure & Large-Scale Logistics Seniority: Finance Director / Project CFO (15+ years’ experience) A prominent, privately-held international energy infrastructure group is seeking a high-caliber Finance Director to lead the financial architecture for its expanding portfolio of large-scale infrastructure projects. With active development across multiple high yield geographies, the firm focuses on the deployment of complex, capital-intensive assets. Operating in a lean, executive-led environment, this role offers the autonomy to build financial structures for 1B+ USD projects and direct accountability to the C-suite. Key Responsibilities Project Finance Architecture: Lead the end-to-end financial structuring for large-scale energy projects. Design and execute SPV/Project Company setups, including the ringfencing of assets to manage risk and optimize capital allocation. Capital Structuring & Debt Management: Manage the debt-equity mix for major developments. Engage with lenders, structure non-recourse project finance, and oversee the financial close of multi-hundred-million-dollar deals. Operational Financial Leadership: Act as the "Project CFO" for designated project entities. Oversee the full financial lifecycle from the development/construction phase through to long-term operational and revenue-generating stability. Risk & Compliance: Implement rigorous financial governance frameworks across multi-jurisdictional environments (specifically focusing on emerging markets). Ensure compliance with international reporting standards and lender requirements. Cross-Functional Coordination: Serve as the primary finance liaison between technical, legal, and operational project teams. Work closely with external financial/tax advisors to manage complex joint venture agreements. Reporting & Strategy: Deliver high-level financial analysis and performance metrics to the board and internal stakeholders, providing the narrative that drives future project investment. Candidate Requirements Experience: 15+ years of progressive finance experience in the energy, infrastructure, or large-scale EPC sectors. Proven track record in managing project-level financials for assets exceeding 200M+ USD. Technical Competence: Expert-level mastery of project finance, financial modeling, treasury management, and corporate structuring. Deep understanding of multi-currency, multi-jurisdiction project accounting. Project Execution: Direct experience in taking large-scale energy or infrastructure projects from Financial Close through to the operational phase. Commercial Mindset: Ability to balance complex financial structuring with the operational realities of working in challenging, emerging-market environments. Communication Skills: Exceptional verbal and written communication. Must be capable of representing the firm in high-stakes negotiations with external lenders, equity partners, and government stakeholders. Personality & Mindset: Entrepreneurial and "hands-on." Comfortable working in a lean environment where taking direct ownership is expected. Culturally adaptable and resilient. Mobility: Willingness to be based in Dubai or Europe, with an appetite for significant travel and potential relocation to project sites in Africa to ensure on-the-ground financial oversight. Education: Strong academic background in Finance, Economics, or Engineering. Professional certifications (CFA, ACCA, CPA) are highly desirable. Please apply by submitting your CV.

data.title: Executive Director, Manufacturing
data.jd_text: Join the UAE’s largest bank and one of the world’s largest and safest financial institutions. Our focus is to create value for our employees, customers, shareholders and communities to grow through differentiation, agility and innovation. We are looking for top talent and your success is our success. Accelerate your growth as you help us reach our goals and advance your career. Be ready to make your mark a top company, in an exciting & dynamic industry. Job Purpose To grow and manage large account portfolios and sensitive client relationships for assigned portfolio in the Industrials & Manufacturing segment; improve service quality levels and supervise the subordinates' day-to-day activities and client interactions to ensure that the team's objectives are constantly met and its revenue targets achieved, while maintaining client satisfaction and portfolio credit quality. Key Accountabilities Client and Market Analysis Conduct financial analysis on clients and their respective geographies/ industries/ markets to identify potential opportunities and risks. Business Origination Originate new business opportunities with target clients across relevant banking products in full compliance with applicable internal policies and the Target Market and Risk Acceptance Criteria. Obtain support from relevant stakeholders and governance forums to close deals (e.g., credit, products, GT). Negotiate and develop Term Sheet, mandate letter pitch deck etc. for new transactions in close co-ordination with product teams. Ability work on financial models, and conduct evaluations to support deal structuring and client presentations. Strong understanding of LMA documentation protocols would be a plus. Examine & resolve credit, market and operational risk aspects of the transactions in collaboration with products and enablement units. Collaborate and work closely with FAB International Branches to increase client wallet share, depth of relationship and X-sell opportunities with key global client relationships. Expand Products Penetration Through Sector-Focused Solutions RORAC [Return on Risk Adjusted Capital] calculation, drafting and submission of CAC note to Capital Allocation Committee in order to meet mandated return thresholds. Maintain the credit quality of the client's relationships within the Portfolio to acceptable parameters set by the Bank's management. Develop and maintain strong relationships with key decision makers in other banks to originate new business. Maintain up‑to‑date market competitor, regulatory and industry and proactively update account plans as required Ability to handle sophisticated transactions and engage assigned team members. Recommend policy amendments as when required to match with the latest business trends. Client Relationship Management To achieve assigned financial and non-financial objectives by meeting all borrowing & non borrowing needs of customers with target segments (E.g., borrowers, transactors, depositors). Prepare and Maintain Group Key Account Plans [GKAPS] for all clients with support from relevant partners (e.g., products, international banking, Employee banking, Asset Management, etc.). ESG: Liaising with clients for collection of necessary data, info for preparation of ESG rating note in compliance with FAB’s ESG policy & SOPs and its subsequent renewals at specified intervals. Support the timely completion of annual/periodic credit facility reviews the recommendation of appropriate credit facilities in line with the banks credit policy to balancing earnings with prudent risk management and potential credit downgrades/ upgrades. Develop, Implement and maintain internal controls in line with group credit policy i.e., documentation, periodical client visits / call reports, inspections and formal credit checks / informal market feedback to monitor the assigned portfolio effectively. Organize client meetings with relevant partners/ seniority to deepen FAB relationship within the portfolio. Identify & develop cross selling opportunities and increase cross-sell ratio and returns with clients. Support client on their day-to-day banking requirements with support from client services, CAD (as applicable). Achieve high degree of customer satisfaction reflected in NPS score and annual client survey reports. Keep abreast of market trends and manage a disciplined marketing process by identifying steps/strategies necessary to effectively initiate relationships with new to bank clients Collaboration Align with Industrials & Manufacturing team as well as other product and coverage verticals to identify and convert opportunities. Collaborate with other business divisions to drive cross-BU collaboration. Develop business proposition strategy, including competitive analysis, targeted client segmentation, service delivery and customer experience model, internal & external campaigns and ensure alignment with other Products proposition covering Risk policies, Payment & Cash management, Trade products,

including agile, lean and hybrid models Embed data-informed decision-making across delivery teams Identify risks early and design mitigation strategies that protect delivery integrity ❖ Team Leadership & Capability Building Mentor and inspire mid-senior level delivery leads and cross-functional teams Drive continuous improvement, knowledge sharing and capability uplift Champion a delivery culture that prioritises quality, clarity and collaboration Requirements WHAT YOU'LL BRING ❖ 10+ years' experience leading digital delivery or transformation programmes ❖ Demonstrated ability to deliver complex, multi-stakeholder digital initiatives ❖ Strong strategic judgement coupled with operational excellence ❖ Excellent communicator, able to influence at senior levels ❖ Comfortable in ambiguity, with a track record of structuring the unknown into clear plans What Success Looks Like ❖ Clear delivery models that reduce risk and increase predictability ❖ High-impact digital change delivered on time, with measurable outcomes ❖ Strong stakeholder trust & alignment across functions ❖ A culture of delivery excellence that scales with the organisation

data.title: Head of Growth
data.jd_text: Head of Growth 📍 Dubai 🚀 Startup / Scale-up / Fintech Environment I am looking for a practical, systems-focused Head of Growth Operations to join a fast-scaling Dubai fintech. This isn’t a traditional marketing job, this role is for someone who can build and manage the technical logic behind how a company communicates with its customers. You’ll be leading a small team while remaining hands-on with strategy and execution. Key Responsibilities Own the performance of all borrower communications across AI voice, email, SMS, and WhatsApp. Design the automated logic, triggers, and fallback rules that drive the borrower's journey. Oversee AI-generated content, including prompt design and conversational flow quality. Run and measure your own A/B tests on messaging, timing, and segmentation. Independently pull and analyse your own data to identify opportunities and diagnose issues. Monitor channel health, including deliverability, pickup rates, and system latency. Translate business needs into technical logic alongside the product and engineering teams. Manage a high-performing team with a focus on fast iteration and clear accountability. Who you are 6+ years in growth, CRM/lifecycle, or operations within high-volume environments. Experience working with AI-generated communication You naturally think in events, triggers, dependencies, and failure modes. Experience managing a small team while remaining hands on. You have a high-agency, ownership mentality and move at a fast pace.

data.title: Executive Director- Privileged Client Banking
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Company Description

First Abu Dhabi Bank is an inclusive environment where each person values the experiences, perspectives, ideas and beliefs of others. We’re in a unique position to learn from all our colleagues, combining international experience with deep cultural knowledge and local expertise. At FAB, you’ll have the support of your team and a strong relationship with your line manager, who will trust you with responsibility and recognize your good performance. As we embrace different ways of seeing the world, listening to each other and respecting different viewpoints, we grow stronger – together.

Job Description

We are seeking an experienced and visionary Executive Director for our Privileged Clients Group managing Corporate & Investment Banking portfolio in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates. This role will drive strategic growth, oversee complex financial transactions, and maintain strong relationships with high-profile clients in the region.

Develop and execute strategic plans to expand the bank's corporate and investment banking business in the UAE and broader Middle East regionMotivate a team of senior bankers, ensuring high performance and professional developmentOversee complex financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, capital raising, and structured finance dealsBuild and maintain strong relationships with key clients, including major corporations, government entities, and high-net-worth individualsCollaborate with other business units to identify cross-selling opportunities and maximize revenueEnsure compliance with local and international banking regulations and internal risk management policiesRepresent the bank at high-level industry events and forumsProvide strategic advice to the board of directors on market trends, opportunities, and challenges in the corporate and investment banking sector

Qualifications

Master's degree in Finance, Business Administration, or a related field; MBA or CFA certification preferredMinimum of 15 years of experience in corporate and investment banking, with a significant portion in senior leadership rolesProven track record of driving business growth and managing high-performing teams in the banking sectorIn-depth knowledge of corporate and investment banking products, services, and best practicesStrong understanding of global financial markets, economic trends, and their impact on banking strategiesExtensive experience in the Middle East financial markets, with a focus on the UAEExceptional leadership, communication, and interpersonal skillsDemonstrated ability to build and maintain relationships with C-level executives and high-profile clientsStrong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to make strategic decisions in a fast-paced environmentThorough understanding of UAE and regional financial regulations and compliance requirementsFluency in English; Arabic language skills highly desirable

data.title: Head of Cabin Crew Training
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text: Synopsis The Head of Cabin Crew Training is a senior leadership role responsible for shaping and leading the delivery of Cabin Crew training across the airline. The role ensures the airline’s service ambition, brand promise, and premium proposition are consistently translated into high‑quality customer experiences. This position provides strategic leadership across safety & service training delivery while actively contributing to the evolution of the cabin crew training strategy. Working closely with training design, innovation, inflight service, and operational stakeholders, the role ensures Safety & Service training remains aligned with premium standards, evolving customer expectations, and future business needs. With a strong focus on service elevation, premium excellence, and innovation, the Head of Cabin Crew Training ensures training delivery is scalable, efficient, and capable of sustaining world‑class service standards at pace. Accountabilities Shape and contribute to the cabin crew training strategy, ensuring training priorities align with brand positioning, premium service ambition, and long‑term customer experience objectives. Lead and govern the delivery of cabin crew training across initial, recurrent, and enhancement programmes, ensuring consistent execution of approved strategies. Translate service strategy into effective training delivery, embedding required competence, behaviours, standards, and service rituals through high‑quality learning experiences. Partner with Training Design, Innovation, and Service stakeholders to shape and influence the development and evolution of training frameworks, curricula, and methodologies. Drive continuous service elevation through training, using delivery insights, operational feedback, and customer data to inform improvements in training approach and content. Champion innovation in training delivery, shaping the adoption of new tools, technologies, and experiential learning methods to enhance engagement, realism, and efficiency. Ensure training delivery models support scale and efficiency, balancing premium service outcomes with optimal use of instructors, facilities, and training time. Set clear expectations for instructor capability and role‑model behaviour, ensuring trainers consistently demonstrate and reinforce premium standards. Provide strategic oversight of service training performance, identifying risks, trends, and opportunities that inform future training strategy and investment decisions. Represent cabin crew training at senior governance forums, contributing insight on service capability, readiness, and training‑led service improvement. Education & Experience Bachelor’s degree level education. Minimum of 12 years’ experience within the airline operation (aviation) industry, including at least 5 years in a senior management role leading team. Extensive airline cabin crew experience is strongly preferred, with a solid foundation in inflight service delivery, premium service standards, and customer experience execution About Etihad Airways Etihad Airways, the national airline of the UAE, was formed in 2003 and quickly went on to become one of the world’s leading airlines. From its home in Abu Dhabi, Etihad flies to passenger and cargo destinations in the Middle East, Africa, Europe, Asia, Australia and North America. Together with Etihad’s codeshare partners, Etihad’s network offers access to hundreds of international destinations. In recent years, Etihad has received numerous awards for its superior service and products, cargo offering, loyalty programme and more. All this ties into Etihad’s ambitious Journey 2030 strategy. The airline plans to double its fleet size and triple the number of customers over the next six years as it sets out to be the airline everyone wants to fly! To learn more, visit etihad.com Recruitment Fraud Alert Beware of fraudulent job offers from individuals or organizations claiming to represent the Etihad group. We will never ask for personal information, bank details, or payment during the recruitment process. Interviews are conducted face-to-face or via video/telephone before any formal offer. If you are asked for money, please treat it as fraudulent.

data.title: Director - Enterprise Solutions Management
data.jd_text: About the Role: King Salman International Airport Development Company (KSIADC) is seeking an experienced Director – Enterprise Solutions Management to lead enterprise platforms across airport operations, ensuring systems are scalable, integrated, and support critical business and operational functions. Key Responsibilities: • Lead the overall strategy, planning, and governance of enterprise solutions across the organization. • Oversee implementation and lifecycle management of ERP, HCM, Finance, Procurement, and airport-related systems. • Ensure integration between enterprise systems and airport operational systems (e.g. passenger, baggage, operations platforms). • Drive system standardization, eliminating duplication and improving efficiency across platforms. • Define and enforce governance frameworks, policies, and architecture standards for all enterprise solutions. • Manage relationships with system vendors, implementation partners, and managed service providers. • Oversee SLAs, KPIs, and vendor performance to ensure service quality and system reliability. • Ensure compliance with cybersecurity, data protection, and regulations (e.g. NCA, SDAIA). • Lead digital transformation initiatives across enterprise platforms to improve automation and user experience. • Collaborate with business stakeholders to translate operational needs into system capabilities. • Oversee system upgrades, enhancements, and release management processes. • Manage enterprise systems budget, cost optimization, and value realization (ROI). • Ensure business continuity, disaster recovery readiness, and incident management processes. • Act as escalation point for major system issues impacting airport operations. • Build and lead a high-performing team across solutions, applications, and support functions. Qualification and Experience: Bachelor’s in IT, Computer Science or related field. 12+ years in enterprise systems including leadership roles.

data.title: Head of Cybersecurity & IT Risk
data.jd_text: Our client is a large enterprise operating mission-critical systems and data platforms within a regulated environment. Cybersecurity and IT risk are board-level priorities for the organisation. The Role The Head of Cybersecurity & IT Risk will lead enterprise-wide cyber risk management and information security strategy. This role will oversee security architecture, risk assessments, incident response, and compliance frameworks. A key focus will be aligning cybersecurity posture with regulatory and national standards. The role will advise executive leadership and support board-level reporting. This position will also manage internal teams and third-party security providers. Continuous improvement and resilience planning will be critical elements of the role. Candidate Profile Candidates must have senior experience in cybersecurity or IT risk leadership roles. Strong knowledge of regulatory frameworks and security standards is essential. Experience in Saudi Arabia or similarly regulated markets is highly preferred. The candidate must demonstrate technical credibility, risk judgment, and executive communication skills. Industry certifications and leadership experience are strongly valued.

data.title: Enterprise Programs Director
data.jd_text:
The driving force behind our success has always been the people of AspenTech. What drives us, is our aspiration, our desire and ambition to keep pushing the envelope, overcoming any hurdle, challenging the status quo to continually find a better way. You will experience these qualities of passion, pride and aspiration in many ways — from a rich set of career development programs to support of community service projects to social events that foster fun and relationship building across our global community.

The Role

The Enterprise Programs Director will find, land, and execute global enterprise deals. The ideal candidate thrives at the intersection of business transformation and technology enablement—able to engage confidently with C-level executives, guide large delivery teams, and drive high-impact outcomes across global enterprises. This role requires someone who can translate business objectives into structured, executable programs while fostering strong client relationships and leading with influence across matrixed environments.

Your Impact

Strategic Leadership: Direct complex customer engagements, both during the assessment (pre-deal) and implementation phase, providing continuity for our customers and ensuring alignment with corporate objectives and client needs.Stakeholder Engagement: Build and maintain strong relationships with C-level executives and key stakeholders across multiple customer levels.Team Management: Lead large teams of consultants, partners and professional services colleagues, fostering a collaborative and high-performing environment.Program Execution: Ensure the successful delivery of enterprise assessments and deployment programs, managing scope, budget, timelines, and resources effectively. Continuously identify opportunities to enhance program delivery and drive operational excellence.Operating in an advisory capacity to identify key problems, develop and evaluate alternative solutions and make recommendations to key customers and stakeholders.

What You'll Need

The successful candidate will have a minimum of twelve (12) years’ experience with Information Technology initiatives, including formal project leadership experience.At least 4 years of Management Consulting experience at a large firm, with the corresponding skillset: business acumen, structured communication style, ability to synthesize complex information, concisely communicate with executive level presentation skillsExperience with data integration and management platformsOutstanding problem solving and analytical skills to align with the customer on the desired outcomes and be able to convert a solution vision into a tangible execution planStrong analytical skills and ability to translate business requirements into a set of project deliverables and associated tasks.Experience in managing large scale projects impacting multiple business functions at multiple locations, including international.Strong communications skills (written and oral), with an ability to listen and to articulate project status and issues. Communication experience should include presentations at all organizational levels.Team building and management skills, to bring a diverse group together to deliver the required business results through influence and execution.Technical understanding to enable effective delivery of technology projects including new systems implementations and upgrades to existing systems.Good working understanding of the project management lifecycle and the key knowledge areas of project management: integration, scope, time, cost, quality, human resource, communications, risk, procurement, stakeholder.Customer focus - proven track record of customer satisfaction and delivery success and ability to establish and maintain appropriate relationships with business and IT stakeholders.Industry recognized Project Management certification desirable.Familiarity with AspenTech's tools and solution is a plusFluent in English, Secondary language preferred in supporting region

data.title: Executive Managing Director
data.jd_text: Job Description: The Executive Managing Director serves as a pivotal leader within the organization, responsible for driving strategic vision and fostering operational excellence. This role requires a seasoned professional with an extensive background in managing large-scale projects and cross-functional teams across diverse sectors. The Executive Managing Director will be at the forefront of cultivating relationships with key stakeholders, ensuring alignment with organizational goals while responding proactively to industry trends and challenges. The ideal candidate will possess a robust analytical acumen to evaluate complex datasets that inform business decisions, alongside exceptional communication skills to advocate for organizational initiatives. The Executive Managing Director is expected to inspire and lead teams, demonstrating a capacity for innovative problem-solving while fostering a culture of accountability and ethical leadership. Job Requirements: A minimum of 15 years of executive-level experience in organizational leadership or strategic management. Proven track record of operating within a complex, matrixed environment, with an ability to influence at all levels of the organization. Demonstrated expertise in financial management, including budget development, fiscal oversight, and financial forecasting. Experience in developing and executing business strategies that lead to operational efficiencies and revenue growth. Superior negotiation and conflict-resolution skills evidenced by past success in stakeholder engagement and partnership development. Strong understanding of industry regulations, compliance requirements, and risk management practices. Proven ability to lead transformational initiatives that improve organizational performance and drive change. Capacity to analyze market trends and competitive landscape to inform strategic decision-making. Job Responsibilities: Develop and execute long-term strategic plans that align with the organization's vision, mission, and values. Drive performance improvement initiatives across all divisions, ensuring alignment with overall organizational objectives. Cultivate and maintain relationships with key stakeholders, including board members, investors, and regulatory agencies, to advocate for the organization’s goals. Oversee organizational governance and compliance, ensuring adherence to legal and ethical standards. Utilize data-driven analysis to inform business strategies, allocating resources effectively to maximize ROI. Coordinate cross-functional teams to ensure seamless project execution and delivery of key initiatives on time and within budget. Foster a high-performance culture through mentoring, coaching, and development of senior leadership to enhance organizational capacity and employee engagement. Communicate effectively with internal and external stakeholders, presenting organizational strategies and performance metrics to enhance visibility and transparency. Required Skills: Exceptional strategic thinking and planning skills, with the ability to translate vision into actionable plans. Advanced emotional intelligence, facilitating strong interpersonal relationships and team dynamics. Highly developed leadership capabilities, with experience in building and leading diverse, high-performing teams. Outstanding analytical and problem-solving skills, capable of synthesizing complex information to inform decision-making. Proficiency in financial analysis and budget management, with a focus on sustainability and growth. Excellent verbal and written communication skills, demonstrating clarity and influence across diverse audiences. Strong change management skills, with a history of leading organizations through transformative change. Effective networking and relationship-building skills, fostering collaboration across various levels of the organization and beyond.

data.title: Senior Program Director (Infrastructure and Transportation)
data.jd_text:

data.title: Director – eVTOL Planning & Implementation (Advanced Air Mobility)
data.jd_text: At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good. Your impact At Jacobs, we'll inspire and empower you to deliver your best work so you can evolve, grow and succeed – today and into tomorrow. Working at Jacobs offers an exciting range of opportunities to develop your career within a supportive and diverse team who always strive to do the right thing for our people, clients and communities. People are Jacobs’ greatest asset, and we offer a competitive package to retain and attract the best talent. To lead the development, planning, programming, and delivery of an integrated eVTOL (electric vertical take-off and landing) ecosystem within the transport business unit. This includes benchmarking global best-practice, preparing business cases and transactions, integrating with other transport modes, ensuring regulatory/licensing frameworks, licensing/approval processes, safety oversight, control & monitoring frameworks, stakeholder and partner engagement, financing including procurement model evaluation and contracting and commercial strategy, training, and capability building. This role will ensure that the eVTOL programme is delivered promptly in a safe, efficient, scalable, and sustainable manner, contributing to the overall multi-modal transport strategy of the authority. Key Responsibilities Strategy & Planning Develop the overall strategic roadmap for eVTOL deployment (short-, medium- and long-term horizons) aligned with the broader city and business unit strategic transport planning. Lead benchmarking of global eVTOL deployment models, regulatory frameworks, commercial operating models, and case-study analysis to inform strategy and decision-making. Undertake market scans, competitor/peer reviews, scenario planning, and risk/opportunity analysis for eVTOL integration. Develop and collaborate for multi-modal integration planning; ensure eVTOL connects to other modes (bus, rail, metro, road, micro-mobility) and is planned as part of a wider transport network. Program development and management define the planning & programming (projects, pilots, scaling phases), set milestones, progress tracking, dependencies, budgets, and deliverables. Transaction Preparation & Commerciality Lead the preparation of business cases, cost-benefit analyses, financial models, funding/financing models, procurement strategies, and partner models for eVTOL infrastructure/operators. Define commercial frameworks pricing/leasing/licensing of eVTOL operations, vertiport development, service provider models, public-private partnership structures, and risk allocation. Interface with internal commercial/legal teams to structure transactions, contracts, and operator agreements. Monitor market developments (technology, aviation certification, battery/propulsion, infrastructure) and ensure commercial propositions remain viable and competitive. Regulation, Licensing & Approvals Lead development and implementation of regulatory frameworks specific to eVTOL (including vertiport siting, airspace integration, operations, maintenance, safety, noise/emissions, certification). Consult with civil aviation authorities, local municipal/regional authorities, airports/heliports authorities and other relevant agencies to define licensing, permit and approval pathways. Oversee the approval process for eVTOL operators, vertiport developers, infrastructure planning consents, and ongoing compliance monitoring. Ensure alignment with aviation safety, air-traffic management, environment, land use planning, and transport policy/regulation. Safety, Control & Monitoring Establish and maintain safety frameworks, governance, and control functions for eVTOL operations (risk management, incident investigation, safety audits, performance monitoring). Define key performance indicators (KPIs) for eVTOL pilot programmes and full operations (safety, reliability, availability, capacity, customer satisfaction). Set up monitoring, reporting and control systems (data collection, dashboards, management reports) for the eVTOL programme and partner/operator performance. data.title: FRANCHISEE, Fractional C-Suite and Part-Time Director service
data.company: Boardroom Advisors: Part-Time CEOs MDs NEDs for Scale-Ups and SME's.
data.jd_text: Background Information Board Advisors provide an excellent solution for ambitious businesses looking for growth, profitability and stability. Increasing numbers of senior level people are turning to a portfolio career, seeking to have more control over their lives and freedom from life within a large corporate environment. Expansion Our aim is to continue increasing our market penetration throughout the world and therefore, we seek ambitious individuals capable of growing with us as Master Licensees in different countries. Our Business We operate within the Scale-Up marketplace servicing companies that ideally have between 10 and 1000 staff and likely $2m - $100m turnover although we do mentor some smaller businesses. We work directly with the business owners as part of the Board team on a long-term, on-going basis. We also work with VCs, PEs and Funds with their portfolio companies as well as family businesses. Typically, this takes the form of working within each business between 1-10 days a month. Our approach is to provide the highest quality Board Advisor skill set, with a strong emphasis on strategic and commercial skills, and to pro-actively engage with business owners to deliver terrific value. We are sector agnostic and provide Director-level expert skills in finance, marketing, sales, IT, HR and Managing. We also supply Non-Executive Directors, Chairs, Mentors and Coaches. Tasks What is the arrangement for Master Licensees? Master Licensees have exclusive territorial rights to using the Boardroom Advisors brand, web site, case studies, business model, marketing systems, processes etc for a period of years. We provide initial induction, training and ongoing support starting with weekly meetings and phasing over to monthly Board Meetings over time where we provide strategic help and advice. In exchange for helping to get the business up and running, we ask for a small initial commitment fee, a 10% royalty and a 10% shareholding in the licensee company. This ensures long-term commitment from the “parent” company in the success of the licensee. How do you deliver your services to a business? Following the initial meeting with a prospect business by a Regional Director, Boardroom Advisors introduces the appropriate Advisor to the business owner and ascertains the objectives and anticipated outcomes. Work is on an agreed package or number of days per month; or occasionally the delivery is an initial project with the opportunity to develop into a package or on-going days per month engagement. For each business there is a lead Advisor and a Regional Director. This team approach helps foster a stronger B2B relationship and business loyalty. What type of work does Boardroom Advisors undertake? Our work covers all aspects of a Board Advisor role in a successful business. Our aim is to establish long-term involvement with a business, generating regular income streams, rather than work on projects. Engagements are extremely varied and dependent upon the business needs and the stage of development the company has reached. Some examples of work the client has as a “trigger” to dealing with us and we might undertake as part of the appointment as Advisor include: ➢ Strategy development ➢ Performance review ➢ Systems development ➢ Funding analysis ➢ Outsourcing and supplier analysis ➢ Pricing review ➢ Growth planning ➢ Staffing structure analysis ➢ Board salary reviews Requirements Who joins Boardroom Advisors? All our Advisors “have been there, seen it and done it”. They are predominantly well qualified, all have scaled businesses, been real life Managing Directors or CEOs in business, and all our Advisors get a real buzz out of our clients’ success. To join Boardroom Advisors, you will need to: I. Share our values. We believe that successful businesses not only need outstanding commercial skills but require a strong and aligned culture in which to thrive. We are proud of our culture. Prospects, businesses owners, suppliers and our team tell us that we are refreshingly different. Our three core values “transparency, energy and teamwork” provide the foundation on which the culture is built. We seek individuals that are aligned with our culture and values. II. Demonstrate that you have a strong empathy towards entrepreneurs in the Scale-Up marketplace as well as VCs, PEs and Fund portfolio companies as well as family businesses. And a strong desire to engage with entrepreneurs and investors to create a position as the “trusted advisor of choice”. We require team members who can work with businesses over the long term with a laser focus on excellent customer service. III. Have the ability to fund yourself outside of joining our team. We are non-exclusive, no cost and no obligation. It will take time to build your Regional Director and Advisor business base, this could take months before earnings start to flow. IV. Team energy. We require individuals who can put all their energies into being part of our team. A key as

data.title: Head of Architectural Integration, Building Safety & Envelope
data.jd_text: Job Description What if you could do the kind of work the world needs? At WSP, you can access our global scale, contribute to landmark projects and connect with the brightest minds in your field to do the best work of your life. You can embrace your curiosity in a culture that celebrates new ideas and diverse perspectives. You can experience a world of opportunity and the chance to shape a career as unique as you. WSP is seeking to appoint a Head of Architecture Integration & Building Performance, a role that provides a single point of portfolio-level direction and leadership across Fire & Life Safety, Building Enclosures / Facades, and Technical Architecture, with clear line management accountability for the Heads of these disciplines. The Heads of Discipline already deliver strongly and cover many of the technical and operational activities within their teams. This role is not created to duplicate that work. It is created to formalize the portfolio-level direction and alignment that has historically been driven by the Director of Specialists, ensuring consistent prioritization, governance, and follow-through across the three business lines. The role will drive disciplined implementation of the Specialist Services Strategy and the Global Specialist Strategy for this portfolio, with particular emphasis on accelerating growth in Technical Architecture (architecture-led technical coordination and expanded scope), while proactively managing delivery risk where FLS and building enclosures performance can impact project outcomes. Responsibilities Portfolio leadership & direction (non-negotiable) Set one clear direction across the portfolio: priorities, standards, decision-making, and escalation routes. Align discipline plans so delivery and growth efforts are coordinated and outcome-driven. Lead delivery recovery when projects are off track due to late inputs, rework, inconsistent advice, or weak coordination, with particular focus on high-risk interfaces (e.g., Envelope ↔ Architecture/Structure/MEP/FLS dependencies). Line management of Heads of Discipline Line-manage the Heads of FLS, Building Enclosures/Facades, and Technical Architecture with accountability for performance, delivery discipline, team health, and capability building. Coach and enable the Heads of Discipline on complex delivery issues, client risk, resourcing constraints, and cross-discipline coordination. Drive an operating rhythm that reduces reliance on the Director of Specialists for day-to-day direction. Technical authority & delivery governance (quality + consistency) Act as a credible technical authority (in at least one of the three disciplines) with the ability to challenge and guide across the portfolio. Define “what good looks like” for deliverables (minimum content, evidence requirements, review expectations) and ensure consistent application across the portfolio. Implement and enforce governance so issues do not repeat: standards, peer reviews, readiness gates, decision logs, and action-closure discipline. Operational Excellence (ways of working that stick) Establish consistent delivery ways of working across the portfolio (review gates, interfaces, escalation routes) and drive adoption. Drive measurable improvement in right-first-time outputs, fewer late changes, and clearer accountability across the three disciplines. Strategy adherence & implementation (Specialist Services Strategy 2025–2027) Own implementation across the four pillars for this portfolio: Clients, Expertise, Operational Excellence, People & Culture. Ensure discipline pillar champions/leads are active and contributing as designed, and translate strategy into practical execution and consistent follow-through. Remove blockers so progress is sustained and not dependent on ad hoc senior intervention. Go-to-market and growth leadership (per business line) Drive growth of each business line through market positioning, bid quality, cross-sell, and consistent go-to-market collateral aligned to the Global Specialist Strategy intent. For Technical Architecture, accelerate growth by expanding scope and strengthening architecture-led technical coordination and design integration aligned to the team’s published strategy direction. Contribute to global alignment outputs relevant to this portfolio (e.g., discipline forums / collaboration inputs and go-to-market strategy contributions) as required. People leadership & capability building (portfolio bench strength) Build bench strength across the portfolio (deputies, succession, coaching) and embed capability uplift through People & Culture and Expertise pillars. Identify targeted upskilling and recruitment priorities aligned to strategy and growth needs. Qualifications 20+ years’ experience with senior leadership exposure in complex building environments, with strong relevance to Architecture / Technical Architecture and the ability to lead across Building Safety and Envelope disciplines. Demonstrated ability to lead senior discip

data.title: Commercial Director, Middle East & North Africa, Cardiometabolic
data.jd_text:
Working at Abbott

At Abbott, you can do work that matters, grow, and learn, care for yourself and your family, be your true self, and live a full life. You’ll also have access to :

Career development with an international company where you can grow the career you dream of.A company recognized as a great place to work in dozens of countries worldwide and named one of the most admired companies in the world by Fortune.A company that is recognized as one of the best big companies to work for as well as the best place to work for diversity, working mothers, female executives, and scientists.

The Opportunity

This position works out of our Dubai location in the Cardiometabolic & Rapid Diagnostics division.

About Rapid Diagnostics Division

Our rapid diagnostic solutions are used in hospitals, laboratories, and clinics around the globe. The crucial information derived from our tests, instruments, and informatics systems is often the first step in patient care decision-making for hundreds of health conditions from heart attacks to blood disorders to infectious diseases and cancers.

The Commercial Director, Middle East and North Africa - CM is accountable for defining and executing the commercial strategy for Abbott Cardiometabolic Diagnostics division across 19 diverse markets within the region. This leadership role is responsible for driving sustainable revenue growth, achieving profitability objectives, and expanding market share across multiple product portfolios.

The incumbent will lead the development and implementation of comprehensive sales strategies, leveraging deep market intelligence and competitive insights to identify growth opportunities and mitigate risks. This position requires a strong ability to translate strategic vision into actionable plans that deliver measurable business outcomes aligned with Abbott’s global objectives

The incumbent leads and mentors a team of Country Leads, Key Account Managers, Distributor partners and technical field personnel, cultivating a high-performance culture focused on flawless commercial execution. The position orchestrates contract negotiations with external customers, ensuring excellence in tendering and contracting processes. Additionally, the Commercial Director fosters an insights-driven culture, informing cross-functional stakeholders of market dynamics and competitive activities to enable strategic decision-making. The role also oversees the implementation of company policies, ensuring operational excellence and compliance across all markets.

What You'll Do-

Commercial Strategy & Execution

Develop and implement regional sales strategies aligned with short- and long-term business objectives.Establish annual sales quotas, forecasts, and pricing strategies, ensuring accurate revenue and profit projections.Drive business growth through acquisition of new customers and expansion of existing accounts.Lead contract negotiations and tendering processes, ensuring competitive and compliant submissions.Monitor market dynamics, competitive activities, and regulatory changes to inform strategic decisions.

Sales Management & Performance Optimization

Plan, direct, and monitor sales activities, providing monthly performance feedback against forecasts.Manage sales administration, customer service, credit collections, invoicing, and ensure timely order processing.Coach Country Leads, Key Account Managers, and field teams on sales techniques, promotions, and execution excellence.Maintain high-level relationships with clients, consultants, and external organizations to strengthen Abbott’s market position.

Leadership & Organizational Development

Foster a diverse and inclusive workplace that empowers all team members to contribute fully toward organizational objectives.Create an entrepreneurial environment, recruiting, coaching, and developing top talent across the region.Keep Abbott’s vision, mission, and values at the forefront of decision-making and daily operations.Demonstrate effective change leadership, ensuring smooth transitions during organizational or market shifts.Build strategic partnerships with internal and external stakeholders to advance departmental and organizational goals.

Operational Excellence & Compliance

Oversee customer service and supply chain operations to ensure efficient product availability and stock flow.Ensure strict compliance with company policies, regulations, and ethical standards.Make operational decisions regarding work processes, resource allocation, and scheduling to achieve departmental objectives.Maintain company standards and procedures across all commercial activities.

Market Development & Insights

Market Development & InsightsGenerate opportunities with existing and new customers, concluding sales calls independently and collaboratively with Marketing.Provide field-based support to commercial partners to drive product sales growth.Assess current and future product opportunities and pursue strategic initiatives to max

data.title: SOC Head | DUBAI | Full-time
data.jd_text: Strategic Head of Security Operations (SOC) | Premier Multi-Sector Enterprise The Opportunity: Orchestrate Resilience at Scale Are you a Cybersecurity visionary who thrives at the intersection of deep technical architecture and executive-level strategy ? We are seeking a sophisticated Security Operations Leader to transform and manage an advanced Security Operations Centre (SOC) for a prestigious, multi-sector group in the Middle East. This is not a "maintenance" role. We are looking for a leader capable of building and transforming security frameworks across complex, multi-industry environments—from critical infrastructure to advanced digital platforms. The Persona: Who You Are We are seeking a candidate who mirrors the professional excellence of industry veterans—someone who possesses: Executive Presence: You can translate complex threat landscapes into operational KPIs and strategic risk-based recommendations for Board-level stakeholders. The Architect’s Mindset: You don't just manage SIEMs; you have a proven track record of implementing Zero Trust Network Architectures (ZTNA) and building SOC functions from the ground up. Technically Proficient & Hands-on: Deep experience with SIEM platforms (Azure Sentinel/Splunk) and a thorough understanding of OT & IT security operations. Elite Communicator: You possess the rare ability to mentor a team of high-performing analysts while maintaining strong relationships with regulatory bodies and global vendors. The Mission: Your Impact Strategic Evolution: Collaborate with the CISO to refresh and evolve a Group-wide Security Strategy that safeguards diverse business domains. SOC Transformation: Lead the end-to-end management of the SOC, overseeing threat monitoring, incident response (containment to recovery), and continuous vulnerability management. Sophisticated Architecture: Support the Zero Trust (ZTNA) strategy across the enterprise. Continuous Validation: Integrate DevSecOps and automated testing frameworks to ensure the organization remains resilient against emerging AI-driven threats. Global Compliance: Align SOC capabilities with international frameworks including GDPR, PCI-DSS, and regional requirements. Your Professional Toolkit Experience: 12–15+ years of progressive Cybersecurity experience, with at least 6+ years in a senior leadership or SOC managerial role. Proven Track Record: Experience managing multi-million dollar security portfolios and leading cross-functional teams (IT, OT, and Network Ops). Education: Master’s degree in Information Technology or Cybersecurity; an MBA in Finance or Strategy is highly preferred. Credentials: CISSP and CISM are essential. Specialized certifications like GCFA Experience with intrusion detection/prevention systems and advanced security technologies

Snap contributes to human progress by empowering people to express themselves, live in the moment, learn about the world, and have fun together. The Company’s three core products are Snapchat, a visual messaging app that enhances your relationships with friends, family, and the world; Lens Studio, an augmented reality platform that powers AR across Snapchat and other services; and its AR glasses, Spectacles. The Comms Team is responsible for conveying Snap’s mission, vision and values to core audiences within our community, which include Snapchatters, advertising partners, content partners, investors, policymakers, and Snap team members. This passionate team works hard to show the world what makes Snapchat the best platform for communication and storytelling, and to ensure the narratives about the company, its people, and its products are accurate and compelling.
