data.title: Head of Media, MENAP
data.jd_text: We are Reckitt Home to the world's best loved and trusted hygiene, health, and nutrition brands. Our purpose defines why we exist: to protect, heal and nurture in the relentless pursuit of a cleaner, healthier world. We are a global team united by this purpose. Join us in our fight to make access to the highest quality hygiene, wellness, and nourishment a right and not a privilege. Marketing Marketing is at the heart of Reckitt. We pride ourselves on our unique way of driving growth for some of the world's most loved and trusted health, hygiene and nutrition brands. Our purpose and fight are what motivates us to design and deliver distinctive communications throughout our consumer funnel, so that we deliver category growth as well as a positive impact on society. With extensive media and digital teams embedded throughout the organisation, we measure both our business and our societal impact. Our global teams lead on our overarching brand strategy, brand guardianship and innovation pipeline in close partnership with R&D. Leaving our local marketing teams to deliver on our regional goals in partnership with our Sales teams. With the scale of our 2500 marketing community - alongside our leading-edge Marketing Academy - your opportunities to grow and upskill will be extensive. About The Role The Head of Media - MENAP role leads the end to end media strategy, investment, implementation, and performance measurement across all channels. The role ensures cohesive media planning aligned with brand, business, and commercial objectives, while driving innovation, efficiency, and excellence across markets. Your responsibilities Define and own the long term media strategy for the MENAP region across all channels. Develop full funnel media investment priorities across platforms and equity and performance executions. Manage regional media budgets, ensuring transparency, governance, and accountability. Act as senior advisor to C suite and business leads on media impact and opportunities. Ensure cohesive collaboration across brand, digital, analytics, procurement, and market teams. Lead annual media planning, budgeting, and optimization cycles. Oversee development and delivery of annual and in-year media plans. Ensure consistency in media architecture, audience targeting, and channel mix across all markets. Ensure dashboards, analytics reports, and insights are actioned by agencies and teams. Oversee relationships with global and regional platforms (Meta, Google, TikTok, etc.). Lead the relationship with media agencies—performance evaluation, scopes, KPIs, commercial models. Manage the media team ensuring clear direction of work, priorities of tasks, capability development, work life balance and mental wellbeing. The experience we're looking for A proven ability to lead, inspire, and mentor teams, driving toward success. Strong interpersonal abilities, paired with excellent negotiation and communication skills. Skilled in data analysis, adept at translating insights into strategic actions. A strategic thinker, proficient in evolving insights into effective, actionable plans. Building Business / Customer Relationships. Educated to minimum bachelors degree level in a business or communications related subject - from a recognised University. 10-12 years of media experience, with good understanding of national media / new media experience. What we offer With inclusion at the heart of everything we do, working alongside our four global Employee Resource Groups, we support our people at every step of their career journey, helping them to succeed in their own individual way. We invest in the wellbeing of our people through parental benefits, an Employee Assistance Program to promote mental health, and life insurance for all employees globally. We have a range of other benefits in line with the local market. Through our global share plans we offer the opportunity to save and share in Reckitt's potential future successes. For eligible roles, we also offer short-term incentives to recognise, appreciate and reward your work for delivering outstanding results. You will be rewarded in line with Reckitt's pay for performance philosophy. Equality We recognise that in real life, great people don't always 'tick all the boxes'. That's why we hire for potential as well as experience. Even if you don't meet every point on the job description, if this role and our company feels like a good fit for you, we still want to hear from you. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, disability or medical condition; colour, ethnicity, race, citizenship, and national origin; religion, faith; pregnancy, family status and caring responsibilities; sexual orientation; sex, gender identity, gender expression, and transgender identity; protected veteran status; size or any other basis protected by appropriate law.

data.title: Country Head of Voice & Virtual (UAE national)
data.jd_text: About Us Our team consists of 25,000 colleagues, spread across 35 countries. We believe in transformation – embracing agile and new ways of working together, which helps us to truly ‘think client’ and serve millions of our affluent and emerging affluent clients, across the world. Our clients include individuals and small businesses, and we support them with a range of traditional banking services, including investment, credit and wealth planning solutions. We offer career opportunities for relationship managers, customer service specialists, product and segment management, sales and wealth planning. If you’re looking to unlock new job growth opportunities, Standard Chartered Bank is the place for you. We are currently seeking an experienced professional to join our team. About Wealth And Retail Banking Wealth and Retail Banking (WRB) serves more than 11 million clients in many of the world’s fastest-growing markets. Our client continuum spans from Mass Retail to Affluent, including high-net worth clients served by our Private Bank. We leverage digital banking channels with a human touch to provide clients with differentiated products and services such as deposits, payments, financing, wealth management and personalised advice. We also support small business clients with their business banking needs. Job Summary We are looking for a Head, Voice and Virtual to provide strategic leadership across the Client Care Centre. You will oversee large-scale service, sales, and governance operations, ensuring consistent, high‑quality client experiences across all voice and virtual channels. In this role, you will shape strategy, lead senior managers, and drive performance, cost discipline, and capability uplift. You will role‑model a deep understanding of client needs, colleague engagement, and operational excellence to enable delivery of strong business outcomes. Key Responsibilities Provide strategic leadership across service, sales, and governance operations within the Client Care Centre Lead and develop senior managers and team leads to deliver strong operational, service quality, and commercial outcomes Drive performance against service, sales, cost, efficiency, and quality targets Oversee escalations and ensure timely resolution of complex client issues Lead resource planning, productivity management, and cost optimisation Use performance metrics and management information to inform strategy and decision-making Champion customer experience initiatives that improve satisfaction, usage, and revenue outcomes Drive engagement, capability development, and sustainable attrition reduction across teams Strengthen governance, risk awareness, and compliance across the operation Skills And Experience Extensive experience leading large-scale contact centre or customer operations Strong people leadership capability with experience managing leaders at scale Proven track record in driving operational performance, service excellence, and cost management Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills with the ability to translate insights into clear actions Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills with the ability to influence at senior levels Understanding of contact centre governance, risk, and operational controls Qualifications Graduate or post‑graduate degree in business or a related discipline Fluency in Arabic and English language is required About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together We Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do Never settle, continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well Are better together, we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What We Offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter, we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance, with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sab

data.title: Chief Information Security Officer - Cloud Security - Saudi Arabia
data.jd_text: Responsibilities About the team: The Cloud Security team is responsible for the security assurance of ByteDance enterprise businesses and the underlying cloud platform, covering areas such as security architecture, SDLC, vulnerability management, security incident response, and security compliance. The team aim to ensure the security and stability of the ByteDance cloud platforms while enabling the success of the cloud business. The Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) is responsible for the overall cybersecurity posture of the organization and for ensuring compliance with applicable cybersecurity laws, regulations, and regulatory frameworks, including the National Cybersecurity Authority (NCA) Essential Cybersecurity Controls (ECC) within the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia (KSA). The CISO will provide independent executive leadership and oversight across cybersecurity governance, risk management, and security operations. The role requires a pragmatic, business-oriented leader with strong expertise in cybersecurity, cloud security, and modern technology environments, capable of ensuring that cybersecurity risks are identified, managed, and reported in alignment with the organization's risk appetite and regulatory expectations. You’ll collaborate closely with senior executives. This role offers the opportunity to shape strategic outcomes, reduce risk exposure, and drive secure innovation across complex, interconnected ecosystems. Responsibilities: Cybersecurity Governance & Leadership: - Establish and maintain enterprise-wide cybersecurity governance in line with SAMA CSF and NCA ECC requirements. - Ensure clear ownership, accountability, and segregation of duties across cybersecurity functions. - Advise executive management on cybersecurity risks, threats, and control effectiveness. - Ensure cybersecurity considerations are embedded into corporate governance and enterprise risk management. Cybersecurity Risk Management & Compliance: - Own and oversee the cybersecurity risk management framework. - Ensure continuous compliance with applicable regulations and industry good practices. - Ensure timely remediation of audit findings and regulatory observations. - Act as the primary point of accountability for cybersecurity matters with regulators. Security Operations & Technology Oversight: - Oversee cybersecurity operations including monitoring, detection, vulnerability management, IAM, and incident response. - Ensure effective prevention, detection, response, and recovery capabilities. - Govern the selection and operation of cybersecurity technologies. - Ensure security by design across infrastructure, applications, cloud, and data platforms. Incident Management & Cyber Resilience: - Ensure effective incident response and cyber crisis management. - Lead executive coordination during material cybersecurity incidents. - Ensure alignment with business continuity and disaster recovery. - Oversee post-incident reviews. Third-Party & Outsourcing Cybersecurity: - Ensure third-party cybersecurity risks are identified and managed. - Ensure compliance of vendors with regulatory and contractual obligations. - Oversee outsourced and managed security services. People, Culture & Capability: - Establish and maintain a compliant cybersecurity operating model. - Develop national cybersecurity talent in alignment with Saudization. - Promote cybersecurity awareness and culture. - Ensure adequate resourcing and training. Authority & Decision Rights: - Define cybersecurity policies and standards. - Escalate risks and incidents to executive management. - Approve or reject cybersecurity risk acceptances. Qualifications Minimum Qualifications: - Education: A bachelor’s degree in computer science, computer engineering, information technology, or a related field. - Experience: 5+ years of relevant experience in security strategy, cybersecurity governance, risk management, or related domains. - Security Frameworks Knowledge: Familiarity with industry standards such as OWASP, SANS CWE Top 25, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, NIST, and SAMA/NCA frameworks. - Proven executive leadership in cybersecurity governance, risk management, and regulatory compliance. - Strong experience engaging with Boards, regulators, and senior executives on cybersecurity matters. - Ability to translate complex cybersecurity risks into clear business and risk implications - Demonstrated leadership in building high-performing cybersecurity teams and national talent. Preferred Qualifications: - Sound decision-making and crisis leadership during material cybersecurity incidents Proven executive leadership in cybersecurity governance, risk management, and regulatory compliance. - End-to-end security design and strategic roadmap of cloud security products and services. - CISSP, CISM, CCSP, GIAC Certifications About Us Founded in 2012, ByteDance's mission is to inspire creativity and enrich life. With a suite of more than a dozen products, including TikTok, Lemon8, CapCut and Pico as

data.title: Director of Business Development, Middle East
data.jd_text:
RETHINK MANUFACTURING 

The only way to ignite change is to build the best team. At Bright Machines®, we’re innovators and experts in our craft who have joined together to create a new category of manufacturing that will help transform the industry. We believe software and data are the answer, thoughtfully applied to solve our customers’ unique challenges. Through intelligent automation, we give factories newfound flexibility, scalability, and resilience. We deliver products to meet the demands of today while building a platform to take advantage of what comes next.

Working with us means you’ll have the opportunity to make lasting, impactful changes for our company and our customers. If you’re ready to apply your exceptional skills to create the factory of the future, we’d love to speak with you.

About The Role

We’re looking for a results-oriented, experienced, and regionally located Account Executive/Director to win and nurture new business for Bright Machines in KSA, UAE and other GCC countries.

Bright Machines is a next-generation technology manufacturer bringing AI and data center infrastructure manufacturing to the edge. Through an integrated platform spanning Virtual Product Development, Software-Defined Automation, and a Unified Data Platform, Bright Machines transforms how complex infrastructure systems are designed, built, and scaled. By connecting design intelligence, programmable automation, and real-time production data, the company shortens time from silicon to revenue, lowers total cost of ownership, and increases the lifetime value of deployed systems.

What You Will Be Doing

What You Will Bring

10+ years of enterprise business development experience in the Middle East with strong knowledge of regional buying dynamicsBachelor’s degree required with advanced degree preferredProven record closing $10M–$100M+ complex infrastructure deals in multi-stakeholder environmentsEstablished C-level relationships across KSA and UAE with the ability to drive executive alignmentExperience selling to Saudi ministries, PIF-backed entities, and leading regional operators including G42, Khazna, Mobily, STC, e&, Equinix, and Moro HubStrong understanding of KSA Vision 2030 priorities, sovereign digital transformation programs, hyperscale and sovereign cloud markets, and AI compute infrastructureDemonstrated success winning net-new strategic accounts and expanding long-term platform engagementsBackground in datacenter, automation, advanced manufacturing, or AI infrastructure environmentsCollaborative, self-directed operator who delivers results in a distributed

Arabic language fluency (not mandatory but strong plus)Prior experience selling Datacenter services, AI infrastructure, automation and/or manufacturing Direct experience with Datacenter expansion and colocation services Familiarity with sovereign cloud requirements and data regulations Experience scaling early-stage technology into GCC Experience at a high-growth technology, robotics, automation, or deep-tech company 

BE EMPOWERED TO CHANGE AN INDUSTRY

Bright Machines is a next-generation, AI-enabled manufacturer focused on data center infrastructure assembly operations. Bright Machines uses its proprietary AI-based robotics and software to assemble AI infrastructure hardware products (i.e., data center servers) for hyperscalers and leading Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs). With its new AI factory, Bright Machines addresses increasing market demands for computing power due to the surge of AI and the U.S. national mandate to reshore manufacturing by building data center infrastructure at scale with higher quality and shorter time-to-market.

Bright Machines is headquartered in San Francisco, California, with an integration center in Guadalajara, Mexico. data.title: Director of Engineering KSA
data.jd_text: Director of Engineering Location: Saudi Arabia (Relocation support to be discussed) Package: Highly competitive, tax-free executive package Sector: Aerial Work Platforms (AWP) & Powered Access Rental Overview Our client is a market-leading organisation within the powered access and equipment rental sector, with a strong and growing footprint across the GCC. As part of their continued expansion, they are seeking an experienced Director of Engineering to lead and transform their fleet engineering function across the region. This is a high-impact leadership role offering the opportunity to shape engineering strategy, drive operational excellence, and play a key role in supporting significant regional growth. The Opportunity Based in Saudi Arabia, you will take full ownership of the company’s engineering and fleet strategy across multiple GCC markets. You will be responsible for optimising asset performance, leading large-scale investment programmes, and ensuring best-in-class maintenance and reliability standards across a diverse fleet. This role offers significant executive exposure, long-term career progression, and the chance to contribute to major infrastructure and development projects across the Middle East. Key Responsibilities Strategic & Financial Leadership Define and deliver the regional engineering strategy aligned with business growth objectives Lead CAPEX planning, fleet investment, and lifecycle optimisation programmes Provide strategic guidance to senior leadership on fleet performance, renewal, and cost efficiency Drive data-led decision making through KPI monitoring, utilisation analysis, and cost control Fleet & Asset Management Oversee fleet operations across multiple GCC locations, ensuring consistency and best practice Manage full asset lifecycle from specification and procurement through to disposal Implement predictive and preventative maintenance strategies to maximise uptime Leverage telematics and digital systems to enhance visibility and performance Maintenance & Reliability Lead all maintenance, inspection, and compliance programmes across the fleet Drive reliability-centred maintenance and continuous improvement initiatives Optimise parts supply chains, vendor relationships, and maintenance contracts Support sustainability initiatives including emissions reduction and energy-efficient technologies Leadership & Organisational Development Lead and develop a high-performing, multi-national engineering team Foster a culture of safety, accountability, and continuous improvement Partner with Operations and HR on workforce planning and capability development Budget & Performance Management Manage engineering budgets and capital investment across the region Identify cost efficiencies through innovation, procurement, and process optimisation Report on operational and financial performance to executive leadership Safety, Quality & Compliance Champion a safety-first culture across all engineering activities Ensure full compliance with regional HSE and regulatory standards Lead audits, investigations, and continuous improvement programmes Candidate Profile Minimum 8 years’ leadership experience within fleet, plant, or equipment engineering (powered access experience essential) Strong technical expertise across mechanical, hydraulic, and electrical systems Proven track record managing large-scale CAPEX programmes and engineering budgets Experience with telematics, digital fleet management, and asset optimisation tools Prior experience in the GCC or emerging markets is advantageous but not essential

data.title: PMO Senior Manager (Western Cluster, Jeddah)
data.jd_text: Job Description This role involves leading and managing a Project Management Office (PMO) to ensure effective oversight, coordination, and delivery of construction projects. Responsibilities include developing project management policies and processes, overseeing budgets, contracts, and quality controls, and ensuring compliance with design specifications and safety standards. The position requires close coordination with clients and stakeholders, monitoring project progress, managing resources and risks, and driving continuous improvement in project management practices. It also includes providing leadership and guidance to teams, evaluating project performance, and ensuring successful project execution aligned with organizational objectives. Responsibilities Develop and implement comprehensive PMO plans and processes for overseeing, coordinating, and reporting all work processes. Establish policies and procedures for project management. Participate in the planning and formulation of design alternatives and solutions for the construction project. Communicate with the Client’s representatives continuously regarding Project Progress and areas of concern. Implement standardized project management and business analysis practices. Work directly with the Project Management leads, interact frequently with the project functions, Source and Deliver in the development of Project Management processes that drive continuous improvement and deliver improved business results. Develop and administer project budgets and fiscal controls, contracts, and quality control provisions. Direct work sequencing to expedite project delivery and minimize disruption of ongoing operations. Inspect, approve, and accept completed projects for the institution, ensuring conformance with institutional standards, building and life safety codes, and architectural/engineering specifications. Provides coaching, advice and guidance to the team, maintaining high levels of integrity, motivation and morale. Researches and prepares various reports pertaining to operations, equipment, policies, procedures, and other issues, as appropriate. Stakeholder Management & Business Engagement. Evaluate detailed project status reports and present key highlights and related implications for business partners & executives. Oversee all aspects of the day-to-day management of the construction project. Oversees the consolidated cost estimates and general financial management, incorporating risk for the overall project. Leads resource and capacity management process for assigned project(s) to ensure project(s) are managed to meet deliverables and timelines and develops employees to ensure sustainable delivery capability. Ensure that project operations are in compliance with design specifications and client requirements. Informs themselves of the relevant quality, environmental, safety, and occupational health policies, as well as manuals and procedures within Hill International, and ensures continued compliance with these requirements while employed by Hill. Perform other duties as assigned by the line manager/supervisor. Qualifications Bachelor's degree in engineering from an accredited university. Minimum 20 of relevant project management experience, including leading major government and/or semi-government international programs and projects with a proven track record of delivering project excellence in a direct project manager role. Experience with major government and/or semi-government international construction sites with direct professional knowledge and management of the following: site engineering, project controls, environment, health and safety, contracts management and quality. Experience in implementing a zero-accident philosophy. A record of engagement with government entities/utility providers. Record of implementing continuous improvement on site and supporting capacity building/talent development in a construction environment. Excellent verbal and written communication skills. Experience of working internationally (preference KSA experience).

data.title: Country Director, (Based in Saudi Arabia)
data.jd_text: About Agoda At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world. Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide. No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us. Our Purpose – Bridging the World Through Travel We believe travel allows people to enjoy, learn and experience more of the amazing world we live in. It brings individuals and cultures closer together, fostering empathy, understanding and happiness. We are a skillful, driven and diverse team from across the globe, united by a passion to make an impact. Harnessing our innovative technologies and strong partnerships, we aim to make travel easy and rewarding for everyone. Job Summary: We are seeking a dynamic and results-driven Country Director to lead and drive Agoda’s growth and operations across the Middle East and Africa region. This role requires a strategic thinker with a deep understanding of the MEA market, strong leadership skills, and the ability to build and maintain relationships with key stakeholders. The Country Director will be responsible for developing and executing strategies to expand Agoda’s presence, optimize performance, and achieve business objectives in the region. Key Responsibilities Strategic Leadership Develop and implement a comprehensive growth and market strategy, informed by market data, customer insights, and product capabilities. Identify market trends, opportunities, and structural challenges to shape both commercial priorities and product direction. Partner with global leadership to align regional strategy with Agoda’s long‑term vision, platform roadmap, and investment priorities. Business Development Build and maintain strong relationships with key partners, including hotels, travel agencies, and ecosystem stakeholders. Identify partnership opportunities that unlock new use cases, supply models, or customer segments. Negotiate strategic partnerships that enhance Agoda’s product offering, distribution, and competitive advantage. Identify and pursue new business opportunities that translate into scalable product and revenue growth. Team Leadership Lead, mentor, and develop a high‑performing team across commercial, partnerships, and market functions. Create a culture that values strategic thinking, data‑driven decision‑making, and strong collaboration with product teams. Attract and retain top talent capable of operating at the intersection of growth, product, and execution. Operational Excellence Oversee regional operations, ensuring efficient execution across partnerships, marketing, and product initiatives. Translate strategy into clear operating plans, milestones, and KPIs, tracking progress and course‑correcting when needed. Ensure operational readiness for new product launches, localization initiatives, and regulatory requirements. Maintain compliance with local regulations and company policies. Market Insights and Reporting Gather and synthesize market intelligence, customer feedback, and partner input to inform product localization and roadmap discussions. Act as the voice of the region in product planning and prioritization conversations. Provide senior leadership with clear insights on performance, product gaps, competitive risks, and growth opportunities. Prepare and present reports that connect market outcomes to strategic and product decisions. Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, or a related field (MBA preferred). 10+ years of experience in a leadership role, preferably in the travel, hospitality, or e-commerce industry. Proven track record of driving business growth and achieving targets in the MEA region. Strong understanding of the Middle East and Africa markets, including cultural, economic, and regulatory nuances. Exceptional leadership and team management skills, with the ability to inspire and motivate a diverse team. Excellent negotiation, communication, and interpersonal skills. Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data and make data-driven decisions. Why Join Agoda? Be part of a global, innovative, and fast-growing organization. Work in a dynamic and collaborative environment with opportunities for professional growth. Lead impactful projects that shape the future of travel in the MEA region. Discover More About Working At Agoda Agoda Careers https://careersatagoda.com Facebook https://www.facebook.com/agodacareers/ LinkedIn https://www.lin

data.title: Head of Compliance I KSA I Financial Services I Orient Insurance PJSC
data.jd_text: Job Requisition ID: 177138 Established in the 1930s as a trading business, Al-Futtaim Group today is one of the most diversified and progressive, privately held regional businesses headquartered in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. Structured into five operating divisions; automotive, financial services, real estate, retail and healthcare; employing more than 35,000 employees across more than 20 countries in the Middle East, Asia and Africa, Al-Futtaim Group partners with over 200 of the world's most admired and innovative brands. Al-Futtaim Group’s entrepreneurship and relentless customer focus enables the organization to continue to grow and expand; responding to the changing needs of our customers within the societies in which we operate. By upholding our values of respect, excellence, collaboration and integrity; Al-Futtaim Group continues to enrich the lives and aspirations of our customers each and every day Job Description The Head of Compliance is responsible for establishing, leading, and maintaining an effective compliance framework to ensure the organization adheres to all applicable laws, regulations, and internal policies. The role plays a critical part in safeguarding the company’s integrity, particularly in alignment with Saudi insurance regulations and governance standards. What You Will Do Compliance Strategy & Leadership Develop and implement the company’s compliance strategy and annual compliance plan. Lead and manage the compliance function, ensuring adequate resources and expertise. Promote a strong compliance culture across the organization. Report functionally to the Board/Audit Committee and administratively to senior management. Regulatory Compliance Ensure adherence to regulations issued by Saudi Central Bank and other relevant authorities. Monitor regulatory changes and assess their impact on the business. Act as the primary point of contact with regulators. Policy & Procedure Governance Develop, review, and update compliance policies and procedures. Ensure policies are aligned with local laws, including insurance and corporate governance regulations. Oversee implementation across all departments. 4 . Risk & Compliance Monitoring Establish compliance monitoring programs and conduct regular reviews. Identify and assess compliance risks across underwriting, claims, sales, and operations. Ensure timely reporting and remediation of compliance issues. Anti-Money Laundering (AML) & Financial Crime Oversee AML/CTF framework in line with Saudi regulations. Ensure effective Know Your Customer (KYC) processes. Investigate suspicious activities and report to relevant authorities when required. Training & Awareness Develop and deliver compliance training programs for employees. Increase awareness of regulatory requirements and ethical standards. Reporting & Advisory Provide regular compliance reports to senior management and the Board. Advise business units on regulatory matters and compliance risks. Support business initiatives by ensuring compliance requirements are embedded early. Investigations & Ethics Lead internal investigations related to compliance breaches. Oversee whistleblowing mechanisms and ensure confidentiality and protection. Required Skills To Be Successful Bachelor’s degree in Law, Finance, Business Administration, or related field (Master’s preferred). Professional certifications such as CAMS, CRCM, ICA Diploma, or equivalent are preferred. Minimum 10+ years of experience in compliance, with strong exposure to the insurance industry. At least 5 years in a leadership role within Saudi Arabia. Strong knowledge of Saudi regulatory frameworks, especially insurance and financial services. What Equips You For The Role Strong knowledge of regulatory compliance and governance frameworks. Leadership and stakeholder management skills. High ethical standards and integrity. Excellent analytical and risk assessment capabilities. Strong communication and influencing skills. Ability to manage regulatory relationships effectively Key Relationships Board of Directors Audit Committee / Risk Committee Senior Management Regulatory Authorities (e.g., Saudi Central Bank) External Auditors Performance Indicators (KPIs) Compliance with regulatory requirements Timely submission of regulatory reports Number and severity of compliance breaches Effectiveness of compliance monitoring programs Training completion rates Regulatory inspection outcomes About Orient Insurance PJSC Orient Insurance Company commenced operations in 1982 as a part of the reputed Al-Futtaim Group and has since recorded a progressively steady growth. The Company is counted among the leaders in the UAE insurance market. Orient Insurance Company has a paid-up capital of AED 500 million which is the highest in the insurance industry in UAE. With head office in Dubai, the company serves its clientele through an extensive branch network in Jebel Ali, Abu Dhabi, Al Ain, Sharjah and Ras Al Khaimah in UAE, Muscat in Sultanate of Oman, Riyadh

data.title: Head of Creative Strategy
data.jd_text: About Us: We’re building an ecosystem of digital products and services that power everyday life across the Middle East—fast, scalable, and deeply customer-centric. Our mission is to ring every doorbell, everyday. We want to redefine what technology can do in this region, and we’re looking for a Head of Creative Strategy who can help us move even faster. What you’ll do: The Creative Strategy function sits at the core of how noon shows up to customers — driving high-impact campaigns, creative execution, and brand storytelling across all markets and platforms. This role requires someone who can own end-to-end creative output, from ideation through to execution, and translate business priorities into campaigns that drive growth. You’ll work closely with the CEO, acting as a creative operator, strategist, and force multiplier. You will lead campaign development, shape creative direction, and ensure execution across digital, media, and on-ground channels is best-in-class. This role is built for someone with real creative leadership instinct, sharp thinking, and the ability to operate in fast-paced, high-pressure environments. You should be able to develop strong campaign ideas independently and drive them through to execution with high standards. Key responsibilities include: Lead the development of breakthrough campaign ideas-from concept through to execution Own end-to-end campaign delivery across digital ads, media, social, and key commercial moments Translate business priorities into high-impact creative platforms that drive measurable outcomes Drive hero campaigns across key moments (Ramadan, sale events, growth pushes) Oversee creative production including ad films, digital content, and campaign assets Bring strong filmmaking sensibility — storytelling, pacing, and visual direction Ensure high-quality, fast-moving, performance-driven execution across all channels Shape and evolve noon’s creative direction and tone of voice Drive execution across ATL, BTL, digital, and on-ground activations Work closely with internal teams, agencies, and production partners to deliver at scale Partner with CEO / GrowCo and business leaders on high-priority growth initiatives Build and lead a culture of creative excellence, ownership, and high standards What We’re Looking For: 10–15+ years of experience in creative strategy, advertising, or campaign leadership roles Proven track record of delivering high-impact, full-funnel campaigns Strong creative instinct and independent ideation ability Deep understanding of campaign development across digital, performance, and brand marketing Strong production and filmmaking sensibility Comfortable operating both strategically and hands-on Experience working in fast-paced, high-growth environments Strong interpersonal skills — able to work across creative, marketing, product, and leadership teams High ownership — able to drive ideas from concept to execution without drop-off Comfortable working under pressure with tight timelines and high expectations Detail-oriented, with a strong focus on quality and output Commercially aware — understands how creativity drives performance and growth Bonus Points If You Have: Experience in e-commerce, tech, or digital-first businesses Strong portfolio of ad films, campaigns, or large-scale creative work Experience working across multiple markets or culturally diverse audiences Prior experience working closely with founders or CEO offices Who will excel? We’re looking for people with high creative standards who understand that great work requires both ideas and execution. You need to be relentlessly resourceful, with a strong bias for action and the ability to move quickly from concept to output. We value individuals with the confidence to push bold ideas, challenge thinking, and raise the bar on creative quality. Success here requires speed, resilience, and the ability to operate in ambiguity while delivering consistently high-quality work.

data.title: Head of Insights
data.jd_text: About Easygenerator We’re a fast-growing EdTech scale-up on a mission to revolutionize learning through our award-winning authoring tool. Used by global giants like Kellogg’s, T-Mobile, and Siemens, Easygenerator helps organizations simplify knowledge sharing: fast, easy, and impactful. We’re an AI-first company , utilizing cutting-edge tools in both our products and internal workflows. You’ll gain hands-on AI experience and develop a future-proof skillset while contributing to a fast-moving, high-impact product. About The Role As Head of Insights, you’ll lead our product analytics function and oversee user research. Your primary focus will be on driving deep, strategic insights from data that inform product decisions, shape our roadmap, and ultimately improve learner outcomes. This is a hands-on leadership role where you’ll coach a small but mighty analytics team and work cross-functionally with product managers, designers, and engineers. What You’ll Do Lead and develop our product analytics team, ensuring delivery of high-impact, actionable insights. Partner with cross-functional teams to drive a data culture, define success metrics and craft measurement plans. Translate data and user research into clear, compelling narratives that influence stakeholders. Build and maintain dashboards, KPIs, and data infrastructure to support self-serve and strategic analytics. Design and evaluate experiments and causal analyses that inform product decisions and strategy. Define and maintain a consistent tracking and event taxonomy across the product. Identifies new opportunities for leveraging data. Forecasting trends, interpreting complex data, and communicating actionable insights to stakeholders. Oversee the user research function, embedding customer insights throughout the product development lifecycle. Create scalable processes for insight delivery, balancing ad-hoc needs with long-term learning goals. Coach and grow talent across analytics and research disciplines. Champion a culture of analytics, promoting literacy across departments, and advocating for robust data governance practices. Be a champion of data-informed decision making across the entire organization. Must-Have Skills 10+ years in product analytics, data science, or insights in a B2B SaaS environment. Experience leading a data team. Track record of influencing product or business decisions through proactive insights. Strong command of SQL and fluency in at least one analytical language (e.g., Python or R). Hands-on experience with product analytics tools (e.g., Mixpanel, Amplitude, Looker, GA). Experience with Snowflake or equivalent modern data warehouse technologies. Solid understanding of user research methodologies (e.g., interviews, usability tests, surveys). Experience leading a team and mentoring junior analysts or researchers. Excellent communication and storytelling skills—translating complexity into clarity. Nice to Have Prior ownership of both analytics and user research functions. Experience working in low-volume, high-complexity environments (e.g., B2B vs. B2C). Familiarity with data pipeline tools (e.g., dbt, BigQuery, Segment). What Success Looks Like Delivery of high-quality insights that shape product strategy and roadmap decisions. Champion a culture of analytics, promoting literacy across departments, and advocating for robust data governance practices. Increased customer understanding across all product teams. Scalable systems for measurement and research embedded in team workflows. Growing capabilities of the insights team and wider product org.

data.title: Head of Affiliates
data.jd_text: Remote Permanent or Full-time Contractor Competitive package Meta Hire Group is working with a leading Crypto Casino and Sports Betting brand who are looking for a Head of Affiliates to be a part of building the affiliate programme from zero. Reporting directly to the Founder, you will have total autonomy over deal frameworks, team hiring, and strategy. It’s a role for someone who already knows what a market-leading programme looks like and simply wants the environment to build it properly. Responsibilities Building the affiliate programme from zero, deal framework, CPA benchmarks, platform, tracking, reporting, and partner pipeline operational before the first Affiliate Manager joins. Personally owning the top 10–15 partner relationships, sourcing, negotiating, activating, and growing the most commercially significant affiliates on the programme. Setting the commercial and channel strategy defining where to play, how to structure deals, and how to make the programme every serious affiliate wants to be on. Hiring and leading the affiliate team: Affiliate Manager and Strategy & Ops Manager in Phase 1, with headcount added only when the programme proves it needs it. Owning the numbers: Active users and revenue targets, tracked weekly, with full accountability for hitting them. Building the brand: conferences, community presence, and a reputation for fast payments and real data; making the brand known in the affiliate world for the right reasons. Requirements 5+ years in affiliate or partnerships at a gaming or crypto operator, operator-side only, not agency or network. Has owned a revenue or acquisition target and is personally accountable for hitting it. Has structured and negotiated CPA, rev share, and hybrid deals independently, not just executed within someone else's framework. Has hired and led a team, this is a people leader role from day one. Understands multiple affiliate channels and can model a deal before signing it. Industry presence, attends the major conferences and has a partner network they can activate from week one. About Meta Hire Group Born in UAE Operating Globally. MetaHire Group is a specialist recruitment consultancy positioned at the high-velocity intersection of emerging technology, global gaming, and digital performance. With over a decade of elite expertise, we partner with visionary founders to architect the high-performance leadership teams defining the new economy.

data.title: IT & Systems Director
data.jd_text: IT & Systems Director - on behalf of a client LOCATION : DIFC, Dubai SALARY : Depending on experience ABOUT US We are launching a brand-new and exclusive members' club, located in the heart of Dubai, that offers a private world of unparalleled connections. Representing a new pinnacle of sophistication and elegance in the region, the club sets itself apart as a carefully curated membership offering, fusing Emirati cultural nuance with timeless heritage hospitality. We are committed to providing exceptional service and creating unforgettable experiences for our distinguished members. ROLE OVERVEW The IT & Systems Director holds executive responsibility for the club’s technology ecosystem, ensuring all digital platforms, infrastructure and operational systems function as a secure, resilient and fully integrated environment. This includes CRM, POS, finance systems, member-facing platforms, access control and data governance. Reporting to the Club General Manager, the role ensures technology supports the full member lifecycle while enabling efficient operations and high service standards across the club. The IT & Systems Director oversees systems procurement, integration and vendor management, ensuring platforms operate as a coherent and scalable architecture aligned with the club’s membership strategy, privacy standards and long-term growth. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES System Strategy & Architecture Own the end-to-end systems architecture, with Salesforce as the core CRM and system of record . Define how all digital and operational systems connect, interact and evolve over time. Ensure technology supports the full member lifecycle, from first introduction to long-term retention. Establish clear principles for privacy, data governance, security and system ownership across the stack. Ensure infrastructure resilience, cybersecurity standards and operational continuity across all systems. Salesforce & Core Platform Ownership Lead the i n-house implementation, configuration and ongoing ownership of Salesforce . Design Salesforce architecture to support: Prospecting, referrals and provenance tracking, Applications, committee workflows and approvals, KYC, onboarding and founder engagement, Member engagement, usage and retention. Act as internal product owner, translating operational and commercial needs into Salesforce delivery. Configure objects, workflows, automations, dashboards and reporting aligned to the approved membership operating model. Systems Integration & Delivery Oversee integration of Salesforce with all core systems, including: Website and application flows, Member app and portal, POS, reservations and table management, Access control and identity systems, Payments and finance. Ensure integrations are stable, well-documented and future-proofed. Minimise dependency on external vendors by retaining system knowledge in-house. Selectively manage specialist external partners where required, with clear accountability and quality control. Ensure alignment between CRM, POS, finance and operational systems to maintain a unified data environment. Manage technology vendors and implementation partners, ensuring performance, accountability and knowledge retention in-house. Pre-opening Implementation Lead delivery of a fully integrated systems environment ready for soft opening. Ensure all systems are tested end-to-end against real operational scenarios. Align systems delivery tightly with the pre-opening critical path. Enable founder onboarding, early bookings and pre-opening engagement through digital channels. Live Operations & Optimisation Own systems performance once the club is live, ensuring reliability, data quality and operational clarity. Continually optimise systems based on member behaviour, staff feedback and operational learnings. Support Membership, Brand, Operations and Finance teams with insight, reporting and system-driven decision-making. Introduce phased enhancements to automation, personalisation and intelligence over time. Build internal documentation, training and handover processes to ensure long-term resilience. SALESFORCE EXPERIENCE Please note, this role requires deep Salesforce experience and applications without this will not be considered. While formal certification is not a substitute for real-world delivery, the successful candidate is expected to demonstrate strong Salesforce platform expertise, supported by relevant credentials or equivalent hands-on experience. Candidates should have personally led Salesforce delivery within complex, live operational environments. REQUIREMENTS Must be currently based in Dubai or available to relocate to Dubai for a full-time role. 10+ experience in senior IT, systems or digital leadership roles within hospitality, private clubs, luxury environments or comparable service-led organisations. Deep, hands-on experience owning Salesforce implementations in-house, from architecture and configuration through to live operation and optimisation. Demonstrated abilit

data.title: Head of Application Support - MSIT du
data.jd_text: About This Opportunity Accountable for end-to-end service delivery, service performance, and continuous improvement across multiple IT service lines, ensuring contractual compliance, service stability, operational efficiency, and customer satisfaction, in alignment with Ericsson Managed Services standards. What You Will Do Accountable for 24x7 service delivery across IT Landscape (OSS/BSS) in line with customer contract. Ensure consistent SLA, KPI, and compliance adherence across incident, problem, change, and release management. Provide leadership and governance to Service Delivery (L1/L2/L3) teams, including partners. Act as escalation authority for major service issues and chronic service risks. Act as Enterprise / E2E Architect for operational stability and transformation initiatives. Drive Continuous Service Improvement (CSI) initiatives to improve service quality, stability, and efficiency. Ensure effective major incident management, including executive-level communication and root cause closure. Drive standardization and adoption of tools, processes, and automation across service line. Proactively identify service risks and ensure mitigation and recovery plans are in place. Own consolidated service reporting and provide fact-based performance insights to customers and internal stakeholders. Contribute to cost governance, productivity improvements, and operational efficiency targets. Develop service delivery leadership capability and ensure succession planning and skill readiness. Hands-on operations responsibility for managing third-party applications and production services (e.g., Siebel CRM, HP Service Assurance, HP Mediation, Ericsson Billing & Charging, digital channels — App/Web/Selfcare/eShop, CVM such as Pega, etc.). Provide line management of large onsite and offshore teams (50+ employees), including resource planning, performance management and capability development. Support business development, growth and pre-sales activities related to managed services offerings. Ensure 24/7 operational readiness and decision-making (on-call leadership and ability to deliver under pressure). Promote and lead adoption of operations automation and TM Forum L4 capabilities, including AIOps and other automation tools. You will bring: Bachelor’s degree in engineering, IT, or equivalent. 15–18+ years of experience in IT service delivery and operations, with a minimum of 15 years in Managed Services IT (MSIT) roles. Proven experience leading large-scale managed services delivery in telecom or complex IT environments (including knowledge on Ericsson products like BSCS, Charging, Mediation or similar COTS products from other vendors). Hands-on operations experience managing third-party applications and digital channels (Siebel CRM, HP SA, HP Mediation, Ericsson Billing & Charging, App/Web/Selfcare/eShop, CVM/Pega, etc.). Line management track record managing large teams onsite and offshore (50+ employees). Strong expertise in ITIL-based service management and CSI. Hands-on experience or strong working knowledge of automation aligned to TM Forum Autonomous Operations (L4), including exposure to AIOps platforms and their application in production IT operations; deep knowledge of operations automation tools is a plus. Extensive experience in customer-facing senior delivery roles. End-to-end service ownership. Senior stakeholder and customer management. Risk management and decision making. Data-driven governance and reporting. Leadership development and mentoring; people coaching skills. Continuous improvement and efficiency mindset. Leadership and soft skills including: customer interfacing, ability to deliver under pressure, a 24/7 mindset, business development / pre-sales orientation, strong presentation and communication skills. Certifications (Preferred) ITIL eTOM PMP (Preferred) Scrum Master Why join Ericsson? At Ericsson, you´ll have an outstanding opportunity. The chance to use your skills and imagination to push the boundaries of what´s possible. To build solutions never seen before to some of the world’s toughest problems. You´ll be challenged, but you won’t be alone. You´ll be joining a team of diverse innovators, all driven to go beyond the status quo to craft what comes next. What happens once you apply? Click Here to find all you need to know about what our typical hiring process looks like. Encouraging a diverse and inclusive organization is core to our values at Ericsson, that's why we champion it in everything we do. We truly believe that by collaborating with people with different experiences we drive innovation, which is essential for our future growth. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and realize their full potential as part of our Ericsson team. Ericsson is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. learn more. Primary country and city: United Arab Emirates (AE) || Dubai Req ID: 779653

data.jd_text:
About Us:

Fortes Education?has been a leading education provider in the UAE for over four decades with premium international schools and nurseries that have experienced outstanding success and growth. Our schools offer the most innovative and character-building education in the entire Middle East region.

Our core ethos and philosophy of education is Positive Education (PosEd). Through PosEd, our schools foster active engagement in learning,?celebrating character strengths and accomplishments. We encourage children to cultivate fulfilling relationships with others and seek meaning and purpose in their own lives.?We enable children to become happy learners and develop into innovative thinkers, confident young adults, and life-long learners. Our students develop resilience, a growth mindset and deep character strengths that allow them to flourish in an ever-changing world.?

About Sunmarke School –?Where Amazing Happens

Sunmarke School provides an ambitious and inspiring, values-based education that nurtures the hearts and minds of students so that they can flourish and achieve their dreams. Powered by our suite of signature programmes, Sunmarke is a uniquely innovative school, transforming young lives.?This was reflected in Sunmarke School winning the coveted ‘Most Innovative School’ award at the international Which School Advisor ceremony in Dubai and London.

Sunmarke is an all–through school which offers a premium education across a fantastic range of pathways for students, including the National Curriculum of England, GCSE/IGCSE,?‘A’?Levels, BTEC and the IB Diploma Programmes.??Our students regularly win examination boards’ ‘best in world’, ‘best in Middle East’ and ‘best in country’ awards every year, across a diverse range of subjects. The latest incredible IB results show that Sunmarke School is in the top 3 schools in Dubai and 69th in world rankings. Our suite of Signature Programmes is unrivalled in Dubai, with inspiring opportunities for students to experience transformative learning opportunities. These include Environmental and Sustainability Studies, where students learn in Forest School settings, as well as utilising cutting-edge hydroponic technology to examine how science can support sustainability in future terrains; a world-class STEAM centre and curriculum, which offers students an incredible opportunity to apply their learning in solving real-world problems, from Foundation Stage through to Year 9; Augmented and Virtual Reality AR/VR experiences alongside our cutting edge use of Artificial Intelligence for staff and students; outstanding performing arts opportunities with full black box and auditorium facilities alongside peripatetic music spaces and media suites; and a fantastic sports programme augmented with professional coaches, which led Sunmarke to be overall champions at the BSME Games.?

Sunmarke School has been judged by the Dubai School Inspection Bureau (DSIB) as a?‘Very Good’?school since our third?year of operation and is consistently judged ‘Outstanding’ by British?Schools Overseas (BSO).

We present an exciting opportunity to join our all through school and to play an important part in our journey beyond?“outstanding”. Sunmarke School, Dubai is one of our flagship schools, located at?a prime location in Jumeirah Village Triangle.?

About You:

We are seeking an exceptional and inspirational Head of Faculty – Art & Design Technology to lead and shape a vibrant, innovative department across all key stages. This is a teaching and middle leadership role, and the successful candidate will maintain an active teaching commitment across Art and Design Technology, delivering outstanding lessons while driving creativity, innovation, and excellence across the faculty.

About You:

We are looking for a leader who:

data.title: Head of Product Design
data.jd_text: 1inch is the DeFi ecosystem building financial freedom for everyone. 1inch products help users and builders trade, hold and track digital assets - with the self-custody, comprehensive security and the intuitive user experience they need to unlock the potential of true crypto ownership. 1inch protocols and APIs provide core infrastructure across the DeFi industry and beyond. So if you’re someone who thinks big, moves fast and wants to make an impact right from day one, then get ready to join our industry-changing team. Location: We prioritise candidates who are based in Dubai then consider those working remotely in one of our European Hub Locations (UK, Spain or Germany). As Head of Product Design, you will shape the future of user experience at 1inch by driving the vision, strategy, and execution of design across all our products in crypto, blockchain, and DeFi. You are a key leader aligning design with business goals and technology innovation, influencing product roadmaps at the executive level and inspiring teams to deliver world-class, user-centered experiences. You will ensure validated, end-to-end UX flows are delivered ahead of launch milestones, using data, experimentation, and user insight to reduce late-stage UX changes and accelerate delivery. Design under your leadership directly supports revenue growth through higher trading conversion, improved retention, and a cohesive, scalable product experience. By strengthening design systems and cross-functional collaboration, you help reduce UX-related support tickets and raise the overall quality bar across platforms. This role requires a strategic mindset, strong leadership, deep Web3 and crypto UX expertise, and the ability to operate seamlessly across product, engineering, marketing, and business teams. Key Responsibilities Lead long-term UX planning across 1inch products, defining a multi-year design direction while delivering fast, incremental improvements that balance innovation, scalability, and reliable execution Own UX readiness for product launches, ensuring validated end-to-end user flows are delivered ahead of engineering milestones and late-stage UX changes are minimized Ensure a cohesive, user-centric product experience at scale through strong design system adoption Oversee the evolution of the design system, brand consistency, and accessibility standards across all platforms Manage and grow a team of Product Designers, including hiring, mentorship, performance management, and long-term career development Requirements 7+ years of progressive experience in UX/UI/Product Design leadership roles, ideally in crypto, fintech, or complex tech ecosystems Proven track record of managing and scaling design teams delivering cross-platform products (web, iOS, Android) Proven ownership of design systems and scalable UX foundations across multiple products Demonstrated ability to drive measurable business outcomes (conversion, retention, revenue) through design Deep understanding of UX principles, interaction design, accessibility, and branding within blockchain/Web3 context Strong strategic thinking with the ability to balance visionary leadership and operational execution Excellent communication and storytelling skills for diverse audiences, including executives and technical teams Fluent English communication skills (written and spoken), with the ability to clearly articulate complex design concepts to executive and technical audiences Nice to Have Background in cryptocurrency DeFi and blockchain ecosystems UX or product design certifications (e.g. Nielsen Norman Group) are a plus Why work for us? Join a young, creative team in a fast-paced and supportive environment We’re open to new ideas — if you’ve got a vision, pitch it and make it happen Enjoy competitive pay that matches your skills and experience Be part of a company that’s shaping the future of DeFi Take time when you need it — we offer unlimited vacation days Get fully compensated for your work gear — we’ll set you up for success We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
