data.title: Head of Veterinary Nursing
data.jd_text: The nursing team makes or breaks a veterinary clinic. Not the marketing. Not the technology. Not even the vets. It’s the nurses who set the pace, hold the standard, and determine whether a clinic feels chaotic or calm. We’re looking for someone who knows this — and wants to build a nursing culture from the ground up. A new veterinary group is launching across the UAE with a technology-first, client-obsessed approach to veterinary care — and ambitious plans for rapid expansion into the wider GCC, particularly KSA. This is a founding Head of Veterinary Nursing role — an opportunity to define nursing standards, build the team, and shape the clinical culture across an entire network of clinics. The Role This is a hands-on leadership role. You’ll spend roughly 60% of your time in active clinical practice and 40% on leadership, training, and operational responsibilities. You won’t be managing from behind a desk — you’ll be the most experienced nurse on the floor, leading by example while building the systems and standards around you. You’ll design and implement nursing protocols across every clinic in the network. You’ll recruit, train, and mentor veterinary nurses and technicians — building a team that delivers consistent, compassionate, high-quality care regardless of which location a client walks into. You’ll own the nursing department budget, manage staffing costs, and ensure the nursing function operates efficiently as we scale. You’ll work closely with the Veterinary Clinical Director to ensure nursing and clinical standards are fully aligned, and with the Practice Manager to ensure staffing, scheduling, and workflow run seamlessly. As we scale, every nurse in the network will look to you as the standard-setter and the advocate for their profession. Day to day, you’ll be: Clinical (approx. 60%): Assisting in surgeries — anaesthesia monitoring, patient preparation, surgical support, and post-operative recovery Running nurse-led consultations where appropriate — post-op checks, weight clinics, vaccination appointments, and chronic condition monitoring Performing and processing in-house diagnostics — blood work, urinalysis, cytology, and imaging preparation Managing inpatient care — treatment administration, patient monitoring, and clinical record-keeping Triaging emergency and walk-in cases alongside the veterinary team Leadership (approx. 40%): Designing and implementing nursing protocols, SOPs, and clinical care standards across all clinics Recruiting, onboarding, and training new veterinary nurses and technicians Running regular training sessions, skills assessments, and CPD planning for the nursing team Managing the nursing roster, staffing ratios, and department budget Conducting performance reviews and mentoring junior nurses toward career progression Collaborating with the Clinical Director and Practice Manager on workflow, capacity planning, and quality improvement This will suit you if: You’re a qualified and experienced veterinary nurse (5+ years) with leadership or supervisory experience You want to lead without stepping away from clinical work — you’re at your best when you’re still on the floor You’ve built or significantly improved nursing standards in a previous practice You believe veterinary nursing deserves more recognition, more autonomy, and better career paths — and you want to prove it You’re a natural teacher who gets fulfilment from developing junior nurses into confident professionals You’re comfortable with technology and excited to work with AI-powered tools, digital patient records, and modern practice management systems You’re capable of operating in a fast-paced, high-growth, entrepreneurial environment where things move quickly and you’re expected to lead, not wait You’re based in the GCC, or you’re an exceptional international candidate open to relocation What’s on offer A founding nursing leadership role with genuine influence over clinical culture Competitive salary reflective of the seniority and scope of this position Equity participation through the company’s Long-Term Incentive Plan (LTIP) — a share-based equity programme for the right candidate Relocation support for international candidates — visa sponsorship, housing assistance, and transition support Technology and infrastructure that empowers nurses rather than burdening them with admin The chance to work with a proven team that has already disrupted and scaled 50+ sites across the GCC A clear career path from single-site nursing lead to regional Head of Nursing as we scale across the UAE and into KSA If you’ve ever wanted to define what great veterinary nursing looks like — not just practise within someone else’s version of it — this is your moment. Apply now. All applications are completely confidential. Shortlisted candidates will receive full details on the brand, the technology, the team, and the timeline.

data.title: VP - Engineering (Exchange)
data.jd_text:
The OpportunityCronos Labs is building the infrastructure to collapse every speculative market into one app. Tokenized stocks, crypto perpetuals, prediction markets, spot: one balance, every market, powered by Crypto.com's 150M+ users and regulated infrastructure for tokenized equities.
We need a VP- Engineering to build the trading engine that makes this possible. The core technical mandate is a hybrid onchain Central Limit Order Book (CLOB) and matching engine with institutional-grade risk infrastructure: cross-asset margin, real-time liquidation systems, and a risk engine that can handle correlated drawdowns across tokenized stocks and crypto derivatives simultaneously. This is the single biggest capability gap on the team. Our blockchain and platform engineering is well-staffed and capable. What we lack is the person who has built centralized, high-performance trading infrastructure and can translate that expertise into a hybrid onchain architecture.
This is a greenfield build. You are not inheriting someone else's architecture. The Sidecar Sequencer (Rust, off-chain) handles order matching at <5ms P50 latency and 100K OPS throughput. The Cronos EVM handles settlement and custody. You will own the full technical stack from matching engine to smart contracts to mobile app, with the team across Smart Contracts, Blockchain, DevOps, Platform, QA, Trading, and Inventory.
The Product You Are BuildingCronos App is a mobile-first trading app that lets users:Trade tokenized stocks (NVDA, TSLA, etc.) 24/7 with increased buying powerTrade crypto perpetuals and spotTrade sports and event outcomes (prediction markets) with the ability to exit earlyUse one balance to collateralize positions across all asset classesChoose between "Simple/Fun Mode" (guardrails, plain language) and "Pro Mode" (advanced execution, lowest fees)The product is powered by Crypto.com's regulated infrastructure for tokenized equities and prediction markets and built on the Cronos blockchain.
What You'll OwnPrimary Mandate: Trading Infrastructure (The Gap)
Hybrid CLOB & Matching Engine. Architect and ship the off-chain Sidecar Sequencer (Rust) with <5ms P50 tick-to-trade latency, 100K OPS throughput, and <1ms latency variance. Integrate with Cronos EVM for onchain settlement (<60s settlement delay). Route between onchain CLOB and offchain RFQ for best execution.Risk Engine & Margin Systems. Build the full risk stack: collateral weighting, cross-asset netting, initial margin checks, dynamic maintenance thresholds. Unified margin where tokenized equities can margin crypto trades and vice versa. Real-time risk calculations across stocks, crypto, and prediction markets.Liquidation Infrastructure. Design and implement the liquidation engine, auto-deleveraging (ADL) system, and insurance fund architecture. These must survive correlated market crashes, not just normal volatility. The insurance fund must be verifiable onchain with clear deployment rules.Perpetuals & Derivatives Engine. Perpetual futures with funding rate mechanisms, leverage management, and position lifecycle. Phase 1 forks the CDC Exchange matching engine; Phase 2 transitions to a fully onchain Global Order Book.Oracle Integration & Market Data. Manipulation-resistant price feeds for liquidation triggers, mark price computation, and cross-market reference pricing.
Secondary Mandate: Platform & Blockchain Smart Contract & Blockchain Infrastructure. Cronos chain upgrades, USDC migration (IOU to native stablecoin), bridge and cross-chain infrastructure, smart contract security and audit pipeline. Vault, Clearing House, Insurance Fund, and Governance contracts.Platform & Mobile Engineering. Mobile-first consumer app for emerging markets. Embedded wallet with passkey-based authentication. Account abstraction and TEE/verifiable compute for signing. API Gateway and SDK.DevOps & QA. CI/CD, infrastructure reliability, automated testing, performance benchmarking.CDC Integration & Coordination. Coordinate with the Crypto.com tokenization team on shared Confluence/documentation workflows and align timelines across organizations.
What We're Looking ForRequiredDeep trading infrastructure and risk management experience. You have built matching engines, margin systems, liquidation engines, or real-time risk platforms. You understand order matching semantics, cross-collateralization models, and how liquidation cascades propagate under stress. This experience can come from tier-1 investment banks, hedge funds, prop shops, centralized crypto exchanges, or DeFi derivatives protocols.Derivatives market structure fluency. Perpetuals, options, funding rates, mark price computation, portfolio margin. You don't just understand the theory. You've built systems that handle these instruments at production scale.Hands-on blockchain/DeFi depth or proven ability to ramp fast. EVM smart contracts (Solidity), protocol design, bridge security, token standards. If you're crossing over from TradFi, you need to demonstra

data.title: Technical Director / Associate Director – Façade Engineering
data.jd_text: Job Description WSP Middle East is currently hiring an Associate Director / Technical Director – Façade Engineering to join our team in Dubai, UAE. In this senior leadership role, you will drive the strategic growth, technical excellence, and innovation of our façade discipline across the region. You will lead early-stage design direction, guide complex iconic projects, and deliver solutions that balance performance, constructability, sustainability, and architectural ambition. As a recognised façade engineering authority, you will champion bold and buildable façade concepts from inception, ensure rigorous technical governance, and elevate multidisciplinary collaboration. You will oversee the delivery of high‑quality consulting services, inspire a high‑performing team, and strengthen client confidence through expert guidance and exceptional service. Your leadership will directly support regional business growth—advancing WSP’s technical reputation, contributing to major project wins, and ensuring strong P&L performance across the façade portfolio. Responsibilities Technical & Design Leadership Lead façade design across concept, schematic, detailed design, and construction stages. Develop innovative concepts and design strategies during early stages, influencing geometry, performance, feasibility, and sustainability outcomes. Provide expert guidance on curtain wall systems, unitised façades, glazing technologies, cladding systems, metal structures, and complex geometries. Oversee thermal, structural, fire, acoustic, and energy performance analyses. Ensure compliance with Middle East codes and international standards (ASTM, BS, EN, NFPA). Conduct QA/QC reviews and sign off on major technical submissions. Project Delivery & Technical Management : Lead multidisciplinary coordination with architects, structural and MEP engineering teams, contractors, and specialists. Develop design delivery strategies, resource plans, risks registers, and project schedules. Resolve complex technical issues and ensure seamless design-to-delivery execution. Ensure façade solutions meet client expectations for quality, performance, and efficiency. Market Expansion: Manage commercial elements, including pricing, forecasting, margins, and fee negotiations. Support regional growth plans, identifying new business opportunities and supporting RFPs and proposals. Strengthen relationships with developers, architects, contractors, and façade fabricators. Maintain project profitability and financial governance across façade packages. Conferences, Public Speaking, and Innovative Thinking. Leadership, Mentoring & Team Development: Lead, inspire, and manage a diverse team of technical and strategic consultants and subject matter experts. Foster a collaborative, innovative, and inclusive culture centered on growth, technical excellence, strategic advisory services, and accountability across business lines and global regions. Build and lead a high-performing façade engineering team. Promote knowledge sharing, digital innovation (BIM, parametric modelling, automation), and continuous improvement. Represent the façade discipline in industry forums, conferences, and technical committees. Qualifications Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Façade Engineering, Structural Engineering, Architectural Engineering, or a related discipline. Chartered Engineer (CEng, PE, or equivalent) preferred. 15-20 years of façade engineering experience, with significant Middle East experience delivering major projects. Demonstrated experience delivering high-rise towers, complex geometry façades, and large-scale developments. Strong understanding of façade systems, building physics, sustainability, fire safety, and advanced materials technologies. Core Leadership Capabilities Exceptional leadership, communication, and stakeholder engagement, and client-facing experience. Demonstrated ability to drive innovation and early‑stage design excellence. Strong commercial acumen and experience with P&L or division-level budget ownership. With us, you can. Apply today.

data.title: Director Business Development - Lummus Consultants
data.jd_text: About the Company At Lummus Technology, innovation thrives, and excellence is a shared pursuit. When you join Lummus, you are empowered to make an impact and be part of a dynamic team that celebrates creativity, encourages professional development, and fosters collaboration. From our technology portfolio to our global workforce, Lummus is growing at a rapid pace, and we hope that you will grow with us during this exciting time. Lummus Consultants International (LCI) is an autonomous business within Lummus Technology that provides technical, environmental & social and management consulting services worldwide. We work primarily in the energy industry, with a depth of expertise in power generation, oil & gas, refining and petrochemicals. We maintain a very strong presence in the international technical due diligence markets to support the global financial industry. LCI has a far-reaching organization with offices in the United States, UK, the Middle East and Asia. About the Role Lummus Consultants is looking for a Business Development Director to grow the company into the area of technical and management consulting in the energy industry – offshore and onshore oil & gas, LNG, pipelines, power generation and transmission, as well as refining and petrochemicals. This role will be expected to help grow the consulting practice area in the fields of oil & Gas, power delivery and decarbonization and will primarily entail growing relationships with the financial community and project developers. Assignments require a high level of experience and ability to focus quickly on important issues. The individual we are seeking must be comfortable and confident to work in a seller/doer organization structure. Responsibilities Grow Lummus Consultants’ business – in cooperation with the existing business heads. His growth strategy should be heavily focused on technical solutions but with a broad business perspective beyond traditional due diligence assignments. Develop a business plan to capitalize on existing talents and resources within Lummus Consultants and Lummus Technology; the candidate’s strengths; and market opportunities. Grow name recognition of Lummus Consultants in all Energy industry segments (with assistance from the Lummus Communication team and other internal resources). Carry out business development, marketing, and proposal preparation efforts and lead Key Account Management teams efforts. Lead or participate in consulting project work. Prepare clear and concise written reports on assignments, develop conclusions and present clear and precise written and verbal results to clients. Expand the Lummus Consulting practice area (globally), consistent with the business plan – identify gaps in skills and potential recruits. Achieve objectives with minimal oversight. Management of the P&L forecast by close collaboration with his supervisor and finance. Report to Senior Director of Lummus Consultants in Dubai office. Qualifications A degree-educated Engineer; MBA is a plus but not required. The candidate should have at least 20 years relevant professional experience. Prior experience with an owner/operator or major engineering company and/or consultancy. Strong verbal and written communication skills, including ability to write clear and concise reports in English. The candidate should have strong interpersonal skills and be comfortable conversing with clients and senior management. Demonstrated management and project management skills. Required Skills Strong leadership abilities and excellent interpersonal skills. Strong communicator with excellent written and oral communication skills. Must have a high degree of self-motivation and energy. Must have proven ability to manage business forecasting, short and long term projection. Capability of earning respect from the team and capable of influencing others without authority. Excellent professional image/presentation. Knowledge of many cultures/international style. Strong commercial savvy and negotiating skills. Interest in being knowledgeable in all services offerings in Lummus Consultants portfolio. Ability to work well with finance partners. Must have a strong balance of technical and commercial capabilities. Dubai office. Relocation is not being offered. Job-related travel (business trips usually requiring less than a two-week duration; often just one or two days) which includes international travel to financial institutions in London, is an important part of the work. Time away from the office may average 10 to 30 percent of work hours. Lummus Technology is the global leader in developing technology solutions that make modern life possible and focus on a more sustainable, low carbon future. We license process technologies in clean fuels, renewables, petrochemicals, polymers, gas processing and supply lifecycle services, catalysts, proprietary equipment and digitalization to customers worldwide.

data.title: Commissioning Director
data.jd_text: JDL Recruitment are delighted with a highly reputable Data Centre Developer who are expanding their team with a Commissioning Director to be based in the UAE. Applicant must be willing to relocate to the UAE or already be based in country. Responsibilities Develop and lead the implementation of testing & commissioning and handover plans/strategies that support the programme delivery requirements. Collaborate with programme management, engineering & development, and project teams to ensure construction timelines and methods support future scalability and modular expansion. Create long-term design strategies that align with customer requirements and expectations Champion a proactive collaboration and co-operation culture across the programme through leadership engagement, workforce empowerment, and behavioural initiatives. The development and implementation of programme-wide policies, systems, processes, procedures, and controls for testing & commissioning and handover. The standardisation of document workflows across the programme. Prepare testing & commissioning and hand over elements of the Employer’s Requirements (ERs) for contractors as part of their contract agreements. Participate in and respond to all questionnaires related testing & commissioning and hand over as raised by customers during the RFQ/Bid stage. Provide guidance to the design team to ensure project design captures all necessary testing & commissioning requirements. Participate in consultants and contractors daily, weekly, and monthly project meetings. Conduct regular inspections of on-site work to ensure adherence to good practices, international standards, and design specifications. Define customer requirements to define project technical requirements and objectives related to testing & commissioning and handover Lead the development of project-related testing & commissioning documentation, including Site Acceptance Testing (SAT) and Integrated System Testing (IST) scripts in line with company standards and best practices, ensuring consistency and excellence across the programme. Oversee the review and approval of testing & commissioning method statements and risk assessments Oversee and collaborate with contractors, suppliers, vendors, and internal team members to ensure effective execution of all testing and commissioning tasks to ensure Identify, troubleshoot, and resolve technical issues or faults encountered during the testing and commissioning phases. Resolve on-site conflicts related to testing & commissioning between contractors and technical advisors from a design perspective, proposing the best solutions for the benefit of the company. Act as the main point of contact with customers, leading testing & commissioning discussions and clarifications. Drive HSE leadership among project teams, ensuring integration of HSE in the planning, testing & commissioning, and handover phases. Requirements Relevant bachelor’s degree or equivalent Relevant professional qualification or chartered status 15+ years of relevant professional experience (including a previous director role), including the testing & commissioning of data centers or other complex engineering projects across the entire project life cycle.

data.title: Senior Director, Operations Excellence
data.jd_text: The Brink’s Company (NYSE:BCO) is a leading global provider of cash and valuables management, digital retail solutions, and ATM managed services. Our customers include financial institutions, retailers, government agencies, mints, jewelers, and other commercial operations. Our network of operations in 51 countries serves customers in more than 100 countries. We believe in building partnerships that secure commerce and doing that requires fostering an engaged culture that values people with diverse backgrounds, ideas, and perspectives. We build a sense of belonging, so all employees feel respected, safe, and valued, and we provide equal opportunity to participate and grow. We are seeking an accomplished and dynamic BGS and IMEA & APAC Operations Excellence Leader to join our team in Dubai, UAE. In this role, you will play a critical part as the Operations team, contributing to our ongoing success and strategic goals. As part of the Operations Function, you will be responsible to lead the design and execution of strategies that deliver world-class operations management , driving 3% year-on-year productivity improvement and embedding a culture of continuous improvement. Key Responsibilities Strategic Leadership Develop and execute the Operational Excellence roadmap aligned with corporate objectives. Champion a culture of continuous improvement and operational discipline across all functions. Productivity & Efficiency Deliver minimum 3% YoY productivity improvement through process optimization and waste elimination. Implement world-class operations management practices to maximize resource utilization and reduce cost-to-serve. LEAN & Process Improvement Deploy LEAN tools (Value Stream Mapping, 5S, Standard Work, Visual Management) across operations. Facilitate Kaizen events and Gemba walks to identify inefficiencies and drive actionable improvements. Standardize processes and procedures to ensure scalability and consistency. Customer Satisfaction Improve operational processes to enhance service quality and customer experience. Collaborate with commercial and customer service teams to align operational improvements with client expectations. Change Management & Capability Building Develop training programs to build internal expertise in LEAN and continuous improvement. Act as a change agent, ensuring adoption of new processes and tools across the organization. Governance & Reporting Establish governance frameworks for Operational Excellence initiatives. Preferred Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Supply Chain, Business, or related field; Master’s preferred. 10+ years of experience in operational excellence, process improvement, or industrial engineering within logistics, supply chain, or manufacturing sectors. Proven track record of implementing LEAN, Kaizen, and Gemba practices at scale. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with expertise in data-driven decision-making. Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Proficiency in English. Strong collaborator. Can prioritize. Manage tight deadlines. Additional Requirements Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as needed. Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and projects simultaneously. Flexibility to work across different time zones. Continuously identify areas for improvement.

data.title: Director of Operations
data.jd_text: Hays are working with a leading investment firm as they are looking to hire a Director of Operations to work directly with the CEO and oversee key control and operational functions across the business, the role can be based out of the UAE or Bahrain. They are looking for an experience Operational professional with a strong background within the Investment ecosystem. As a key member of the leadership team, the Director of Operations will own the firm’s end‑to‑end operating framework, ensuring strong governance, regulatory discipline, and execution excellence across the platform. Working closely with the CEO, the role will translate strategy into scalable operating processes, providing oversight across compliance, finance, and transaction execution while ensuring the firm’s operating model supports disciplined growth. The position will act as a control point between strategy and execution, strengthening decision‑making, board engagement, and stakeholder confidence, while continuously optimising how the business identifies, executes, and manages investments. Requirements: Bachelors degree in Finance or related field 10+ years of experience in investment management organisations Preferably 7+ years of experience in Big 4 (FS clients only) Strong understanding of the asset management / investment firm operating model and how control functions enable execution at pace Comfortable operating as a senior business partner to the CEO and working across multiple functions with confidence and maturity. The client is offering an attractive salary and benefits for the selected candidate, and rare opportunity to work for a top tier investment firm in the region and report directly to a high regarded and well respected regional CEO. Please follow the link to apply directly, only candidates that meet the above criteria will be contacted to discuss further.

data.title: Managing Director
data.jd_text: Job Title: Managing Director Location: Dubai, United Arab Emirates Industry: Manufacturing Job Summary: We are seeking a visionary and results-driven Managing Director to lead and oversee all aspects of our manufacturing operations in Dubai. The Managing Director will be responsible for driving business growth, ensuring operational efficiency, and executing the company’s long-term strategy. This role requires a dynamic leader with strong commercial acumen, the ability to manage cross-functional teams, and a deep understanding of the manufacturing sector. Key Responsibilities: Develop and implement the company’s overall business strategy to achieve growth objectives and profitability. Oversee daily operations, ensuring optimal performance across production, supply chain, sales, and quality control. Lead and inspire cross-functional teams, fostering a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement. Drive business development efforts, identify new market opportunities, and expand the company’s presence in the region. Collaborate with the executive team to align business operations with corporate goals and strategic initiatives. Manage financial performance, including budgeting, forecasting, and financial reporting to ensure the company meets its targets. Ensure compliance with industry regulations, health and safety standards, and environmental requirements. Build and maintain relationships with key stakeholders, including customers, suppliers, and regulatory authorities. Lead risk management initiatives to safeguard the company’s financial and operational health. Monitor market trends and competitive landscape to stay ahead of industry changes and challenges. Represent the company at industry events and in key business negotiations. Foster a positive and collaborative workplace culture, driving employee engagement and retention. Requirements: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business Administration, Engineering, or a related field. Proven experience as a Managing Director, General Manager, or in a senior leadership role within the manufacturing sector. Strong understanding of manufacturing operations, supply chain management, and production processes. Demonstrated experience in driving business growth and managing P&L responsibilities. Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence and inspire at all levels. Strategic thinker with a proven track record of successfully executing long-term business strategies. Strong financial acumen and experience in managing budgets and financial performance. Ability to navigate complex challenges, lead change, and make data-driven decisions. Knowledge of local and international market trends and regulations in the manufacturing industry. Experience working in the Middle East or familiarity with the UAE business environment is an advantage.

data.title: Managing Director
data.jd_text: You will build and run the operational and governance engine for a new business school in Dubai. This is a founding leadership role responsible for getting the institution ready to launch and scale - including accreditation readiness coordination, institutional policies and operating model, academic operations, board governance, and external partnerships support . It is a builder/operator role with real accountability. Founding institutional operator + governance lead + accreditation quarterback + partnerships hustler. This person will: Build the operating system of the school (policies, committees, processes, templates, cadence) Run/coordinate licensing + accreditation workstreams with the Quality/Accreditation lead Build org structure + hiring plan (faculty + admin) with leadership Set up board mechanics (secretary role, agendas, minutes, actions) Drive international partnerships (MoUs, dual degrees, articulation, validation models) Support fundraising/external relations (donors, sponsors) Must Have 15+ years in higher education operations / academic administration / institutional development. Proven track record in building institutional infrastructure (policies, governance, operating model) in a new or scaling environment. Hands-on experience supporting or delivering accreditation/licensing readiness (UAE and/or international frameworks). Strong board/committee governance experience (minutes, action tracking, confidentiality, compliance). Strong stakeholder management skills — can influence senior leaders without formal authority. Fluent English; Arabic is an advantage.

data.title: Director of Digital Asset Trading & Exchange Operations
data.jd_text: Welcome to MultiBank Group , a global financial pioneer established in 2005 in California and now proudly headquartered in Dubai, UAE. We specialize in delivering cutting-edge trading technology, unparalleled liquidity, and exceptional customer service. Our extensive range of financial products includes Forex, Metals, Shares, Indices, Commodities, and Cryptocurrency CFDs. Join our thriving community of over 2 million clients across 100 countries, contributing to a daily trading volume exceeding US$ 35 billion. As a heavily regulated institution with oversight from 17+ financial regulators across 5 continents, and recipient of over 70 financial awards, MultiBank Group is devoted to innovation, excellence, and empowering our clients to achieve their financial goals. mb.io is the regulated cryptocurrency exchange under MultiBank Group, offering a user-friendly platform for instant, secure trading including Bitcoin, Ethereum and more. As we expand our offerings in the cryptocurrency space, we are looking for a Director of Digital Asset Trading & Exchange Operations to join the team. Role Overview: We are seeking a senior crypto trading leader to build, lead, and scale a new chapter in our digital asset trading capabilities. This is a strategic and hands-on role responsible for shaping and advancing the firm’s crypto trading, liquidity, exchange infrastructure, and derivatives capabilities. The role spans OTC, perpetuals, funding logic, market making, liquidity sourcing, hedging, and market structure design , while also playing an important part in the strategy surrounding token-related initiatives, exchange engagement, and broader platform growth. The ideal candidate will bring a strong combination of trading leadership, derivatives expertise, liquidity and market-making experience, token exposure, and operational depth . They should understand how to build and scale a crypto trading function that is commercially effective, technically sound, and aligned with a more structured and institutional operating environment. Key Responsibilities: Trading Leadership & Strategy Lead and scale the crypto trading function across Spot, Derivatives, OTC, and Market Making. Define and execute the overall trading strategy in alignment with the company’s growth plans, platform roadmap, and commercial objectives. Take ownership of the trading desk’s performance, operating model, and long-term capability buildout. Develop a clear framework for how the business prices risk, sources liquidity, manages execution, and supports product growth. Derivatives, OTC & Pricing Lead the design and launch of the derivatives trading capability from the ground up, including perpetual products, funding logic, hedging frameworks, and exposure management — this is a greenfield build requiring someone who has stood up a derivatives desk before. Lead the development of OTC pricing and execution frameworks, including liquidity sourcing, cross-venue hedging logic, and real-time pricing across crypto and fiat pairs. Ensure pricing, execution, and risk systems reflect actual market behaviour and support sustainable commercial performance. Improve the desk’s ability to manage complex market conditions, funding dynamics, and non-linear risk. Market Making, Liquidity & Market Structure Design and oversee market-making strategies to optimise liquidity, spreads, order book depth, and execution efficiency. Define and execute liquidity strategy across exchanges and providers , improving access, reducing slippage, and enhancing execution quality. Shape market structure, execution workflows, and venue strategy to support client flow and platform growth. Develop frameworks to manage flow dynamics, adverse selection, and client behaviour effectively. Manage liquidity across CEX and DEX environments (including AMMs) , optimising execution in both off-chain and on-chain markets. Oversee integration with custodial providers (e.g. Fireblocks) , addressing latency, settlement, and operational coordination. Drive end-to-end latency optimisation across order routing, signing, and on-chain settlement. Maintain tight spreads and consistent market depth across venues , managing fragmented liquidity and multi-exchange listings. Token Strategy & Exchange Engagement Lead token trading and liquidity strategy, including managing simultaneous listings across multiple Tier 1 CEXs, coordinating with exchange listing teams, meeting liquidity requirements, and managing launch windows and post-listing market support. Manage cross-exchange price consistency, arbitrage mitigation, and unified order book strategy to minimise price discrepancies and adverse arbitrage pressure when the token is live on multiple venues. Work closely with relevant stakeholders on exchange relationships, listing processes, and ongoing liquidity support across key venues. Ensure token and exchange strategy are underpinned by strong compliance awareness, including wash trading monitoring, market manipulation pre

data.title: Director, Market Planning - Screening
data.jd_text: LSEG (London Stock Exchange Group) is more than a diversified global financial markets infrastructure and data business. We are dedicated, open-access partners with a commitment to excellence in delivering the services our customers expect from us. With extensive experience, deep knowledge and worldwide presence across financial markets, we enable businesses and economies around the world to fund innovation, manage risk and create jobs. It’s how we’ve contributed to supporting the financial stability and growth of communities and economies globally for more than 300 years. Through a comprehensive suite of trusted financial market infrastructure services – and our open-access model – we provide the flexibility, stability and trust that enable our customers to pursue their ambitions with confidence and clarity. LSEG is headquartered in the United Kingdom, with significant operations in 70 countries across EMEA, North America, Latin America and Asia Pacific. We employ 25,000 people globally, more than half located in Asia Pacific. LSEG’s ticker symbol is LSEG. OUR PEOPLE People are at the heart of what we do and drive the success of our business. Our culture of connecting, creating opportunity and delivering excellence shape how we think, how we do things and how we help our people fulfil their potential. We embrace diversity and actively seek to attract individuals with unique backgrounds and perspectives. We break down barriers and encourage teamwork, enabling innovation and rapid development of solutions that make a difference. Our workplace generates an enriching and rewarding experience for our people and customers alike. Our vision is to build an inclusive culture in which everyone feels encouraged to fulfil their potential. We know that real personal growth cannot be achieved by simply climbing a career ladder – which is why we encourage and enable a wealth of avenues and interesting opportunities for everyone to broaden and deepen their skills and expertise. As a global organisation spanning 70 countries and one rooted in a culture of growth, opportunity, diversity and innovation, LSEG is a place where everyone can grow, develop and fulfil your potential with meaningful careers. LSEG RISK INTELLIGENCE LSEG Risk Intelligence offer a suite of datasets, products, and services to enable regulated businesses and corporate organisations to manage their know your customer (KYC) & anti-money laundering (AML) compliance, empower digital customer on-boarding, conduct third party risk due diligence, and detect fraud. LSEG Risk Intelligence s the global leader in financial crime & fraud risk management data & analytics. Enabling our customers and partners to deliver sustainable growth, by providing a seamless experience to their customers, whilst helping them to meet their regulatory obligations and handle risk effectively. The LSEG Risk Intelligence mission is to combine our expertise in Digital identity, Financial Crime Screening & Third-Party Risk to deliver market leading solutions to our customers and partners and generate £1bn in annual revenues by year end 2027. Definitions Proposition = Risk Intelligence’s Screening portfolio, which is made up of data and solutions that support client’s financial crime and sanctions programs. ROLE PROFILE Responsible for leading the Market Planning function for the Screening portfolio that owns; the development and management of the portfolio’s market, growth, commercialisation and GTM strategy in support of achieving the near to medium-term (3 year) growth targets. This role requires a deep understanding of the relevant market, market participants and regulatory environment, as it plays a pivotal role in conducting market analysis, identifying commercial opportunities and threats to identify and execute a Growth strategy. Responsible, as part of the proposition lifecycle, for the market validation, design and execution of commercial frameworks, commercial analysis, supporting sales execution in market, and the internal and external communication of the proposition strategy. Key Responsibilities And Accountabilities Own the development and execution of a comprehensive growth and channel GTM strategy aligned with RI’s vision and mission. Conduct ongoing market, client segment and competitor analysis, supported by strong data and Voice of the Customer analysis, to identify trends and growth opportunities in support of the Proposition Strategy. Collaborate and develop relationships with key partners to align and deliver on this strategy, with a specific strong interlock with the Product teams. Work with RI’s Strategy team and Chief Product Officer to develop and support the longer-term vision. Build and maintain individual relationships with key clients & partners, and actively participate in market events to inform and communicate the Proposition Strategy. Lead or support the creation of client focussed and market validated business cases that drive innovation and growth. Su

data.title: Project Director
data.jd_text: Egis is an international player active in architecture, consulting, construction engineering and mobility services. We create and operate intelligent infrastructures and buildings that respond to the climate emergency and contribute to more balanced, sustainable and resilient territorial development. Operating in 100 countries, Egis puts the expertise of its 19,500 employees at the service of its clients and develops cutting-edge innovations accessible to all projects. Through its wide range of activities, Egis is a key player in the collective organisation of society and the living environment of citizens all over the world. With 3,500 employees across 8 countries in the Middle East, Egis has delivered over 700 complex development projects, stimulating economic growth and enhancing quality of life. Ranked among the top ten firms in the Middle East by Engineering News Record (ENR), Egis is committed to sustainable development. The Group’s operations in the Middle East are built on strategic acquisitions and a deep understanding of local market conditions. Egis’ long history of providing comprehensive engineering, consulting, and project management services makes it a trusted partner for regional governments, investors, and developers. Job Description Project: Rail Project Civil Package Location: Dubai, United Arab Emirates Contract Type: Design & Build 1. Position Overview We are seeking a Project Director with proven experience in delivering Detailed Design for a project based in Jordan. The position will be contracted in the UAE, with travel to Jordan required as per client requests. The Project Director will lead and oversee the full design scope of the project, from detailed design through to IFC issuance, ensuring full contractual compliance and alignment with construction requirements, schedule, and budget objectives. Key Responsibilities Leadership & Governance Provide overall leadership and strategic direction for the design scope. Define and implement the design management strategy in line with contractual obligations. Establish and lead the integrated design organization (internal teams and external consultants). Contract & Risk Management Ensure full compliance of all deliverables with the Design & Build contract requirements. Identify, anticipate, and manage technical and contractual risks related to design. Oversee variations, change management processes, and support claims strategy as needed. Technical Oversight Supervise all design disciplines, including alignment, civil works, structures, viaducts, stations, rail systems, E&M, and interfaces. Validate key technical decisions and ensure multidisciplinary consistency. Manage complex technical interfaces between systems and construction packages. Schedule & Performance Management Lead the design schedule in a highly constrained delivery environment. Ensure adherence to milestones, budget, and quality standards. Implement robust reporting and performance monitoring mechanisms. Client & Stakeholder Management Act as the primary point of contact for the Client on all design-related matters. Participate in high-level coordination meetings with authorities and key stakeholders. Represent the project at executive and institutional levels. Team Leadership Lead and mentor discipline leads and design managers. Foster a high-performance, collaborative, and multicultural team environment. Ensure strong coordination with construction and site-based teams. Qualifications Master’s degree in Civil Engineering, Railway Engineering, or related field. Additional qualifications in Project Management (e.g., MBA, PMP) are an advantage. Experience Minimum 15 years of experience in large-scale infrastructure projects. Proven experience in major railway projects. Demonstrated experience delivering projects under Design & Build contracts. Prior experience in the Middle East is desirable. Technical Competencies Strong expertise in multidisciplinary design management. In-depth understanding of railway systems and infrastructure interfaces. Solid experience in risk management, planning, and complex project delivery. Leadership Skills Strong leadership and decision-making capabilities. Excellent communication and negotiation skills. Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced, high-visibility international environment. Languages Fluent English required.

Risk & Quality Management: Implementing risk mitigation strategies, ensuring adherence to health, safety, and environmental (HSE) standards, and overseeing quality control. data.title: Head of Department | Psychology | College of Arts & Sciences
data.location: Sharjah, Sharjah Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text: Head of Department | Psychology American University of Sharjah The Department of Psychology in the College of Arts and Sciences at American University of Sharjah (AUS) invites applications for the position of Head of Department. The department seeks a collaborative and visionary leader with a record of excellence in teaching, research and service, and a strong commitment to student success and faculty development. About The Department The department offers a Bachelor of Arts in Psychology and contributes significantly to the university’s general education curriculum. Faculty members are engaged in diverse areas of research, including mental health and well-being; cognitive psychology and psycholinguistics; organizational psychology; and social, cultural and personality psychology. The department values multidisciplinary collaboration and emphasizes both scientific rigor and applied engagement with the community. Required Qualifications Applicants must meet the following minimum qualifications: PhD in Psychology or a closely related discipline. Minimum rank of Associate Professor (Professor preferred). A record of effective and inclusive academic leadership, including experience in personnel management and decision-making. Evidence of excellence in teaching, research and service. Experience with program accreditation, curriculum development and faculty evaluation. Familiarity with North American models of higher education. Preferred Qualifications In addition to the above, the college will give preference to candidates who demonstrate: Experience as a department chair, program director or equivalent academic leadership role. Success in developing and supporting undergraduate and/or graduate programs. Commitment to interdisciplinary collaboration within the college and across the university. Demonstrated ability to mentor faculty, support student engagement and foster research productivity. A clear vision for the future development of the department, consistent with the strategic goals of the college and university. Responsibilities The Head of Department will: Provide strategic leadership and foster a collegial and inclusive departmental culture. Foster and promote the professional growth of faculty in teaching and research. Oversee curriculum planning, program assessment and accreditation. Represent and advocate for the department within the college and university. Recruit and retain high-quality faculty and students. Engage in teaching and scholarship at the undergraduate and/or graduate level. Participate in university service and governance. Date of Commencement The position begins mid-August 2026. Application Procedure Applicants should apply online at <a href="javascript:aus_external_warning_ShowExternalUrlDialog("https://acg-apps1.aus.edu/cas/empapp/apply.php?p=PSY-DH-26-01");" rel="nofollow">https://acg-apps1.aus.edu/cas/empapp/apply.php?p=PSY-DH-26-01. Applicants Should Submit A cover letter describing their research, teaching and administrative experience, and articulating their vision for how they can contribute to the department’s development and success. A current curriculum vitae. Names and contact information for three referees (references will be contacted only for shortlisted candidates). Additional materials may be requested from candidates advancing to later stages of the search. Applications received by December 15, 2025, will receive full consideration; review will continue until the position is filled. About American University Of Sharjah American University of Sharjah (AUS) is a comprehensive, independent, non-profit, coeducational institution of higher education that promotes excellence in teaching, learning, research and creative work. AUS fosters a community that celebrates diversity and whose members are committed to the ideals of open intellectual inquiry, ethical behavior and civic responsibility. AUS offers undergraduate and graduate degree programs to approximately 6,000 students from over 100 nations. English is the language of instruction and the workplace. Among the top 18 percent of ranked universities in the world (QS World University Rankings, 2026), AUS is consistently among the top ten universities in the Arab region (QS Arab Region University Rankings, 2026). AUS is located in the heart of Sharjah’s University City, adjacent to the Sharjah Research, Technology and Innovation Park and just minutes from Sharjah International Airport and only 30 minutes from Dubai International Airport. The campus is situated in a vibrant and cosmopolitan area offering abundant opportunities for travel, entertainment, cultural experiences and natural beauty. Salary and benefits are competitive. AUS is licensed and its programs are accredited by the Commission for Academic Accreditation of the Ministry of Higher Education and Scientific Research of the United Arab Emirates. AUS has been accredited in the United States of America by the Middle States Commission on Higher Education (1007 Nor

data.title: Director CoE - Strategy and Execution
data.jd_text: Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid. At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world. Join Visa and do work that matters — to you, to your community, and to the world. Progress starts with you. Job Description The Director – Go to market strategy & commercialization plays a critical role in shaping and delivering the strategic agenda for the business. This role serves as the central point of coordination for devising strategy, overseeing execution, establishing operational governance and developing a repository of best-in-class sales pitch material. The role ensures close alignment between global priorities, regional strategy, and in-market execution, working in partnership with the leadership team, regional support functions, market teams, and global counterparts. What’s it all about? Our mission is to develop partnerships with Global Financial Institutes, Merchants, Tech Partners in CEMEA and Acquirers in GCC. The team engages across multiple functions within the Visa organization to bring the very best of Visa’s expertise and assets to our clients - earning well-qualified wins to drive revenue growth while building trust and growing engagement and satisfaction among teammates, partners, and clients. The nitty gritty: Devise the strategic framework: Drive the development and implementation of a multi-year growth strategy for the unit across CEMEA, with a long-term vision extending to 2030. Translate global strategies and priorities into clear, actionable regional plans that ensure consistent execution and optimal delivery across all markets. Use data analytics to pinpoint key growth drivers and prioritize focus accounts, enabling targeted strategies and measurable results. Incorporate diverse perspectives from sub-regions and cross-functional teams to build a cohesive, effective regional strategy. Define, maintain, and communicate the regional strategic narrative, priorities, and focus areas, ensuring clarity and alignment across teams. Collaborate in the creation of annual operating plans by developing both financial and non-financial KPIs, forecasting, and facilitating regular performance reviews to drive sustained progress. Commercialize and oversee execution: Develop a robust framework for prioritizing the commercialization of new solutions across Core, VAS, and CMS for managed accounts in CEMEA. Drive greater efficiency and accelerate speed to market by enabling region-wide implementation of strategic initiatives, moving beyond country-level rollouts for broader and faster impact. Collaborate across sub-regions to set up deal structuring frameworks that support an aligned strategy, while also helping develop straightforward standard deal constructs for smaller deals. Design, manage, and continuously enhance the performance management approach, including KPIs, scorecards, dashboards, and portfolio-level insights. Strengthen analytical rigor across the organization and embed data-led progress tracking into everyday management activities. Establish operational governance: Establish and oversee regional operating and governance processes, ensuring full alignment with global standards to drive consistency across CEMEA. Optimes health checks for all financial and non-financial results to proactively identify and remove bottlenecks. Produce clear, compelling executive materials, including leadership updates, business reviews, and strategic presentations for senior regional and global stakeholders Enable effective leadership alignment and communication across the organization through structured forums and engagement Thought leadership and leadership engagement: Develop best-in-class sales pitch materials aimed at empowering generalists with robust, compelling content to support effective client engagement and sell-in. Collate and maintain a comprehensive library of case studies and best practices to foster knowledge sharing and encourage reapplication across the region. Collaborate closely with Visa Corporate Communications to strengthen Visa’s thought leadership profile in external media across CEMEA, with the strategic objective of creating greater business pull and market visibility. Partner in the rollout of merchant-specific events throughout CEMEA, while also providing support for other client-focused events across the region. Qualifications What we’re after: Relevant Bachelor’s degree required; MBA is preferred for added depth and business acumen. Strong written, oral, and presentation skills, with proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and fluency in leveraging AI tools and agents. 12+ years of experience Demonstrated ab

data.title: VP - Digital Trade Solutions
data.jd_text: Job Summary : The role is a senior leadership role that focuses on driving digital transformation, developing technology-driven products, and enhance commercial value through digital channels. The role will spearhead the digital transformation of products, driving adoption of digital platforms, automating processes, and enhancing customer experience (user journey) to increase revenue and operational efficiency. Key Responsibilities Strategic Leadership: Develop and implement a digital strategy for trade solutions that aligns with organizational goals and market trends. Product Management & Commercialization: Lead the development, launch, and enhancement of digital trade channels, such as Port Community Systems (PCS), Supply Chain Finance platforms, and digital customs systems. Onboarding & Client Services: Manage the onboarding of clients and counterparties to digital platforms, driving adoption rates and ensuring a seamless user experience. Digital Transformation: Lead cross-functional initiatives to move from manual/paper-based trade processes to digital solutions (e.g., e-bills of lading). Operational Risk & Governance: Ensure all digital solutions are compliant with regulatory standards and internal risk frameworks, including KYC/AML in digital onboarding. Vendor & Stakeholder Management: Coordinate with external technology partners, consultants, and internal departments (IT, Sales, Legal, Operations) to implement solutions. Data Analysis & Performance Monitoring: Utilize dashboards and data insights to track transaction volumes, system performance, and ROI of digital platforms. Required Experience & Qualifications Education: A Bachelor's or Master’s degree in Business, Finance, Information Technology, or a related field. Experience: 7+ years of experience in trade finance, banking, or logistics technology, with proven success in leading digital product launches. Digital Literacy: Deep understanding of trade finance technologies (e.g., SWIFT, blockchain), FinTech solutions, and digital banking platforms. Leadership Skills: Proven ability to manage teams, influence stakeholders, and drive projects in a fast-paced environment.

data.title: Head of Business and Economics
data.jd_text: Job Description GEMS Wellington Academy – Al Khail is seeking to appoint an outstanding Head of Business and Economics from August 2026. The successful candidate will lead a high-performing department, ensuring excellent teaching, strong academic outcomes and a curriculum that prepares students for future pathways. Job Purpose: The Head of Business and Economics provides strategic and operational leadership of the department across Key Stages 4 and 5. The role focuses on delivering a high-quality curriculum, supporting staff development and ensuring all students achieve their full potential. Key Responsibilities: Lead curriculum planning, delivery and review across Business, Economics and vocational courses. Ensure high-quality teaching and consistent standards across the department. Monitor and analyse student progress, implementing targeted interventions where needed. Oversee assessment processes, including BTEC verification and exam preparation. Lead, support and develop teaching staff, including coaching and performance management. Promote a collaborative team culture with high expectations for staff and students. Engage with parents through reporting and school events. Develop links with universities, industry and external partners. Contribute to school self-evaluation, inspection readiness and improvement planning. Support whole-school priorities including literacy, inclusion and digital learning. Promote enrichment opportunities and support student career pathways. Skills Proven leadership experience within a secondary school setting. Strong subject knowledge in Business and/or Economics. Excellent understanding of the British curriculum and assessment frameworks. Strong communication, organisational and leadership skills. Ability to inspire and develop both students and staff. Recognised teaching qualification (PGCE or equivalent). Bachelor’s degree in a relevant subject; further qualifications desirable. GEMS Education is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of all of its students and staff. A UK-enhanced DBS or equivalent police check is a pre-requisite for all appointments.

data.title: Head of Subscription | Hertz | Financial Services Transformation & Development
data.jd_text: Job Requisition ID: 176545 Established in the 1930s as a trading business, Al-Futtaim Group today is one of the most diversified and progressive, privately held regional businesses headquartered in Dubai, United A”rab Emirates. Structured into five operating divisions; automotive, financial services, real estate, retail and healthcare; employing more than 35,000 employees across more than 20 countries in the Middle East, Asia and Africa, Al-Futtaim Group partners with over 200 of the world's most admired and innovative brands. Al-Futtaim Group’s entrepreneurship and relentless customer focus enables the organisation to continue to grow and expand; responding to the changing needs of our customers within the societies in which we operate. By upholding our values of respect, excellence, collaboration and integrity; Al-Futtaim Group continues to enrich the lives and aspirations of our customers each and every day. Overview Of The Role: The Head of Subscription supports the General Manager for Hertz and MOOV in overseeing daily operations, ensuring smooth and efficient service delivery, customer satisfaction, and business profitability. This role is pivotal in managing staff, fleet operations, financial performance, and customer service while maintaining compliance with company policies and industry regulations. Success in this role requires effective leadership in managing direct reports, fostering growth, and maintaining high service standards across all displays. What You Will Do: Operations Management Assist in managing daily monthly subscription operations, ensuring efficiency and adherence to company policies. Monitor fleet availability, maintenance schedules, and vehicle utilization to maximize revenue. Oversee compliance with safety standards, insurance policies, and regulatory requirements. Implement process improvements to enhance operational efficiency. Turnaround Time (TAT): Time taken to prepare and rent out returned vehicles. Fleet Availability: Ensuring the right vehicles are available at peak times. Customer Service & Satisfaction Ensure a high level of customer service is provided at all rental locations. Handle customer complaints and disputes, ensuring swift resolution and customer retention. Monitor customer feedback and implement service enhancements. Train staff on customer service best practices and brand standards. Net Promoter Score (NPS): Measures customer satisfaction and loyalty. Customer Retention Rate: Percentage of repeat customers. Financial & Business Performance Financial planning, budgeting, and revenue management. Analyze performance metrics, including revenue, fleet utilization, and operational costs. Assist in developing pricing strategies and promotional offers. Work on cost control initiatives to improve overall profitability. Revenue Growth: Year-over-year or month-over-month revenue increase. Fleet Utilization Rate: Percentage of cars rented versus available. Staff Management & Development Manage and mentor employees, customer service representatives, contract management and operational staff. Assist in hiring, training, and performance evaluations. Develop staff schedules and ensure adequate coverage during peak times. Foster a positive work environment and promote teamwork. Employee Productivity: Measured by subscriptions processed per employee. Effective in setting priorities and scheduling activities. Sales & Marketing Support Assist in implementing marketing campaigns and promotional initiatives. Develop partnerships to drive sales. Identify new business opportunities and contribute to growth strategies. Ensure sales targets and customer acquisition goals are met. Booking Conversion Rate: Percentage of inquiries converted into rentals. Upselling & Cross-selling Revenue: Additional revenue from upgrades, insurance, etc. Compliance & Risk Management Ensure that all rental agreements comply with company policies and legal regulations. Oversee adherence to vehicle maintenance schedules and safety inspections. Implement policies to minimize risks, including fraud prevention and damage control. Compliance & Audit Score: Ensuring adherence to safety and legal regulations. Smooth functioning of the department delivering desired service and quality levels. Required Skills To Be Successful: Strong customer relations and team building skills. Strong commercial acumen. Ability to maintain a good tracking system on applications for better management of business. Excellent communication, problem solving, and analytical skills. What Qualifies You For The Role: University graduate. At least 5 years of experience in the customer service industry. General understanding of the car rental industry. Fluent in English and Arabic. We’re here to provide excellent service but a little help from you can ensure a five-star candidate experience from start to finish. data.title: Regional Head of Operations - Real Assets ME
data.jd_text: The Apex Group was established in Bermuda in 2003 and is now one of the world’s largest fund administration and middle office solutions providers. Our business is unique in its ability to reach globally, service locally and provide cross-jurisdictional services. KPI’s to be consistently above the specified target for each team in your region (accuracy, timeliness, and delivery) Team capacity planning and full awareness of incoming pipeline, including ownership of hire requests. People management with a focus on development, motivation, and succession planning/talent management. Identify training needs within your teams in the region and interact with our training department. Manage employee performance and salary review process. Partner with the relevant Country Head, HR lead and senior leadership team to implement effective strategies for employee engagement and retention. Ensure adherence to all internal and external audit standards. Ensure stakeholder awareness, engagement, and support through effective and timely communication.
