data.title: Head of HVAC/Chillers Transition Strategy
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text: Job Title: Head of HVAC/Chillers Transition Strategy Posting Date: 2 Mar 2026 Requisition ID: 3557 Location: Barakah Posting Status: Active Recruitment Job Purpose Lead and govern the end to end Project Management function for HVAC systems, chiller replacement programs, cooling infrastructure upgrades, and long term maintenance and asset sustainability plans, ensuring that all projects derived from the Asset Management Plan (AMP), Long Term Asset Management (LTAM), Engineering Change Requests (ECRs), Plant Facilities initiatives, and Ad Hoc works are delivered in compliance with FANR requirements, IAEA standards, and ENEC Operations procedures.The role ensures safe, reliable, and lifecycle optimized HVAC and cooling systems that support plant availability, personnel safety, environmental protection, and long term operational resilience Key Activities, Responsibility & Accountability Lead the HVAC and Chiller Project Portfolio within ENEC Operations, covering capital projects, replacements, upgrades, refurbishments, and long‑term maintenance initiatives derived from AMP, LTAM, ECRs, Plant Facilities, and Ad‑Hoc scopes. Act as the single accountable domain owner for HVAC and chiller projects, ensuring technical consistency, prioritization, and alignment with plant operational needs. Translate strategic direction and governance requirements set by the Project Management Manager into executable, domain‑specific project plans and delivery strategies. Ensure portfolio‑level integration between project delivery, long‑term maintenance planning, and asset lifecycle objectives Lead all Project Management activities related to the initiation, planning, execution, and closeout of all HVAC and chiller projects. Ensure project initiation, planning, scheduling, cost control, risk management, and reporting activities are executed in compliance with corporate standards, regulatory requirements, and approved project governance frameworks. Lead key interface with other organizational functions on all related Project Management activities. Lead the management of key project stakeholder expectations and provide periodic communication to all relevant personnel as part of the project portfolio. Lead the development and ensure the accuracy and quality of all required project documentation for submission to Project Review Boards. Lead proactive identification and mitigation of technical, operational, supply chain, and execution risks, particularly for works performed in live plant environments. Support the Project Management Manager in portfolio‑level reporting and decision‑making through accurate domain insights. Provide leadership and coordination for HVAC SME and Chiller SME roles within the organization. Ensure technical standards, design philosophies, equipment selection criteria, and maintainability requirements are consistently applied across all HVAC and chiller projects. Ensure lessons learned, operating experience, and reliability data are systematically captured and embedded into future designs and long‑term maintenance plans. Responsibilities & Accountabilities (contd.) Provide leadership and coordination to HVAC/Chiller Execution Leads responsible for site execution and field coordination. Ensure alignment between project management plans and field execution strategies, particularly for online works, phased execution, and outage‑constrained activities. Interface with the Project Execution organization to ensure execution readiness, constructability, and compliance with plant access, safety, and quality requirements. Monitor execution performance, identifying risks or deviations early and implementing corrective actions in coordination with Project Execution leadershi Lead the development and execution of long term maintenance, refurbishment, and replacement strategies for chillers and HVAC systems aligned with long term asset management objectives. Ensure project scopes are aligned with asset lifecycle objectives, obsolescence management, and reliability‑centered maintenance principles. Coordinate with Maintenance and Engineering to ensure alignment between capital projects and long term maintenance program Contribute to the development and continuous improvement of systems, procedures, and governance applicable to HVAC and cooling infrastructure activities. Monitor and report operational and financial KPIs for the HVAC/chiller project portfolio and long‑term maintenance plans. Ensure accurate, timely, and transparent reporting to senior management and governance forum Professional Certifications Qualifications Bachelor's Degree or Diploma (or Equivalent) Degree in Engineering, Project Management or related Science. Experience 6+ years of experience in industrial, utility, or nuclear project management, plant modifications, and/or long term maintenance planning with demonstrated leadership of HVAC or large mechanical systems portfolio

data.title: AVP, Compliance - Global Corporate Finance (Emiratization)
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text: Job Purpose The Investment Banking & Markets (“IB&M”) Compliance team is responsible of providing compliance advisory services and support to all Investment Banking and Global Markets business areas. The team ensures that that regulatory risks for these business areas are identified, managed, escalated, and reported through FAB’s Compliance Program, encompassing regulatory change and control management, undertaking compliance risk assessments, management and business reporting, assurance activities and training delivered to business staff. The team ensure the business areas receive ongoing oversight for meeting compliance with UAE Federal Laws, SCA and CBUAE Regulations, as well as FAB’s internal Compliance policies and procedures. The role of AVP Compliance Advisory is responsible to support in providing Second Line of Defense compliance advisory support. The primary focus of the role will be to support the Investment Banking Coverage teams and Global Corporate Finance. Key Accountabilities Strategic Contribution Support implementation of the Bank’s Compliance strategy within the IB&M teams. Advisory and Administration Providing Compliance advice and guidance to relevant stakeholders. Support the Head of Compliance – Global Markets, GCF and Head of Surveillance to deliver the wider Compliance strategy, including Annual Compliance Plan, Compliance Risk Assessment, regulatory developments, regulatory engagement, and issue management. Lead Compliance involvement in the product review process, new business initiatives, cross border activities and financial promotions. Timely and diligent management of requests and queries received by the business. Coordinate with Regulatory Compliance (RC) and Financial Crime Compliance (FCC) functions to provide recommendations on compliance risks as and when needed. Support in the administration and response of external requests received from clients and counterparties. Developing and strengthening relationships to promote best practice and ensure a good understanding of Compliance requirements across IB&M. Deliver effective training and provide updates to IB&M businesses on compliance requirements, best practices, market news, international regulations, hot topics and regulatory changes. Provide support for Compliance surveillance activities where required. Continuous Improvement Proactively identify opportunities for continuous improvements related to the systems, controls, processes and practices based on risks and control deficiencies identified. Undertake training as directed and required. Reporting Prepare MI related to the business areas for internal and, where required, external reports. Minimum Qualifications Bachelor’s degree or higher in a field with demonstrable transferability to the role. Certification in Compliance preferred. Minimum Experience Minimum of five years’ experience with a bank or financial institution. Experience in a Compliance Advisory role, preferably in providing support to Corporate Finance or Investment Banking. Strong understanding of UAE rules and regulations. Excellent understanding of key Compliance risks and controls. Team player capable of meeting tight deadlines and managing challenging workloads. Excellent analytical skills and ability to support decisions with sound reasoning. Excellent communication skills, including delivering to Senior Management. Focused on driving tasks to completion at pace, whilst retaining a high level of attention to detail and accuracy.

data.title: Digital Consulting Director
data.jd_text: At AlixPartners, we solve the most complex and critical challenges by moving quickly from analysis to action when it really matters; creating value that has a lasting impact on companies, their people, and the communities they serve. By understanding, respecting, and honouring the needs of our employees, clients, and communities, AlixPartners actively promotes an inclusive environment. We strongly believe in the value that diversity brings to our experiences and are committed to the perpetual enhancements of initiatives, policies, and practices. We hold ourselves accountable by providing the space for authenticity, growth, and equity for everyone. AlixPartners has embraced a hybrid work model to provide flexibility and support our employees’ work-life integration. Our hybrid model combines a mix of in-person (at client site or AlixPartners office) and remote working. Travel is part of this position, but the frequency may vary based on client, team, and individual circumstances. What You’ll Do This is an exciting time to join our growing Middle East Digital and Technology team. You will be able to help evolve our offerings in a number of our core sectors as we are supporting our clients to tackle some of their most complex business problems. You will take an integral role in identifying, shaping and cultivating new opportunities to work with clients, much of the time in close partnership with other areas of the firm. This Director opportunity is a permanent position located in Riyadh and reports to a Partner. The ideal candidate will be at home operating at the intersection of business strategy and technology, partnering with corporate C-suite executives and Private Equity Operating Partners to drive business growth, transformation, and long-term value creation. We bring a distinctive combination of deep technical expertise and sharp commercial acumen to deliver sustainable impact hands-on. Examples of our past work include helping some of the world’s best known companies leverage technology to realise their business objectives, working with private equity investors to evolve their company portfolios, and supporting high-profile turnaround cases. This description is not designed to encompass a comprehensive listing of required activities, duties or responsibilities. What You’ll Need The ideal candidate will have a mixture of 15+ years, digital consulting and industry experience Hands-on leadership experience from a highly relevant Digital and Technology role in industry, in Saudi Arabia, would be desirable Extensive blue chip consulting experience in Digital and Technology strategy development, operating model, capability and governance design is essential Experience of delivering large and complex Digital transformation and technology execution programmes with multiple workstreams Industry experience preferred in Telecom, Retail, Oil & Gas Strong experience in relationship management, proposal development and strong commercial instincts are key. In addition, an ability to generate growing demand for our wider firm capabilities and resources in prospective and existing clients Demonstrable ability to understand AI concepts and how they are applied to the technology solutions Deep expertise with Technology stack(s), IT Architecture, Technology Operating Model and Organizational Design as well as ERP solutions etc. Experience working with clients formulating hypotheses of potential business issues and reasons for financial performance, and an ability to develop data-driven and analytical approaches for improving performance using Technology You will hold valid residency for Saudi Arabia, or be able to obtain such, (administrative support will be provided by AlixPartners to obtain permits); ability to obtain work permit to travel and work across EU Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, Arabic is advantageous Willingness to work outside of normal business hours, and in particular as unique projects/needs arise. Ability to work full time in an office and remote environment; physically able to sit/stand at a computer and work in front of a computer screen for significant portions of the workday Must become familiar with, and promote and abide by, our Core Values as defined by the AlixPartners’ Code of Conduct and foster an inclusive environment with people at all levels of an organisation Office Location: KAFD, Riyadh Working hours: 8:30am – 5:30pm, Sunday to Thursday, hybrid model The firm offers market-leading benefits that provide flexible options to support our employee’s needs, you can read more about the AlixPartners benefits here. This advert will be removed once a suitable candidate has been identified. AlixPartners is an equal opportunity employer. # LI-Hybrid

data.title: Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
data.jd_text: Job Description: Chief Executive Officer (CEO) Brief Description The CEO is responsible for providing visionary leadership and comprehensive strategic direction to the organization. The role focuses primarily on driving growth, innovation, and profitability, while ensuring full compliance with Capital Market Authority (CMA) regulations and managing the investment advisory and consulting sector to achieve the company's long-term goals. Tasks and Responsibilities Develop and communicate the company's mission, vision, and strategic goals to all stakeholders. Provide strategic leadership and guidance to the executive team and the entire organization. Collaborate with the board of directors to set strategic priorities. Directly oversee investment advisory services to ensure quality and compliance with the advisory license. Identify and capitalize on growth opportunities, market trends, and emerging developments. Develop and implement operational plans and policies that support the company's objectives. Ensure full compliance with Capital Market Authority (CMA) requirements and regulatory bodies. Oversee financial performance and budgets to ensure fiscal responsibility. Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, investors, and partners. Represent the company in public forums and industry events. Qualifications and Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, or a related field (Master’s or MBA is a plus). Holding the Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA) designation or having at least one year of proven practical experience in providing investment advice is highly preferred. Must hold the General Securities Qualification Certificate (CME-1). Previous experience as a CEO or in a similar executive leadership role. Deep understanding of business operations, strategy development, and financial management. Exceptional leadership skills and the ability to manage executive teams. High-level communication and negotiation skills. Competencies Strategic Leadership : Providing visionary direction and achieving long-term goals. Advisory Expertise : Ability to provide and guide financial consulting and professional advice. Collaboration : Working effectively with the board of directors, partners, and stakeholders. Decision-Making : Making data-driven decisions based on market analysis. Innovation : Driving continuous improvement in services and operations. Financial Management : Overseeing budgets and fiscal responsibility.

data.title: Cybersecurity BU Head
data.jd_text:
Make an impact with NTT DATA

Join a company that is pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We are renowned for our technical excellence and leading innovations, and for making a difference to our clients and society. Our workplace embraces diversity and inclusion – it’s a place where you can grow, belong and thrive.

Your day at NTT DATA

As a Cybersecurity BU Head at NTT DATA, you will be responsible for driving the growth and strategic direction of NTT DATA’s cybersecurity business across the region. This role combines advanced subject matter expertise with direct sales accountability, overseeing pipeline development, client engagement, and team leadership.

The BU Head works closely with Client Managers, pre-sales architects, and technology partners to pursue qualified leads, develop tailored solutions, and close complex deals. The role also involves expanding market presence, building strategic vendor relationships, and mentoring a team of cybersecurity sales specialists.

You'll play a vital role in developing and maintaining strong relationships with new and existing clients, conducting workshops, and delivering impactful presentations. Your expertise in security technology will enable you to support the closure of sales, address conceptual challenges, and maintain a comprehensive knowledge of our product and service offerings.

You'll be responsible for creating clear account plans, identifying client risks, and using sales methodologies to drive the sales process. Your ability to adapt quickly to new missions and deadlines, along with your client-centric approach to finding the best-fit solutions, will significantly impact achieving our sales goals.

To thrive in this role, you need to have:

An advanced understanding of security principles, concepts, and technologies, including NIST CSF, ISO 27001, and best practices in securing DATA and IT infrastructure.An excellent understanding of the technical concepts of security solutions and the ability to provide technical consultation to customers.Proven success in solution-based selling and achieving/exceeding sales targetsStrong client relationship skills up to the C-level, with the ability to deliver engaging sales presentations.The ability to maintain up-to-date, accurate sales forecasts and close plans.Proficiency in team-selling approaches and collaboration with internal teams.Knowledge of competitors and the ability to apply successful sales strategies.Flexibility to adapt swiftly to new missions, urgent deadlines, and changing client needs.A bachelor's degree or equivalent in a technical or sales field is preferred.Industry/Vendor sales certifications required.

Workplace type: 

Hybrid Working

About NTT DATA

NTT DATA is a $30+ billion trusted global innovator of business and technology services. We serve 75% of the Fortune Global 100 and are committed to helping clients innovate, optimize and transform for long-term success. We invest over $3.6 billion each year in R&D to help organizations and society move confidently and sustainably into the digital future. As a Global Top Employer, we have diverse experts in more than 50 countries and a robust partner ecosystem of established and start-up companies. Our services include business and technology consulting, data and artificial intelligence, industry solutions, as well as the development, implementation and management of applications, infrastructure, and connectivity. We are also one of the leading providers of digital and AI infrastructure in the world. NTT DATA is part of NTT Group and headquartered in Tokyo.

Equal Opportunity Employer

NTT DATA is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer with a global culture that embraces diversity. We are committed to providing an environment free of unfair discrimination and harassment. We do not discriminate based on age, race, colour, gender, sexual orientation, religion, nationality, disability, pregnancy, marital status, veteran status, or any other protected category. Join our growing global team and accelerate your career with us. Apply today.

Third parties fraudulently posing as NTT DATA recruiters

NTT DATA recruiters will never ask job seekers or candidates for payment or banking information during the recruitment process, for any reason. Please remain vigilant of third parties who may attempt to impersonate NTT DATA recruiters—whether in writing or by phone—in order to deceptively obtain personal data or money from you. All email communications from an NTT DATA recruiter will come from an @nttdata.com email address. If you suspect any fraudulent activity, please contact us.

data.title: Associate Engineering Director - MEP (Remote Area)
data.jd_text: Mace combines construction expertise with consultancy to unlock potential in every person or project and redefine the boundaries of ambition. Our values shape the way we consult and define the people we want to join us on our journey. The Project This project offers the chance to join a talented Mace team in delivering a major entertainment and luxury hospitality project, collaborating with renowned architects and global consultants. The project involves extensive infrastructure and built assets, delivered rapidly through a hands-on Construction Management approach, ideal for those experienced in managing both main and sub-contract packages. You’ll Be Responsible For Manage engineering aspects of MEP services, including Programmes, HSW, Quality & Commissioning. Collaborate with internal/external stakeholders to implement strategies and initiatives. Influence and negotiate with senior stakeholders up to C-suite level. Provide technical expertise for tendering, procurement strategies, and specialist packages. Review contracts, scope, schedules, and support commercial teams throughout lifecycle. Drive design reviews for compliance, carbon reduction, and operational energy standards. Implement commissioning strategies aligned with Mace standards and project milestones. Use digital tools for productivity assessments and risk identification. Ensure high-standard project close-out and deliver continuous process improvements. You’ll Need To Have Expertise in Mechanical, Electrical, Public Health, FLS, and systems integration (BMS, SCADA, PMS, CCMS, AV, Lighting Control, Tetra, Security/CCTV, Access Control, IPTV/IRS). Strong knowledge and application of design management principles. Excellent understanding of prefabrication and build strategies. Broad experience across multiple sectors delivering diverse building types. Meets KSA visa criteria with proven experience on high-value projects for PMC businesses. Awareness of carbon and sustainability factors shaping project delivery. Necessary visa criteria for the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and have relevant work experience working on high valued projects/ programmes for a project management consultancy business, which are required for immigration and client approval. Our values Safety first - Going home safe and well: We champion a safe, diverse and inclusive working environment, understanding the importance of wellbeing in every team. Client focus - Deliver on our promise: We own the quality of deliverables, strategic outcomes and build long term relationships with our clients. Integrity - Always do the right thing: We influence positive outcomes within our industry, while always aligning with our compliance obligations. Create opportunity - For our people to excel: We champion a continuous improvement culture throughout all activities, inspiring our people and teams to develop. Mace is an inclusive employer and welcomes interest from a diverse range of candidates. Even if you feel you do not fulfil all the criteria, please apply as you may still be the best candidate for this role or another role within our organisation. We are also open to discussing part time, flexible, and hybrid working options if suitable with-in the role

data.title: Technical Director
data.jd_text: The Technical Director at JASARA PMC is a key leadership position responsible for overseeing all technical aspects of project delivery across a diverse portfolio. This role involves strategic planning, resource management, and ensuring that projects meet industry standards and client expectations. The Technical Director will lead a team of engineers and technical professionals, fostering an innovative and collaborative environment. In this position, the Director will liaise with stakeholders to establish clear project objectives, provide technical guidance, and promote best practices in engineering and project management. Requirements Minimum of 20 years of experience in civil engineering, with a focus on large-scale infrastructure projects Master's Degree in Civil Engineering or a related field is preferred Demonstrable experience in a technical leadership role, preferably within project management consultancies Expertise in project delivery methodologies and best practices in design and construction Strong interpersonal and communication skills, able to effectively collaborate with diverse teams and stakeholders Proven ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously, ensuring quality and timely delivery Sound knowledge of relevant local and international engineering standards and regulations Strong analytical and decision-making skills, with a proactive approach to resolving technical challenges Experience with the implementation of innovative technologies and processes to enhance project outcomes Fluent in English, knowledge of Arabic is an advantage

data.title: Director of Operations
data.jd_text: Operations Director – Airport Parking & Mobility We are looking to connect with experienced professionals for Senior Business Analyst roles. The ideal profile includes: Operations Director – Airport Parking & Mobility Education • Bachelor’s Degree (Engineering / Operations / Business) • Lean / Six Sigma certification preferred Candidate Profile • 8–12 years in large-scale operations management • Experience in airport operations, parking concessions, transport terminals, or facility management • Proven track record implementing SOPs and structured systems • Experience managing large teams Position Objective To design, implement, and enforce professional operational processes, systems, and controls across all parking and mobility functions, ensuring structured, KPI-driven, audit-ready performance aligned with airport-grade standards. Core Responsibilities – Operational Control • Direct oversight of 24/7 parking, shuttle, towing, enforcement, and cash operations • Ensure real-time operational visibility across all terminals • Lead peak-hour traffic deployment planning • Manage incident escalation and crisis response • Guarantee operational continuity at all times Process Development & SOP Implementation • Develop and implement comprehensive SOP manual within 90 days • Standardize shift handover, incident reporting, towing workflow, shuttle dispatch process • Establish gate malfunction and cash reconciliation procedures • Eliminate ad-hoc operational instructions KPI & Performance Monitoring • Shuttle on-time performance ≥ 95% • Gate uptime ≥ 98% • Towing response time within SLA • Revenue leakage incidents minimized • 100% structured daily reporting compliance Control Room & Reporting Discipline • Establish central operational control structure • Implement daily operational reports and monthly dashboards • Ensure data accuracy and audit-ready documentation Subcontractor & SLA Enforcement • Monitor shuttle and towing contractors • Enforce SLA penalties and performance reviews • Ensure compliance with insurance, staffing, vehicle standards, and cleanliness • Prevent unauthorized subcontracting Workforce Discipline & Culture • Establish professional command structure • Train supervisors on structured reporting • Implement attendance control and productivity tracking • Develop middle management capability First 6-Month Deliverables • Complete SOP manual issued • KPI dashboard operational • Daily reporting standardized • SLA enforcement structure active • Reduction in operational incidents • Improved traffic flow metric

data.title: Project Director
data.jd_text: Al Jomaih Energy & Water (AEW) headquartered in Saudi Arabia, is a fast-growing investor, lead develper, and operator of utility and infrastructure projects in various geographical markets such as KSA, GCC, Asia & North Africa, with investments in 6 GW of Conventional and Solar power plants, and around 700,000 m3/day of water. AEW was established in 2007, as a subsidiary of Al Jomaih Holding Company, a leading Saudi conglomerate with a legacy spanning over 80 years, focused on enabling sustainable, social, and economic progress in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and beyond. At AEW, our mission is to tackle new technologies, solve complex problems, and leverage our expertise to unlock opportunities in the Power, Renewables & Water Sectors, while driving impact for our stakeholders across the value chain. Location : Site based (Nearest cities Al Khafji (approximately 70 km from the site); Nairyah (approximately 60 km from the site). Duration: Approx. 4 years Job Overview To lead and oversee all construction activities for the Water Treatment Plant. Ensuring the timely, on-budget, and quality delivery of the asset, meeting contractual requirements, and design specifications for reliable and sustainable operation over 25 years. Implementing the HSSE program and supervision in the Project Company and Owner's team, fostering a culture of health, safety, security, and environment in alignment with Project Company and Stakeholder targets. Monitoring, coordinating, and reporting on multiple Contractors' activities at the site, challenging and negotiating with the EPC contractor when necessary to protect the Project Company and Aljomaih Energy Water Company's interests. Directing the Owner's Engineer team at the site. Facilitating seamless communication and information exchanges among stakeholders, including project partners, local communities, and external parties. Requirements Your Job Functions (Duties & Responsibilities) Oversee plant construction to ensure compliance with quality, schedule, and budget agreements with Contractors and WTA. Manage HSSE implementation onsite and offsite, maintaining a strong HSSE culture. Coordinate activities of the Owner's Engineer, local contractors, local authorities, interface partners, and Contractors. Monitor and enforce EPC Contractor's compliance with Saudi Aramco IKTVA and Saudization targets. Act as the primary link between the off-taker, Owner's Engineer, EPC Contractor, third party, the Phase 1 Operator, --etc to align battery limits and tie-ins. Align technical and schedule interfaces between different contractors, ensuring contract conformity and project needs. Monitor Engineering & Procurement, ensure the EPC Contractor issuing the engineering packages and deliver procurement items ahead of construction needs. Organize and lead construction and progress management meetings with all stakeholders. Identify, assess, and escalate technical deviations, construction risks, and non-conformities to the Project Company CEO, proposing effective mitigation and recovery measures. Manage contractual, commercial, and technical matters arising under the EPC Contract and WTA, in coordination with legal and commercial teams. Review, evaluate, and provide recommendations on change orders, variations, and claims submitted by contractors and service providers. Lead resolution meetings for technical and construction issues encountered on-site and off-site during execution. Lead QA/QC oversight by managing the Owner's Engineer site engineering team to witness, verify, and document key inspections and tests in accordance with the approved Inspection and Test Plans (ITPs) and project requirements. Lead risk assessment and risk identification workshops with the EPC Contractor and Owner's Engineer, ensuring all project risks are identified, formally recorded in the risk register periodically, and supported by clear mitigation and action plans. Interact with Sponsors, Lender's Engineer, Insurance Advisers, and O&M teams. Issue progress reports and technical memoranda as required. Supervise milestone completion certificates, invoices, and mechanical completion certificates. Facilitate the handover of the Project to the O&M Team. Host visitors at the site and manage communications with stakeholders. Contractual/Commercial Familiarity with PPP/BOT project structures, project finance documents, EPC contracts, and WTA. Timely reporting of contractual/commercial variances and proposing mitigation or alternative solutions. Assist in writing contractual letters to stakeholders. Identify and anticipate deliverables to relevant parties. Coordinate progress of commissioning and testing with Off-taker and Dispatch Control Centre. Assist in claims management with Contractors and Service Providers. Qualifications & Skill Sets University Degree in Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, or Chemical Engineering. Additional qualifications (project management/Construction Management) are preferred 15 +years of project and s

data.title: Project Director
data.jd_text: Role: Project Director Location: Al Khobar, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia About Compass Project Consulting Compass Project Consulting is an award-winning, dynamic, and respected project development consultancy, providing high-quality, value-for-money, and fully tailored services to clients across all sectors. As highly regarded project leaders and effective communicators, our Team at Compass has delivered numerous projects for some of the region’s most ambitious and innovative change-makers. Our diverse and disciplined Team facilitates world-class project development solutions. Our core values guide our decisions and constitute our culture, empowering us to perform at our highest level on behalf of our clients. Drawing on our extensive delivery experience, industry-wide knowledge, and sophisticated project development tools, we facilitate the most proficient path toward project success, overcoming constraints and obstacles to deliver projects effectively and efficiently for our clients. We lead project success stories. Role Overview This is a senior leadership opportunity to join a renowned organization known for its high-calibre experience across the region’s built environment. We are seeking a Project Director to lead the delivery of a major mixed-use / retail development in Al Khobar. The role will act as the client’s representative, overseeing project delivery from design through to completion, ensuring alignment with programme objectives, commercial performance, and stakeholder expectations. You will provide strategic leadership across project delivery, commercial governance, and stakeholder engagement, ensuring the successful execution of a high-profile development. Key Responsibilities Project Leadership Lead overall project delivery as the client representative Establish and drive project strategy, governance, and execution plans Manage and oversee multidisciplinary teams across design, commercial, and construction Delivery & Performance Oversee project delivery across time, cost, quality, and risk Monitor contractor and consultant performance Resolve complex delivery challenges and drive project outcomes Commercial & Contractual Oversight Provide leadership on commercial strategy and contractual matters Oversee cost control, variations, claims, and dispute resolution Support key commercial decisions to protect project value Stakeholder Management Act as the primary interface with the client and key stakeholders Lead engagement with authorities, consultants, and contractors Provide clear reporting and strategic updates to senior stakeholders Risk & Governance Establish project governance and reporting structures Identify and mitigate key project risks Ensure compliance with client and project standards Candidate Requirements Minimum 20 years of experience in project management and delivery Proven delivery of at least two major mixed-use or retail developments (100,000 m² BUA) within the GCC Strong experience in Design & Build environments and working with separate CSC contracts Demonstrated experience in a client-facing or client representative role Strong commercial awareness with experience in contractual and financial decision-making Professional certifications such as PMP, MRICS, PgMP, or equivalent Experience working on large-scale developments in Saudi Arabia Strong leadership and stakeholder management capability Ability to operate in a complex, multi-stakeholder environment Application To express your interest in a career with us, please apply via the link attached. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted by a Talent Acquisition representative. Website: https://compass-pc.com/about-compass Instagram: @compass_pc Evolving Tomorrow, Today

data.title: Project Director
data.jd_text: Lead landmark hospitality and high-rise developments in KSA Join a financially strong contractor with a growing mega-project pipeline About Our Client Our client is a leading private-sector contractor with a strong reputation for delivering large-scale real estate, hospitality, and mixed-use developments across the Kingdom. They are well-funded, financially stable, and expanding rapidly due to a robust pipeline of iconic projects. Job Description As project director, you'll be responsible for: Leading end-to-end delivery of major hospitality and high-rise projects Oversee project planning, execution, and timely delivery within budget Manage multidisciplinary teams and ensure alignment with project goals Coordinate with stakeholders, including PPP and PIF entities Ensure compliance with contractual, safety, and quality standards Drive risk management and implement mitigation strategies The Successful Applicant Our client is seeking: Degree-qualified Civil Engineer with 15+ years of experience Proven track record in high-rise towers and mega hospitality projects Strong exposure to PPP projects (mandatory) and PIF-funded developments Previous experience in Saudi Arabia or Egypt Exceptional leadership, stakeholder management, and delivery skills What's on Offer In return, our client can offer: Competitive salary up to circa 50,000 SARR per month Discretionary annual bonus Opportunity to lead iconic projects shaping KSA's hospitality sector Long-term career growth with a reputable and expanding contractor Contact: Sebastian Andreasen Quote job ref: JN-122025-6903711

data.title: Project Director- Highrise
data.jd_text: Job Description WSP Middle East is seeking a high-caliber Senior Project Director to lead the successful delivery of a landmark high-rise tower in Riyadh,KSA. The successful candidate will have an unwavering commitment to quality across every aspect of delivery — from the service we provide and the client relationships we build, to the teams we lead and the construction works we supervise. You will be responsible for overseeing all phases of project delivery, ensuring alignment with client expectations, budget, schedule, and WSP’s commitment to excellence. Responsibilities RESPONSIBILITIES Lead the delivery of a high-rise / supertall tower project, ensuring the highest quality standards are upheld at every stage. Foster a strong client relationship by consistently delivering exceptional service and acting as the primary point of contact. Build and manage high-performing multidisciplinary teams, ensuring collaboration and accountability. Ensure assigned projects are completed within budget and schedule, while meeting client requirements and business objectives. Oversee all aspects of project execution, including design management, construction supervision, cost control, and stakeholder engagement. Provide strategic leadership and guidance throughout the full project lifecycle. Drive innovation, sustainability, and digital best practices in project delivery. Represent WSP at senior-level meetings and within broader industry forums as required. Qualifications/Experience QUALIFICATIONS A degree in a discipline suited to the work to be delivered, with the ability to converse in technical matters across all disciplines associated with our Construction Supervision projects. 20+ years’ experience in leading construction supervision of multi-disciplinary projects. Working for an international consultancy for 20+ years and over 5 years of experience in the Middle East. Exceptional leadership and communication skills with a strong client interface presence. Proven experience in managing large-scale tunnelling and hospitality projects, with a deep understanding of the technical aspects involved. PMP, RICS, or similar project management certification is an advantage. Pre-contract design management experience under the RIBA framework (e.g., coordination during RIBA Stages 2–4, oversight of multidisciplinary design teams, value engineering, and design governance). High-rise project leadership experience, clearly noting the scale (GFA, height, no. of floors) and complexity (mixed-use, hospitality, residential, or commercial) of the developments. Early Contractor Involvement (ECI) delivery strategy exposure, such as constructability reviews, logistics planning, sequencing, and value engineering input at early design stages. Imagine a better future for you and a better future for us all. Work on landmark projects around the world and embrace opportunities to make an even bigger impact in the communities you care about. What if you could do the best work of your life and create a legacy for yourself? With us, you can. Apply today.

data.title: Project Controls Director
data.jd_text: Job Description As a Project Planning and Controls Director, you will play a key role by further developing and enhancing the way we plan and deliver our projects. Working closely with our project and business leaders, the successful candidate will be responsible for the development and delivery of project planning and controls services and initiatives across our impressive portfolio of design and supervision projects. Responsibilities Strategic leadership, development, and growth of the WSP ME ‘Project Planning and Controls’ team. Establish and manage a robust way of working to deliver full project lifecycle project planning and control services. Manage relationships with key internal stakeholders and clients and their representatives. A primary focus on design delivery, with growth opportunities in supporting supervision projects. Drive excellence in prospect and proposal planning and the development of programme inputs and strategies. Drive an integrated and engaging team culture, focused on consistency and quality. Development and management of project control methods, tools, and techniques. Collaborate with the i3D Digital Solutions team to drive the adoption of latest technologies and automation. Develop comprehensive project plans that align with organizational goals and ensure successful project execution. Lead and mentor project teams, fostering a collaborative and high-performance work environment. Oversee budgeting, cost control, and financial forecasting to ensure projects are completed within budget. Identify, assess, and mitigate risks to minimize impact on project timelines and budgets. Qualifications A minimum of 15 years’ professional experience in project planning and controls. Bachelor’s degree in engineering, construction management or equivalent. Experience in the delivery of project planning and controls services across the full project lifecycle A digital focus with energy to lead and grow a highly efficient and quality output focused team. Expertise in creating and managing detailed project schedules using tools like Primavera P6, ensuring timely project delivery. Implement and maintain quality control processes to ensure all project deliverables meet the highest standards. Excellent verbal and written communication skills to effectively interact with stakeholders at all levels. Strong ability to address and resolve complex issues efficiently, ensuring minimal disruption to project timelines. Proficient in project management software and tools, particularly Primavera P6, for planning, scheduling, and control. Flexibility to adapt to changing project requirements and environments, ensuring continuous alignment with business goals. Imagine a better future for you and a better future for us all. Join our close-knit community of talented individuals who share your passion for making a positive impact. Our global team includes more than 69,000 employees, working together to make a difference in communities both close to home and around the world. With us, you can. Apply today.

data.title: MEP Head of Commissioning
data.jd_text:
Hiring for OCA GLOBAL
Job Summary:We are seeking a commercially driven and technically strong leader to establish and scale our MEP Commissioning & Technical Services division in Saudi Arabia. The role is accountable for business development, operational delivery, team leadership, and divisional P&L performance, covering Mechanical, Electrical, and Testing & Balancing (TAB) services.
Key Responsibilities:Establish and grow the MEP Commissioning division in the Saudi market.Develop and convert sales pipeline with major contractors and consultants.Lead technical execution of MEP commissioning, TAB, and electrical testing (LV/MV systems).Manage and mentor engineers and technicians.Oversee proposals, pricing, and contract negotiations.Ensure compliance with international standards and client/consultant requirements.Drive divisional revenue, cost control, and margin performance.
Mandatory Requirements:Bachelor’s degree in mechanical or electrical engineering.10–15 years of commissioning experience with leadership exposure.Strong background in HVAC, TAB, and electrical testing.Proven experience in Saudi Arabia market.Experience in budgeting, cost control, and project profitability.
Preferred Certifications (Advantage):Certified Commissioning Professional (CCP)ISO 9001 Lead AuditorNEBOSH / IOSHCertified Energy Manager (CEM)

data.title: Regional Commercial Director
data.jd_text: About the Role Future Pipe Industries is seeking an experienced Regional Commercial Director to lead and coordinate sales and commercial activities across the Middle East and North Africa region. In this role, you will translate the global commercial strategy into regional execution plans, driving revenue growth, market expansion, and profitability. You will serve as the key link between global commercial leadership and regional sales teams, overseeing pipeline management, strategic account development, and the successful execution of key commercial opportunities. Key Responsibilities • Regional Commercial Strategy Execution: Translate the global commercial strategy into regional sales plans aligned with FPI's business goals, and coordinate commercial initiatives across MENA markets to ensure consistent execution. • Regional Sales Performance Management: Oversee and monitor the performance of General Manager Sales roles across MENA, reviewing pipeline development, sales forecasts, and key project opportunities to ensure targets are met. • Strategic Accounts & Major Projects: Support negotiation and closure of major regional contracts and strategic projects, while maintaining strong relationships with key clients, consultants, contractors, and partners. • Market Expansion: Identify new business opportunities and sectors within the MENA region, and support market entry strategies and expansion initiatives in emerging markets. • Commercial Governance & Risk Management: Ensure adherence to company policies on pricing strategy, contract governance, and commercial risk management. Monitor margin performance and enforce commercial discipline across regional operations. • Leadership & Team Development: Provide leadership, guidance, and coaching to General Manager Sales and regional sales teams. Support talent development, succession planning, and capability building across the regional sales organization. Qualifications & Experience • Bachelor's Degree in Engineering or a relevant commercial discipline • 15+ years of experience in sales or commercial roles within an industrial or infrastructure environment • Minimum 8-10 years of leadership experience in sales or commercial management • Experience managing commercial operations across multiple MENA markets • Strong background in industrial projects, EPC contractors, or infrastructure sectors Job-Specific Skills • Strong understanding of regional industrial markets and the competitive landscape • Excellent negotiation and relationship management skills • Ability to lead and coordinate multi-country commercial teams • Strategic thinking and strong commercial acumen • Strong financial awareness with the ability to manage revenue and margin targets • Ability to operate effectively in a dynamic and diverse regional environment Core Competencies • Strategic Thinking – Translates global strategy into regional execution plans • Leadership – Inspires and guides teams to achieve commercial objectives • Customer Focus – Builds strong long-term relationships with key clients and partners • Communication – Communicates clearly across diverse teams and stakeholders • Market Insight – Demonstrates strong understanding of regional market dynamics • Financial Acumen – Ensures commercial decisions support profitability and growth

data.title: VP, Chief of Shared Services
data.jd_text: Job Scope As the Shared Services Leader, you will own the strategic roadmap for our centralized operations. Your mandate is to integrate Finance, Procurement, Logistics, and Admin services into a single, high-performing ecosystem. This is a high-visibility role reporting to the C-suite, focused on institutionalizing operational excellence, driving digital transformation, and optimizing. Main Responsibilities Strategic Governance: Define and execute the long-term vision for Shared Services , ensuring alignment with broader corporate objectives. Financial Stewardship: You will oversee multi-million budgets, drive capital allocation for operational tech, and deliver measurable ROI through cost-containment. Supply Chain & Logistics Oversight: Orchestrate the integration of warehousing and procurement, ensuring a resilient, data-driven supply chain that mitigates global risk. Digital Transformation: Lead the migration toward Intelligent Automation (RPA), , and ERP integration to move the needle from "manual" to "predictive". Stakeholder Influence: Act as a strategic partner to BU leaders, navigating complex internal politics to drive adoption and satisfaction across the organization. Academic Qualifications Bacholer’s degree in Finance, Supply Chain, or International Business or equivalent. Proven leadership experience, with at least 5 years leading multi-functional teams. Core Competencies Transformational Leadership: The ability to lead through change and build a culture of continuous improvement. Executive Presence: You are as comfortable in the boardroom as you are on the warehouse floor; your communication is clear, data-backed, and influential. Global Acumen: Experience managing cross-border operations, diverse talent pools, and international regulatory frameworks. Strategic Sourcing & Vendor Ecosystems: High-level negotiation skills that go beyond "price" to "value-based" partnerships.

data.title: Mechanical Director
data.jd_text: Company Description DataVolt is a global leader in sustainable and innovative data center solutions. We provide businesses across the world with the infrastructure they need to connect, collaborate, and grow in the data-driven landscape of today. Our solutions focus on security, reliability, scalability, and sustainability, empowering our clients to truly achieve digital transformation. We have strategically positioned operations in Riyadh, Dubai, California, Mumbai and Tashkent and are committed to shaping a sustainable digital future through groundbreaking innovation. Responsibilities You will lead and manage all mechanical engineering activities for our data centre projects. You will play a critical role in ensuring the efficiency, reliability, and sustainability of our data centre infrastructure. Oversee the design, implementation, and maintenance of mechanical systems for data centres. Lead a team of engineers and technicians, providing guidance and expertise. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure seamless integration of mechanical systems with other data centre operations. Manage project timelines, budgets, and resources effectively. Ensure compliance with industry standards, safety regulations, and best practices. Identify opportunities for innovation and improvement in mechanical systems and processes. Experience & Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering or a related field (master’s preferred). 15+ years of experience in critical services and a proven track record of mechanical design engineering. Proven experience in mechanical management within data centre Strong leadership and project management skills. Excellent problem-solving and analytical abilities. Familiarity with industry standards and best practices.

data.title: Strategic Accounts Management Director (MedTech)
data.jd_text: Global MedTech Leader | Enterprise Partnerships | Executive-Level Impact A globally recognized medical technology leader with a strong presence across KSA and international markets is seeking a Strategic Account Management Director to lead enterprise-level partnerships across its most critical private healthcare systems. This is not a traditional sales leadership role. This is a country-level strategic leadership position responsible for orchestrating multi-therapy engagement, building C-suite partnerships, and unlocking sustainable long-term growth across complex healthcare ecosystems. The organization operates across multiple therapy areas and brings innovation that impacts millions of patients globally. The role offers high visibility, strong regional exposure, and leadership team participation. The Opportunity As Strategic Account Management Director, you will: Lead the country’s strategic accounts organization Own enterprise-level relationships with selected private healthcare groups Sit on the local Leadership Team Drive sustainable revenue growth, share expansion, and therapy penetration Mobilize cross-functional teams across multiple business units Represent the organization at CEO and executive level within major hospital systems You will act as the strategic connector across therapies — ensuring the full strength of the portfolio is brought to each key account. Key Responsibilities Develop and maintain senior executive relationships across strategic hospital systems Identify, qualify, and drive multi-therapy enterprise opportunities Design and execute structured strategic account plans Lead long-term commercial agreements and partnerships Deliver revenue and profitability targets Coach and develop high-performing strategic account leaders Drive forecasting discipline and pipeline visibility Operate effectively within a complex matrix environment Contribute actively to country-level strategic direction What Success Looks Like Sustainable growth across enterprise accounts Expansion of therapy footprint within strategic partners Long-term contractual partnerships across multiple therapy areas Strong cross-functional alignment Executive-level credibility within the healthcare ecosystem Ideal Profile 15+ years of progressive commercial leadership experience Strong background in medical devices or healthcare technology Experience managing complex hospital systems Demonstrated success in matrix organizations Executive presence and comfort engaging C-suite stakeholders Strong business acumen and financial literacy Experience leading and developing senior teams Fluent in English

data.title: Head of Customer Success
data.jd_text: This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Head of Customer Success in Saudi Arabia. We are seeking a strategic and results-driven Head of Customer Success to lead and scale the customer experience function across global markets. This role is responsible for shaping the vision, strategy, and operational excellence of the entire Customer Success organization, including technical support and regional teams. You will drive customer retention, satisfaction, and growth while fostering a culture of service excellence, accountability, and collaboration. This position offers the opportunity to influence cross-functional initiatives, mentor emerging leaders, and implement scalable systems that enhance the customer journey across a fast-paced, highly regulated environment. Accountabilities The Head of Customer Success will: Develop and execute a comprehensive customer success strategy aligned with overall business objectives, focusing on retention, satisfaction, and product adoption. Lead, coach, and mentor Customer Success leaders and regional teams, fostering a high-performance, customer-centric culture. Optimize systems, processes, and operational workflows to ensure effective, scalable, and compliant customer support. Maintain oversight of regulatory compliance (KYC, AML, data privacy) and quality assurance programs. Act as a cross-functional partner with Product, Engineering, Compliance, Sales, and Marketing teams, representing Customer Success in strategic initiatives. Serve as the escalation point for high-priority customer issues and complex operational challenges. Track and report key success metrics, including revenue growth, client retention, CSAT, and operational adoption of SOPs and KPIs. Requirements Ideal candidates should have: 7+ years of progressive leadership experience managing multi-disciplinary client-facing teams in fintech, banking, or wealth management. Proven ability to manage P&L or revenue targets and drive operational change across senior teams. Deep understanding of retail and institutional client onboarding processes and global regulatory requirements (KYC/AML). Exceptional leadership, coaching, and conflict resolution skills. Strong cross-functional communication, decision-making, and stakeholder management abilities. Familiarity with cryptocurrency or a strong personal interest and knowledge in the space is highly advantageous. Ability to work remotely with a dedicated workspace and reliable high-speed internet connection. Alignment with values-driven leadership principles and a commitment to ethical customer engagement. Benefits Fully remote role with the flexibility to work from anywhere while impacting global customer experience. Opportunity to shape a high-impact function in a fast-growing, innovative organization. Flexible work schedule and PTO plan to support work-life balance. Professional growth opportunities with a high-performance, globally distributed team. Annual learning and development budget to pursue individual goals, conferences, and certifications. Exposure to cutting-edge technology and regulatory practices in the financial services and crypto sectors. Why Apply Through Jobgether? We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team. We appreciate your interest and wish you the best! Why Apply Through Jobgether? Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time. We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

data.title: Head of Product
data.jd_text: 🤖 Head of Product - FinTech 📍 Riyadh 💰 SAR 55,000 - 60,000 per month I am looking for a strategic and hands-on Head of Product to lead the vision, strategy, and delivery of a cutting-edge fintech platform in Riyadh. This is a rare opportunity to build, scale, and own products that align with my clients group strategy and regulatory requirements. 1. Product Strategy & Ownership Define and drive the product vision and roadmap, aligned with the MD/CPO and overall group strategy. Translate regulatory, risk, and commercial requirements into actionable product initiatives. Prioritize initiatives using data, customer insights, and regulatory considerations, including SAMA Sandbox phases and go/no-go gates. Maintain a clear product architecture and scope across core platforms, customer journeys, integrations, and APIs. 2. Product Delivery & Execution Lead the end-to-end product lifecycle: discovery, requirements gathering, UX design, specifications, delivery, launch, and post-launch optimization. Break roadmap themes into well-structured epics and user stories with clear acceptance criteria for engineering. Facilitate sprint planning, backlog grooming, and agile rituals to ensure timely, high-quality delivery. Embed regulatory, risk, compliance, and security requirements into product design from the outset. Manage product documentation for SAMA Sandbox and partners, including flows, BRDs/PRDs, risk controls, and customer journeys. 3. Leadership & Team Management Lead, mentor, and develop mid-level Product Managers into a high-performing product team. Set clear objectives, KPIs, and development plans for team members. Establish and uphold product best practices, including discovery methods, documentation standards, experimentation frameworks, and stakeholder updates. Foster a culture of accountability, ownership, and fast-paced delivery. 4. Stakeholder Management & Governance Serve as the primary product liaison to the MD/CPO and leadership team. Collaborate closely with Risk & Compliance, Technology, Operations & Customer Support, and Commercial & Partnerships teams. Contribute to SAMA interactions from a product perspective, explaining flows, controls, and proposed changes. 5. Data, Customer Insight & Continuous Improvement Define, monitor, and optimize core product KPIs (activation, usage, conversion, NPS, risk metrics). Establish feedback loops through user research, client input, and operational insights, translating findings into actionable backlog items. Use experimentation, including A/B tests, pilots, and controlled rollouts, to validate product hypotheses within regulatory limits. Continuously refine customer journeys to minimize risk, cost, friction, and time-to-value. Who You Are A hands-on product leader with proven fintech experience. Deep understanding of SAMA regulations and experience building compliant, scalable financial products. Skilled in leading and growing product teams. A strong collaborator, capable of balancing multiple stakeholder priorities across regulatory, technical, and commercial domains. This role offers the chance to shape the future of fintech in Riyadh while leading a talented product team in a highly dynamic environment.

data.title: Head of Anti-Fraud
data.jd_text: Department: Risk B2C Employment Type: Full Time Location: KSA Reporting To: Sergey Bogdanov Description We are seeking a dynamic and experienced professional to join our team as the Head of Anti-Fraud, a key position within our rapidly growing BNPL company. In this role, you will be responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive fraud risk management strategy to safeguard our platform and customers from fraudulent activities in line with SAMA regulations. Key Responsibilities Utilize data analysis, statistical modelling, and fraud detection tools to identify suspicious patterns, trends, and anomalies in financial transactions. Implement monitoring systems to promptly detect and investigate potential fraud incidents. Develop an Anti-Fraud strategy within the company as per SAMA Anti-Fraud Rules Execute all required processes that are primarily designed to manage antifraud risk or comply with regulations, policies and procedures including conducting the required investigations. Contribution in the preparation of the antifraud reports to the Board of Directors. Proactively monitor and review any activities, transactions and data suspected of possible fraud by utilizing appropriate analytical techniques. Work well with our credit risk team to establish rules and scenarios for Fraud prevention, mitigation and detection and continuously enhance them. Act as the main POC for the regulator (SAMA) in terms of exhibiting Tabby’s anti-fraud unit, its strategies, policies, processes, registers, and metrics. Create awareness internally at Tabby on the risks of fraud Research and stay on top of all trends related to Fraud especially in BNPL, digital financing, payments, and other fintech related fields and work with our risk and product teams to set into place the required systems to mitigate that fraud risk Conduct a periodic assessment of monitoring rules to validate the efficiency of frauds detection and prevention systems. Skills, Knowledge & Expertise A bachelor’s degree in Mathematics, Computer Science or a related technical ﬁeld. 8+ years of working on fraud prevention (within a fintech would be a huge bonus). Have led previously end-to-end standardization of fraud risk identification and detection procedures including the assessment of fraud risk scenarios. Experienced working with SAMA regulations Strong analytical and data capabilities SQL/Python is a plus. Visualisation technologies such as Tableau is a plus. You thrive in ambiguous environments. Ask incisive questions and have an intellectual curiosity. You have a structured approach to problem-solving alongside strong quantitative skills. Strong verbal and written communication skills, including an ability to effectively lead interactions with both business and technical teams. Job Benefits We are an international team of inspired professionals located all over the globe. We have an inclusive company culture, embracing diversity, integrity and transparency. We strive for work-life balance and cherish the moments you spend with your loved ones, off-work. In the same spirit as for our product, we are caring and nurturing for our employees. Our people are granted 100% trust and freedom to apply their own vision and come up with their ideas from day 1 at Tabby. You are the one who takes responsibility for your area of work. We encourage everyone to think and make decisions like Tabby was their own business, well because it is. Our employee stock options programme is available for everyone. You will have an opportunity to learn and grow in one of the fastest growing fin tech companies in the region We offer you relocation support as well as we guide you through all the process. We’ll set you up with the devices required for your work.

data.title: Head of Finance
data.jd_text: Overview A well-established organization within the social sector is seeking a Head of Finance to lead its financial strategy, governance, and overall financial management. This role requires a seasoned finance leader with direct experience working within non-profit organizations or closely partnering with them , ensuring alignment with sector-specific regulatory, funding, and reporting requirements. The successful candidate will play a key role in driving financial sustainability, strengthening governance frameworks, and supporting executive leadership through data-driven financial insights. Key Responsibilities Lead the development and execution of the organization’s financial strategy and long-term financial planning. Oversee the annual budgeting cycle, ensuring effective allocation and utilization of financial resources. Manage financial reporting, accounting operations, and compliance with relevant regulations and standards. Provide insightful financial analysis to support strategic decision-making by senior leadership. Establish and enhance internal controls, governance frameworks, and financial policies. Monitor cash flow, financial performance, and organizational risk exposure. Prepare and present financial reports to executive leadership and governance committees. Lead, mentor, and develop the finance function to ensure high performance and continuous improvement. Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or Business Administration. Master’s degree (MBA or Finance) is preferred. Professional certifications such as CPA, CMA, or ACCA are highly desirable. Experience Minimum of 20 years of progressive experience in finance leadership roles. Proven experience within non-profit organizations or in roles involving direct engagement with the non-profit/social sector is essential. Strong background in financial planning, financial management, and governance. Demonstrated experience operating within regulated environments and managing compliance requirements. Proven ability to partner with senior leadership and contribute to strategic decision-making. Key Competencies Financial strategy and planning Financial analysis and reporting Risk management and internal controls Data-driven decision making Stakeholder communication and presentation skills Leadership and team development

data.title: Data & Analytics Director
data.jd_text: Position Summary: The Director – Data Analytics will lead the end-to-end analytics strategy for TOD, transforming data into actionable insights that drive business performance, growth, and customer experience across the OTT ecosystem. The role combines strong technical depth with commercial acumen, acting as a strategic partner to senior leadership across Product, Marketing, Commercial, Technology, and Operations. Will be deeply focused on developing TOD and BMG data into a monetizable asset. Key Responsibilities and Accountabilities: Data Strategy & Governance Own and define TOD’s data analytics vision, roadmap, and operating model. Oversee data collection, storage, processing, and governance to ensure data quality, integrity, and security. Drive the integration of new and existing data platforms, tools, and sources. Advanced Analytics & Insights Lead advanced analytics initiatives including predictive modeling, machine learning, and customer behavior analysis. Translate complex datasets into clear, actionable business insights supporting decision-making at executive level. Identify trends, patterns, risks, and opportunities across content performance, subscriptions, churn, revenue, and engagement. Big Data & Technology Enablement Guide the use of big data technologies (e.g. cloud platforms, Spark, Hadoop, modern data stacks). Partner closely with Technology and Product teams to ensure analytics solutions are scalable, efficient, and future-ready. Business Partnership & Stakeholder Management Act as a trusted analytics advisor to senior leadership and cross-functional teams (Product, Martech, Marketing, Sales, Operations, Finance). Translate business requirements into analytics use cases and measurable outcomes. Present insights, dashboards, and recommendations in a clear and concise manner to executive stakeholders. Performance Management Define, monitor, and continuously improve KPIs and dashboards across the organization. Measure the effectiveness and ROI of analytics initiatives and data-driven programs. Team Leadership Build, lead, and mentor a high-performing analytics team. Foster a data-driven culture across TOD through capability building and best practices. Education: Minimum bachelor’s degree in data science, Computer Science, Statistics, Engineering, or a related field (Master’s preferred). Experience: 10+ years of progressive experience in data analytics, with senior leadership experience in a complex, digital or consumer-facing environment. Experience in OTT, media, digital platforms, telecom, or subscription-based businesses is highly desirable. Proven track record of leading large-scale analytics initiatives and teams.

data.title: Director of Engineering
data.jd_text: Position Overview The Technical & Engineering Director is responsible for leading the engineering function and ensuring the successful technical delivery of complex infrastructure and mixed‑use development projects, from concept through handover. The role provides strategic direction, governance, and oversight across all engineering disciplines to ensure compliance with international standards, contractual requirements, and the company’s business objectives. Key Responsibilities Provide overall leadership and management of the Technical & Engineering Department, including civil, structural and architectural disciplines, ensuring high standards of performance and technical excellence. Establish, implement and continuously improve engineering policies, standards, procedures and best practices in line with international codes and project requirements. Oversee the full technical lifecycle of major infrastructure, roads, utilities and mixed‑use building projects (design, design review, construction support and handover), ensuring quality, safety, cost and time objectives are achieved. Lead the technical component of design‑and‑build and EPC tenders, including preparation and review of technical submissions, value engineering proposals, risk identification and mitigation, and constructability assessments. Review and approve design documents, calculations, drawings, specifications and shop drawings, and ensure effective coordination between civil, structural, architectural and MEP disciplines as well as external stakeholders. Develop and implement design risk‑mitigation strategies and make decisions on design changes, technical alternatives and value‑engineering opportunities that enhance project outcomes. Work closely with Project, Commercial and Contracts teams to address technical aspects of variations, claims, contractual correspondence and technical clarifications with clients and consultants. Monitor engineering progress across projects, identify bottlenecks and risks, and ensure timely provision of technical support and solutions to site teams. Represent the company in high‑level technical meetings with clients, consultants, authorities and partners, maintaining strong professional relationships and protecting the company’s technical and commercial interests. Prepare, manage and control the Technical & Engineering Department budget, including resource planning, allocation and optimization across projects. Drive innovation and digitalization within the engineering function through the adoption of advanced modelling, automation tools and interfaces with CAD and other engineering software to improve quality and efficiency. Lead the recruitment, onboarding, coaching and development of engineers, ensuring effective succession planning and a strong pipeline of technical talent. Qualifications and Experience Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering is required; a Master’s degree in Structural Engineering or a related discipline is preferred. Minimum of 20 years of progressive experience in civil/infrastructure and building projects, including at least 8–10 years in a senior technical leadership role (e.g. Technical Director, Engineering Director, Technical Manager). Demonstrable experience in the delivery of large‑scale infrastructure, roads, utilities and high‑rise or mixed‑use developments, preferably within the GCC region. Strong exposure to design‑and‑build and EPC contract models, including bid preparation, design management and value engineering. Professional memberships and certifications (e.g. PMI/PMP, national engineering council registration, LEAN/LEED/GSAS exposure) are highly desirable. Skills and Competencies Deep technical knowledge of civil and structural engineering, including static and dynamic analysis of concrete and steel structures in accordance with international codes (e.g. ACI, AISC, IBC, BS). Proven ability to align technical solutions with business strategy, project commercial objectives and client expectations. Strong leadership capabilities with a track record of managing large, diverse and multidisciplinary engineering teams. Advanced skills in design review, multi‑discipline coordination and resolution of complex technical issues on fast‑track projects. Solid understanding of contracts, claims and variation procedures from a technical standpoint, with the ability to support and defend the company’s position. Excellent communication, negotiation and stakeholder‑management skills; fluency in English is required, and Arabic is an advantage. High level of analytical thinking, problem‑solving, decision‑making and resilience under pressure.

data.title: Subsurface Director
data.jd_text:
Job PurposeThe Subsurface Director leads all subsurface activities of the company. This includes geology, geophysics, reservoir engineering, petrophysics, geomechanics, and subsurface data. The role covers the full lifecycle—from exploration to field development and production—and ensures safe, efficient, and maximum recovery of resources while supporting the company’s long-term energy strategy.
Experience & Qualifications10+ years of subsurface experience in the energy sector (oil & gas, geothermal, CCS, hydrogen storage, or integrated energy).Strong experience in exploration, appraisal, field development, and reservoir management.Proven track record in reserves growth and production optimisation.Experience leading large technical teams.Proficient in industry software (Petrel, Eclipse/CMG, DecisionSpace, Geolog, OFM, etc.).Master’s degree in a relevant technical field (or equivalent professional certifications).
Key Responsibilities1. Strategy & Technical LeadershipDevelop and implement the company’s subsurface strategy.Ensure high-quality technical evaluations of prospects, reservoirs, and field development plans.Set and control subsurface technical standards and workflows.2. Resource & Reserves ManagementLead resources from early concepts through to reserves.Oversee reserves estimation and classification (PRMS or equivalent).Approve reserves booking, production forecasts, and depletion plans.3. Geology & GeophysicsOversee seismic acquisition, processing, and interpretation.Ensure accurate geological and reservoir models.Support exploration, appraisal, and development drilling decisions.4. Reservoir Engineering & Field DevelopmentLead reservoir studies, simulation, and production forecasting.Approve Field Development Plans (FDPs), well locations, and recovery strategies.Ensure continuous monitoring and optimization of reservoirs.5.
