data.title: Associate Director, WHOOP Labs Doha
data.jd_text:
At WHOOP, we're on a mission to unlock human performance and healthspan. WHOOP empowers its members to perform at a higher level by providing a deep understanding of the interactions between their unique physiology and chosen behaviors.

We’re looking for a talented leader to help open WHOOP Labs Doha, our first international product research facility. The Doha lab will partner with the WHOOP Labs Boston team to conduct mixed-methods research to support physiology, user experience, and consumer studies. To serve the company’s diverse testing and validation needs, WHOOP Labs completes tens of thousands of data collections a year and creates best in class experience for our participants. WHOOP Labs Doha will set the bar for product innovation research in the GCC, setting strategy for participant experience and contributing to advancing WHOOP technology.

We’re looking for someone with a proven track record collaborating internationally and leading a cross-functional team, who is excited to work with our members and isn’t afraid to roll up their sleeves and get sweaty. This position is in-person in Doha, Qatar with potential travel to Boston, MA 1-2 times per year.

This role is based in the Doha, Qatar. We will only consider candidates who currently live in Doha, Qatar.

Responsibilities

Own the development and execution of the WHOOP Labs Doha opening strategy, including team hiring, participant compensation structure, and setting operating hoursChampion research integrity, including ensuring that data collection tools and team practices align with local regulations for ethical human subjects researchDefine and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure the effectiveness of program initiatives and track progress towards goalsEnsure clear communication between the Boston and Doha labs, developing ways of working that support multi-facility research execution Represent Doha culture and unique participant experience factors in broader strategic conversations with the rest of the WHOOP human subjects research teamBuild the WHOOP Labs Doha brand and represent WHOOP in the GCC region Reports to the Director of Human Subjects Research

Qualifications

Bachelor’s Degree in a field related to human subjects research such as psychology, neuroscience, exercise physiology, or consumer science5+ years of full-time professional experience in a related area2+ years of success leading a diversely skilled teamExperience working on a distributed international team preferredDeep knowledge of local culture and ability to thoughtfully communicate localization considerations for research at WHOOP Labs DohaUnderstanding of best practices regarding human subjects research, including how to obtain IRB approval for new research projects; research ethics, data privacy, and consent Track record of successful project management, including resource allocation, timeline management, and risk mitigationExcellent verbal and written communication skills. Strong organizational skills, ability to plan ahead while still being flexible and able to work around challengesStrong networking and collaboration skills, with experience in building partnerships and engaging with external stakeholdersAbility to present findings to stakeholders and effectively communicate with company leadersExcitement to work in a high-energy, fast-paced startup environmentUnderstanding of branding and marketing as it applies to research in industry spacesFluency in verbal and written English, WHOOP’s working language

This role is based in the Doha, Qatar. We will only consider candidates who currently live in Doha, Qatar.

Interested in the role, but don’t meet every qualification? We encourage you to still apply! At WHOOP, we believe there is much more to a candidate than what is written on paper, and we value character as much as experience. As we continue to build a diverse and inclusive environment, we encourage anyone who is interested in this role to apply.

WHOOP is an Equal Opportunity Employer and participates in E-verify to determine employment eligibility. It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

data.title: Head of Cargo Business Insights
data.jd_text: Job title Head of Cargo Business Insights Ref # 232384 Location Qatar - Doha Job family Cargo & Airport Operations Closing date: 08-Apr-2026 The air cargo industry is entering an accelerated phase of change and transformation. From digitalizing the end-to-end value chain, to fortifying a true e-commerce experience, to the launch of digital freight marketplaces, and much more. As the world’s largest global air cargo carrier, Qatar Airways is leading this change. To support our vision, we are looking for talents – the bold, the imaginative and the change-maker. All of who will support Qatar Airways Cargo’s position as a true leader in the air freight industry. If you want to make an impact, to grow your experience and to challenge your comfort zone, then join us at Qatar Airways Cargo and be part of our passionate team. As the business expands, we are pleased to announce an incredibly exciting opportunity to be part of Qatar Airways Cargo’s Business Insights team in Doha as the Head of Cargo Business insights About The Role In this role, you will be responsible for leading the development, planning and implementation of data strategy by providing analytics and governance to maximize revenue and profit generation across the regions and ensure achievement of budget targets. You will manage a central team which provides insightful analytics to key stakeholders across all regions covering commercial, operations and revenue management to ensure data driven decisions are made to achieve business objectives. As part of your role, your additional responsibilities will include the following: Strategic Define and execute the Cargo Business Insights strategy aligned with the overall Cargo and Corporate Planning strategy Act as the senior commercial insights partner to Cargo leadership, providing fact based recommendations on revenue performance, market dynamics, and strategic initiatives Business Insights Oversee the analysis of cargo revenue, yield, volume, capacity, load factors, customer segments, and product performance at network, regional, POS, and route levels Lead competitive and market intelligence, including market share, pricing dynamics, trade flows, macro economic and geo political impacts on air cargo Ensure insights are clearly communicated to senior management through executive ready dashboards, reports, and presentations Provide insights to Chief Cargo Officer and VP Corporate Planning and BI on daily basis on commercial performance of the airline highlighting challenges and opportunities by looking at both flown and forward data Forecasting, Budgeting and Planning Own and govern the cargo revenue forecasting framework, ensuring accuracy, transparency, and timely updates Lead the analytical input into the annual budget and long term planning cycles, including scenario analysis and risk/opportunity identification Partner closely with Revenue Management, Network Planning, Sales, and Finance to align assumptions and outcomes Stakeholder Management & Communication Translate complex analysis into clear, concise commercial narratives tailored to different audiences Lead executive forums, commercial reviews, and strategic decision making processes with robust insights Team Leadership & Capability Building Lead, develop, and inspire a global team of Business Insights managers, analysts, and specialists Be part of an extraordinary story Your skills. Your imagination. Your ambition. Here, there are no boundaries to your potential and the impact you can make. You’ll find infinite opportunities to grow and work on the biggest, most rewarding challenges that will build your skills and experience. You have the chance to be a part of our future, and build the life you want while being part of an international community. Our best is here and still to come. To us, impossible is only a challenge. Join us as we dare to achieve what’s never been done before. Together, everything is possible Qualifications About you Bachelor’s degree with minimum of 10 years of job-related experience, preferably within the cargo airline or freight forwarded environment Strong understanding of airline cargo economics, commercial KPIs, how they are linked to each other and how they affect the performance of the airline Ability to design and interpret complex analytical models, using tools such as Python, R, SQL, and BI platforms (Power BI, Tableau, Qlik). This includes forecasting, demand modeling, pricing elasticity analysis, and network performance modeling. Proficiency in analyzing global cargo trends, trade lane performance, macroeconomic indicators, and competitor capacity trends using tools such as Seabury, WorldACD, and IATA data—translating these into predictive models and actionable insights for strategic decisions. High level knowledge of cargo operations & supply chain processes Strong stakeholder influence (C suite & Commercial leadership teams) Excellent spoken and written English skills Ability to perform effectively in high pre

and partnerships to enhance impact across women-focused fields Identify emerging trends, opportunities, and research priorities to position the organization at the forefront of its sector data.title: Vice President, Capital Markets Origination
data.jd_text: MENA | Asia | Latin America Cebron Capital is a capital markets firm focused on originating and structuring complex cross-border transactions across emerging and developed markets. We advise corporations, developers, and institutions on accessing private credit and equity capital for growth, acquisitions, and large-scale projects. We operate across energy, technology, natural resources, infrastructure, and industrials. Position Overview The Vice President, Capital Markets Origination is responsible for sourcing, developing, and securing mandates from companies and project sponsors seeking capital solutions. This role is exclusively focused on origination — identifying and engaging qualified issuers and borrowers globally. It does not involve investor solicitation or securities placement activities. This is a performance-driven role designed for senior professionals who maintain established relationships capable of generating actionable transaction flow. Key Responsibilities Originate proprietary opportunities with corporates seeking growth or acquisition capital, project sponsors in infrastructure, energy, and industrial sectors, family-owned businesses and conglomerates, and government-linked or quasi-sovereign entities. Develop and maintain senior-level relationships with CEOs, CFOs, founders, and sponsors. Identify transactions requiring private credit financing, structured debt solutions, minority or majority equity capital, and hybrid capital structures. Evaluate opportunities at a high level — including capital requirements, transaction feasibility, and strategic alignment — and collaborate with internal teams on structuring and execution strategy. Maintain a disciplined and active pipeline of mandates. Candidate Profile 6–15+ years of experience in investment banking, private credit or direct lending, corporate finance or M&A, project finance, or principal investing and advisory. Demonstrated ability to originate transactions independently with a strong existing network in one or more of the following regions: GCC / Middle East, Africa, Southeast Asia, or Latin America. Deep understanding of capital structures, debt and equity markets, and cross-border transactions. Highly commercial, relationship-driven, and self-directed. Compensation Structure This role is structured for senior professionals seeking direct economic participation in transactions rather than fixed salary. Commission tied to mandates successfully originated and transactions that proceed to closing Profit participation in transaction-level economics generated from originated deals Alignment with long-term value creation and repeat mandates Compensation reflects origination contribution and advisory value.

data.title: Client Solutions Middle East - Vice President
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text: iCapital’s Client Solutions EMEA team is seeking a Vice President to support and accelerate our international growth across the GCC markets. This individual will play a key commercial and strategic role in expanding iCapital’s footprint with wealth managers, private banks, independent financial advisors, and family offices. As a member of the regional team, you will help shape the business development strategy, deepen existing client relationships, and drive new distribution partnerships. The role offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of private markets, wealth management, and financial technology in a fast‑growing, global organization. This position is Abu Dhabi‑based. Responsibilities Support the Client Solutions EMEA team in executing the business development strategy for iCapital, covering Wealth Managers in the GCC region. Develop and manage a robust and sustainable pipeline of prospective Wealth Manager clients. Execute Wealth Manager onboarding process from start to finish, working closely with related teams. Nurture strong relationships with clients and prospects. Educate clients on Private Markets/Alternatives and articulate iCapital’s solutions and capabilities to clients and partners. Provide constant feedback from the field on competitive landscape, investors’ appetite, trends and share with colleagues globally. Build strong internal relationships within iCapital and work closely with other teams (Research & Fund Management, Product Development, Implementation Specialist group, Client Success, Marketing, Legal & Compliance, Technology & Operations, etc.) to achieve the best client outcomes and successful long-term client relationships. Serve as the relationship manager and deliver an exceptional client experience for our Wealth Manager clients (i.e. banks, wealth managers, multi-family office and independent financial advisors). Candidate Profile Solid academic credentials in relevant field. 7-10+ years of solid work experience in the Asset or Wealth Management industry, with a demonstrated track record in winning and interacting with clients. Experience as business development professional or similar role in the asset management industry is a must. Strong understanding of the wealth management landscape across the GCC is a must, if possible with an established network across private banks, wealth managers, independent financial advisors, and family offices. In-depth experience across Private Markets asset classes, combined with a strong grasp of the Wealth Manager distribution model, is strongly preferred. Strong attention to detail. Excellent presentation and verbal/written communication skills, with a professional and positive attitude. Highly collaborative, and able to think strategically about business opportunities and client management. Language skills: Native in English and Arabic is a plus but not a must Ability to work independently with a strong appetite to work in a fast-growing, collaborative environment. We believe the best ideas and innovation happen when we are together. Employees in this role will work in the office four days, with the flexibility to work remotely one day (Friday). Benefits iCapital offers a comprehensive benefits package that includes a total compensation program consisting of competitive salary, annual performance bonus, and equity for all full-time employees; For additional information on iCapital please visit https://www.icapital.com/about-us X (former Twitter): @icapitalnetwork | LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/icapital-network-inc

data.title: Vice President Pharmacy Automation META
data.jd_text: Job Description Summary We are the makers of possible BD is one of the largest global medical technology companies in the world. Advancing the world of health™ is our Purpose, and it’s no small feat. It takes the imagination and passion of all of us—from design and engineering to the manufacturing and marketing of our billions of MedTech products per year—to look at the impossible and find transformative solutions that turn dreams into possibilities. Why join us? A career at BD means learning and working alongside inspirational leaders and colleagues who are equally passionate and committed to fostering an inclusive, growth-centered, and rewarding culture. You will have the opportunity to help shape the trajectory of BD while leaving a legacy at the same time. To find purpose in the possibilities, we need people who can see the bigger picture, who understand the human story that underpins everything we do. We welcome people with the imagination and drive to help us reinvent the future of health. At BD, you’ll discover a culture in which you can learn, grow and thrive. And find satisfaction in doing your part to make the world a better place. Become a maker of possible with us! Our vision for Pharmacy Automation at BD Integrating automation into your pharmacy improves medication management, reduces the risk of errors, and allows staff to focus on patients and offer them quality personalized care. At BD, we understand that pharmacies have unique needs when it comes to medication preparation and distribution. That's why we offer a large range of solutions, including: Blister Packagers, Pouch Packagers, Will Call Solutions, and Controlled Substances Management Solutions, adapted to each production or prescription volume, ensuring that we can meet the needs of Retail Pharmacies, Outpatient, Long-Term Care, Closed-Door, and Central Fill Pharmacies. Overview Job Description The Vice President Pharmacy Automation META (“VP”) is a supervisory role reporting directly to the President META with close alignment to the VP/GM Pharmacy Automation Platform. In this role, you will lead, develop and implement Marketing, Sales, and Support strategies for the Pharmacy Automation Business Unit for the META market. As a member of the META Leadership Team and the Global Pharmacy Automation Leadership Team, you will be the main responsible for setting and driving the three-year strategic, operational and financial plan for Pharmacy Automation, setting high-reaching financial targets and achieving profitable growth, above market growth, conducting new market / opportunity analysis and building and engaging a highly effective team. Key Responsibilities Develop and maintain an understanding of the META market including future trends and the competitive landscape. Develop a strategic understanding / relationships with key customers. Effectively engage network of key influencers to drive strategic health care policy directions Responsible for driving the annual Pharmacy Automation META operating plan including meeting sales, gross profit and key financial metrics. Collaborate with Global Platform Leaders and Country General Managers to define new opportunities for entry into BD’s product development funnel and ensure commercial excellence for new product launches. Work closely with peer regional VPs to support country organizations to implement strategic priorities as defined under the regional Commercial Excellence program. Drive meticulous rigor in sales excellence and adherence to SFDC. Partners with leaders across platforms, regions, R&D, manufacturing, quality, regulatory, and finance to understand, develop and implement the strategic agenda through operational planning, process development and implementation of key projects. Supervise regional product pricing corridors, guidelines and policies for products sold in the META markets, in close alignment with Country Business Leaders to ensure regional pricing consistency and local competitiveness. Develop and implement the interpersonal capability plan in support of the strategy, crafting a highly customer centric organization demonstrated via personal leadership. Responsible for strengthening associate and leadership engagement in the Pharmacy Automation META organization. Identify acquisition targets in the region that can potentially have an immediate, transparent impact on BD's profit margin and growth rate in the region. Experience Meaningful experience in Sales and Marketing, including proven P&L management experience, preferably in the medical devices' market. Proficiency in marketing, selling and new business development skills. Knowledge And Skills Preferred Knowledge and Experience Previous working experience with general management either in global, regional or country business leadership role Consistent track record of business / commercial success in a health care environment Proven knowledge of META healthcare system, environment and its key partners Experience in mark

data.title: Senior Vice President POM Product Manager Middle East
data.jd_text: Job Description At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day, our teams harness cutting-edge AI and breakthrough technologies to collaborate with clients, driving transformative solutions that redefine industries and uplift communities worldwide. Recognized as a top destination for innovators, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we power the future of finance – and this is what is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary. We’re seeking a future team member for the role of Product Manager to join our Custody team. This role is located in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia In this role, you’ll make an impact in the following ways: Product Strategy & Ownership Own the end‑to‑end product roadmap for the middle east, aligned to firm‑wide platform and regional growth priorities Define product vision, scope, and success metrics for MENA custody capabilities, including onboarding, settlement, safekeeping, corporate actions, and reporting Market & Regulatory Engagement Act as the primary product interface with local market infrastructure and regulators Translate local regulatory, membership, and technical requirements into clear product and delivery plans Partner with Legal, Compliance, and Risk to ensure ongoing regulatory adherence and change management Delivery & Execution Work with cross‑platform engineering and operations teams to translate requirements into prioritized backlogs and delivery milestones Support quarterly planning with clear capacity, dependency, and risk articulation Stakeholder & Client Focus Collaborate with internal stakeholders across the Enterprise including Commercial teams to align client demand to capabilities Incorporate client and market feedback into product enhancements and roadmap evolution Clearly communicate product status, risks, and dependencies to senior stakeholders To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following: Product management experience in custody, securities services, or post‑trade domains Direct experience with MENA capital markets, custody models, or market infrastructure Strong understanding of custody operating models, settlement flows, asset servicing, and market connectivity Proven experience working with regulators, or market utilities Demonstrated ability to lead complex cross‑functional delivery in regulated environments At BNY, our culture speaks for itself, check out the latest BNY news at: BNY Newsroom BNY LinkedIn Here’s a Few Of Our Recent Awards America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2025 World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune 2025 “Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2025 Our Benefits And Rewards BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay-for-performance philosophy. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter. BNY is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer - Underrepresented racial and ethnic groups/Females/Individuals with Disabilities/Protected Veterans.

data.title: Chief Operating Officer (COO) - Saudi Nationals
data.jd_text: Role Overview The Chief Operating Officer will serve as the second-in-command, overseeing all day-to-day operational aspects of the company. As a newly licensed EMI under SAMA, the COO will ensure scalable, compliant, and efficient operations for payment acquiring, digital wallet services, and money transfers targeted at SMEs. The role focuses on translating strategy into execution, driving operational excellence, ensuring regulatory adherence, managing risk, and supporting rapid growth in the Saudi fintech ecosystem. Key Responsibilities ▪ Develop and implement operational strategies aligned with the company’s business objectives, growth targets, and SAMA regulatory framework for EMIs. ▪ Oversee core operations including payment processing, transaction acquiring, digital wallet onboarding, fund transfers, merchant services, customer support, and back-office functions. ▪ Ensure full compliance with SAMA regulations, including anti-money laundering (AML), counter-terrorism financing (CTF), data protection, cybersecurity, customer due diligence (CDD), and ongoing reporting requirements. ▪ Lead cross-functional teams (operations, compliance, risk, technology, customer success) to optimize processes, reduce costs, and enhance service reliability for SME clients. ▪ Build and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs), operational dashboards, SLAs, and reporting mechanisms for transaction success rates, uptime, fraud prevention, and customer satisfaction. ▪ Manage vendor and partner relationships (e.g., payment schemes, banks, processors, technology providers) to ensure seamless integration and performance. ▪ Drive continuous process improvement, automation, and technology adoption (e.g., AI for fraud detection, scalable cloud infrastructure) to support business scaling. ▪ Oversee risk management, operational resilience, business continuity planning, and incident response in line with SAMA guidelines. ▪ Collaborate with the CEO and leadership team on budgeting, resource allocation, forecasting, and strategic planning. ▪ Foster a high-performance culture focused on compliance, innovation, and customer- centric operations. Requirements ▪ Bachelor’s degree (Master’s/MBA preferred) in Business Administration, Finance, Operations Management, or related field. ▪ 10+ years of progressive leadership experience in fintech, payments, digital banking; prior EMI, PI, PSP, or payment acquiring experience strongly preferred. ▪ Proven track record as a COO or senior operations leader in a regulated financial environment. ▪ Deep understanding of payment systems, digital wallets, merchant acquiring, transaction processing, and fintech regulations in Saudi Arabia. ▪ Strong knowledge of SAMA’s EMI/Payment Services regulations, AML/CTF, and cybersecurity standards. ▪ Excellent leadership, strategic thinking, and change management skills. ▪ Experience scaling operations in a high-growth fintech startup/scale-up environment. ▪ Fluency in English and Arabic (spoken and written). ▪ Saudi nationals with strong local market knowledge.

data.title: Senior Manager Advanced Analytics
data.jd_text: Head of Advanced Analytics will build and lead a centralized analytics capability that unlocks value from the company’s diverse data ecosystem across markets and brands. The successful candidate will build and lead a team of data scientists, analysts, and data engineers, and partner closely with leaders across marketing, store operations, supply chain, finance, HR, Legal and digital commerce to drive measurable impact across the business. This role combines advanced analytics leadership, AI transformation, stakeholder influence, and team development to drive measurable commercial and operational outcomes across Americana’s restaurant portfolio. The role is expected to establish an enterprise level data architecture integrating data across multiple reporting systems, including customer data, restaurant operations, last mile delivery platforms, financial performance, and employee data for advanced analytics use cases and data backed decision making. Key Responsibilities: Build and Lead the Advanced Analytics Function Establish and lead Americana’s Advanced Analytics Center of Excellence. Define the analytics vision, roadmap, and operating model. Recruit, develop, and lead a high-performing team including: Data Scientists Advanced Analysts Data Engineers Visualization specialists Implement best practices in AI transformation, machine learning, and data-driven decision making 2. Develop analytical models to drive business efficiency and transformation Create advanced analytics models to deliver high-impact projects across multiple domains, including: Customer & Growth Analytics (customer segmentation, cohort analysis, churn, frequency, LTV, acquisition effectiveness) Marketing Analytics & ROI Measurement (campaign effectiveness, attribution, media ROI, performance tracking) Personalization and Recommendation Engines (menu layout and customization, customer journey personalization, CRM personalization etc.) Sales Forecasting & Commercial Planning (demand forecasting, store-level planning, budgeting and outlook support) Pricing & Menu Analytics (price elasticity, menu optimization, mix improvement, margin diagnostics, basket analysis) Promotions & Campaign Analytics (promo affinity modelling, uplift measurement, cannibalization analysis, offer effectiveness) Labor, home delivery & transport optimization and scheduling models (delivery time prediction, logistics efficiency, labor transport scheduling, labor productivity) Store & Operations Analytics (store performance diagnostics, productivity, cost optimization, efficiency levers) Workforce Analytics (employee performance analytics, attrition models) Ensure analytics outputs are decision-oriented , commercially relevant, and actionable. 2. Integrate Data Across Enterprise Oversee the creation of robust, scalable, and high-quality data foundations to enable analytics. Develop frameworks to integrate data across multiple systems – customer data platform, restaurant POS and operating systems, delivery logistics platforms, enterprise financial and sales reporting systems, HR and employee systems Own data sanity, metric definitions, and consistency across systems Ensure alignment and synchronization between source systems, data layers, and analytical outputs Manage the end-to-end customer data stack , ensuring usability for analytics and business teams 3. Embed Analytics into Decision Making Drive analytics and AI initiatives from problem definition to impact delivery: Partner with business stakeholders to identify high-value analytics and AI use cases Lead experimentation frameworks (A/B testing) . Promote a data-driven culture across markets and functions Lead project scoping, prioritization, and delivery governance Ensure models, insights, and tools are explainable, trusted, and adopted Track and articulate business impact and value realization 4. Establish Analytics Governance & Best Practices Act as a trusted analytics partner to senior stakeholders across Marketing, Commercial, Operations, Finance, and Digital: Define standards for Data modeling, Machine learning deployment, Experimentation, Analytics documentation Ensure compliance with data privacy and governance standards Partner with technology, data engineering, and IT teams to ensure sustainable data access and governance 5. Team Leadership & Capability Building Build and lead a high-performing analytics team: Manage, coach, and develop analytics talent across experience levels Foster a culture of curiosity, ownership, and continuous improvement Build future-ready capabilities across advanced analytics, forecasting, and AI Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Engineering in Computer Science / Mathematics/ Statistics / Economics Experience: 10+ years of experience in analytics, data science, or related fields with exposure to stakeholder engagement and project management. Technical Skills: Strong foundation in statistics, predictive modeling, and machine learning . Strong exposure to F&B, QSR, retail

data.title: Chief Technology Officer
data.jd_text: Company Description City School Holdings International (CSHI) is a global education organization managing schools across six countries, including the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Pakistan, Oman, Malaysia, and the Philippines. Established in 1978, CSHI has over 45 years of expertise in education management. The organization serves more than 150,000 students worldwide, offering academic programs from Foundation Stage (FS) to A Levels. CSHI is dedicated to delivering high-quality education and shaping the future of young learners across diverse communities. Role Overview The CTO will drive enterprise-wide technology governance, infrastructure management, and in-house software development. This is a strategic and hands-on leadership role for a technology leader capable of building systems from the ground up while supporting multi-campus and international expansion. Key Responsibilities Technology Strategy & Governance Develop and execute a 3–5 years technology roadmap aligned with growth strategy. Lead digital transformation across academic, finance, HR, and operational functions. Establish IT governance, compliance, and risk management frameworks. Report technology strategy and performance metrics to the Board. ERP & Enterprise Systems (SAP Mandatory) Lead ERP implementation, integration, and optimization. Ensure seamless integration across Finance, HRMS, Admissions, and Academic systems. Drive automation, data accuracy, and reporting standardization. Manage ERP vendors and system performance. In-House Software Development (Critical) Architect and develop a proprietary School Management System (SMS). Build and lead an internal software engineering team. Oversee development of student information, attendance, grading, parent portal, teacher dashboard, and fee modules. Ensure cloud scalability (AWS/Azure), API integrations, and strong security architecture. Implement Agile and DevOps practices. Infrastructure, Cybersecurity & Innovation Ensure secure, high-availability IT infrastructure across campuses. Implement cybersecurity, disaster recovery, and compliance frameworks. Integrate EdTech tools, analytics dashboards, AI, and automation initiatives. Qualifications & Experience Master’s degree in computer science, IT, Software Engineering, or related field. 12–15+ years of progressive IT leadership experience. Minimum 5+ years in a senior technology leadership role. Proven experience in: SAP ERP implementation Large-scale system integration Leading in-house software development teams Education sector experience preferred but not mandatory. Ideal Profile Experience managing multi-site organizations. Strong Board-level communication and stakeholder management skills. Proven ability to build scalable digital platforms from the ground up.

data.title: Chief Technology Officer
data.jd_text: About Atos Group Atos Group is a global leader in digital transformation with c. 67,000 employees and annual revenue of c. €10 billion, operating in 61 countries under two brands — Atos for services and Eviden for products. European number one in cybersecurity, cloud and high performance computing, Atos Group is committed to a secure and decarbonized future and provides tailored AI-powered, end-to-end solutions for all industries. Atos Group is the brand under which Atos SE (Societas Europaea) operates. Atos SE is listed on Euronext Paris. The purpose of Atos Group is to help design the future of the information space. Its expertise and services support the development of knowledge, education and research in a multicultural approach and contribute to the development of scientific and technological excellence. Across the world, the Group enables its customers and employees, and members of societies at large to live, work and develop sustainably, in a safe and secure information space. Role Purpose The CTO for the Middle East & Turkey region is responsible for leading the regional technology strategy and owning the end-to-end presales and solutioning function for all Atos portfolios. The role acts as the primary technology advisor to customers and internal teams, ensuring that all solutions are innovative, competitive, and fully aligned with business growth objectives. Key Responsibilities Technology Strategy & Innovation Define and execute the technology vision for the MET region aligned with global CTO strategy. Drive adoption of Cloud, Data, AI/ML, Cybersecurity, and Digital Platforms. Translate regional market trends into relevant offerings and use cases. Presales Lead the regional presales and solution architecture function across all business lines. Own solution development for major bids, RFPs, and digital transformation programs. Ensure quality, feasibility, and competitiveness of all technical proposals. Strengthen partnerships (Azure, AWS, Google, OEMs) to support winning solutions. Client Engagement Serve as a trusted technology advisor to CIO/CTO-level stakeholders. Support key accounts with solution roadmaps, architecture planning, and technology advisory. Represent Atos in CXO engagements, technology councils, and industry forums. Technical Governance & Delivery Assurance Provide architecture governance and technical oversight for large regional programs. Establish and enforce technology standards, patterns, and best practices. Act as final escalation point for critical solution or design issues. People & Capability Development Lead and develop a high-performing team of architects and presales specialists. Build strong technical capability in Cloud, Data, Cyber, AI/ML, and industry domains. Strengthen certification frameworks and engineering excellence in the region. Required Experience 15+ years in senior technology leadership roles (CTO / Chief Architect / Technical Director). Deep expertise in Cloud, Data, Cybersecurity, AI/ML, and modern engineering practices. Strong experience running presales and large-scale solution architecture functions. Proven engagement with senior stakeholders across Public Sector, BFSI, Energy, and Telecom. Strong commercial acumen and ability to influence at C-level. Choose your future. Choose Atos.

data.title: Vice President Customer Service
data.jd_text: 🚀 We’re Hiring: VP – Customer Operations (Loyalty) 📍 Dubai, UAE We are partnering with a leading organization to appoint a Vice President – Customer Operations (Loyalty) , a strategic leadership role responsible for driving end-to-end customer operations excellence across a rapidly growing loyalty ecosystem. This is an exciting opportunity for a seasoned professional to lead large-scale operations, enhance customer experience, and build scalable, high-performing service models. 🔹 Key Responsibilities Own and drive the end-to-end customer operations strategy across all physical and digital touchpoints Lead service delivery models , ensuring best-in-class customer experience and operational excellence Oversee loyalty systems operations (LMS, CRM, card linking, receipt scanning, etc.) ensuring performance, stability, and security Manage partner and merchant operations , including onboarding, integration, and ongoing support Drive continuous improvement across customer journeys (onboarding, earning, redemption, complaints handling) Establish and govern SLAs, KPIs, and operational frameworks (NPS, resolution time, platform stability, etc.) Act as a senior escalation point for operational risks and service issues Lead vendor management, outsourcing models, and cost optimization strategies Build and develop high-performing teams with a strong customer-first culture 🔹 Key Requirements Minimum 10+ years of experience in customer operations, service delivery, or operations leadership At least 3+ years in a managerial role Strong background in loyalty programs, financial services, payments, or large consumer ecosystems Proven experience managing complex partner/vendor ecosystems Strong understanding of SLAs, KPIs, operational governance, and compliance frameworks Analytical mindset with experience leveraging VOC and data-driven insights Excellent stakeholder management and executive communication skills Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Operations, or related field ( Postgraduate preferred ) 🔹 Why This Role? This position offers the opportunity to: ✔ Lead operations at scale within a high-growth environment ✔ Drive innovation in customer experience and loyalty platforms ✔ Work closely with senior leadership and cross-functional teams ✔ Shape the future of a dynamic and evolving customer ecosystem 📩 Interested candidates or referrals are welcome to connect or apply directly.

data.title: Vice President of Engineering (EPC)
data.jd_text: Provide overall leadership, governance, and strategic direction for EPC Engineering to ensure safe, compliant, high-quality, and cost-effective engineering delivery across all EPC projects. Accountable for engineering strategy, technical assurance, resource planning, engineering productivity, design integrity, and effective integration with Procurement, Construction, Commissioning, and Project Management—supporting order intake, project execution excellence, and long-term capability development. Key Responsibilities Policies, processes & procedures: Develops and oversees the implementation of department policies, procedures and controls covering all areas of finance functional activity so that all relevant procedural/legislative requirements are fulfilled while delivering a quality, cost-effective service to customers. Maintain compliance with all company policies, procedures, and codes of conduct, including strict adherence to the Zero Tolerance to Alcohol policy at all times. Budget/Financial Prepares and recommends the department budget by preparing analysis and data related to specific elements as directed. Monitors the financial performance of the section against budgets so that areas of unsatisfactory performance are identified and rectified promptly and potential performance improvement opportunities are capitalised upon. Day-to-day operations: Define and execute the EPC engineering strategy aligned to company growth plans, target markets, and project portfolio (topside, hull & marine, conversions, modules, offshore EPC). Establish the engineering operating model (roles, governance, stage gates, review boards, interface management) to ensure predictable project delivery. Sponsor engineering transformation initiatives (digital engineering, standardization, modularization, work packaging, automation). Develop, monitor and ensure compliance to all systems and processes required for execution of all engineering activities in three core departments, each managed by a dedicated Engineering Director: Topside (incl HVAC and architectural) Hull & Marine Engineering Production Engineering Responsibility for the review and approval of resource recruitment within each department and allocation of personnel to projects Responsible for ensuring that each department provides sufficient resources to support Tender efforts. Develop and maintain standardize job titles and salary structure across all engineering departments Develop and maintain initiatives for the constant improvement and training of personnel Responsible for ensuring that the engineering effort on projects are delivered in accordance with company and project requirements, on-time and within budget. Ensure Technical Assurance and Compliance of all Engineering activities. Liaise with Project Managers / Client / IVB & Class Societies as necessary for discussion on project resolution issues. Responsibility for employee recruitment, training, performance evaluation, retention and termination activities. Develop and manage strategic engineering delivery centres based on project requirements, including high-value engineering centres in Mumbai and co-location with Engineering partners / subcontractors in their offices Develop mobility plans and process in conjunction with the people departments to standardize remote assignments. Safety, Quality, Environment & Information Security Rules: Ensuring suitable resources are available to achieve satisfactory OHS compliance of all work within their responsibility Ensuring that there is a documented Risk Management process for all work within their responsibility Driving continuous Safety improvement through setting, monitoring and reporting on safety performance against objectives within their department Ensuring identified corrective actions assigned to their department are closed out by agreed due dates Demonstrating desired behaviours and leading, encouraging and communicating on safety issues when interacting with the workforce Meeting all responsibilities as outlined in the DDW OHSMS Participating in any training and safety initiatives implemented by the Company Complying with all Safety directives, and work instructions Immediately reporting any incident or potential hazard to their supervisor or Safety Dept STOP the task if you feel yourself or others may be harmed. Device and implement controls to ensure no violation of ISR regulations in order to protect the company information / intellectual properties Leadership and People Management: Supervises the activities and work of subordinates by providing formal and informal feedback to ensure that all work within a specific area is carried out in an efficient manner and in accordance with set individual targets. Change Management Support the management of change through continuous improvement of departmental systems, processes and practices taking into account ‘international best practice’, changes in international standards and changes in the business environmen

data.title: Vice President of Product Engineering, 2 Hour Learning (Remote) - $400,000/year USD
data.jd_text: 2 Hour Learning seeks a technical product leader with the expertise to serve as the architect of our AI-powered learning ecosystem. This position is designed for someone who combines the technical depth of a CTO with the product acumen to drive transformational outcomes, applying both to unlock student potential at scale never before achieved. Where most organizations treat AI as a supplemental layer on top of conventional education systems, we have taken a fundamentally different approach: reconstructing the school experience from its foundation using AI and learning science to eliminate the limitations inherent in traditional classroom structures. Our results speak for themselves — students achieve multiple 4s and 5s on AP exams, demonstrate multi-year academic advancement within a single year, and regularly exceed their own projected outcomes. When AI serves as the foundational operating system for learning, student performance reaches extraordinary levels. This is not a conventional product management position centered on user feedback cycles or iterative feature releases. This is an executive-level technical product ownership role in which you will control the architecture, technical direction, and AI integration across our complete learning platform. Your responsibility will be to navigate the complex technical tradeoffs that keep our products efficient, scalable, and unwaveringly centered on student achievement. You will operate with the strategic mindset of a CTO while executing with the agility of a product entrepreneur, merging engineering discipline with learning science principles to deliver continuous innovation. If you are prepared to lead the technical transformation of education and possess the operational discipline to realize that vision, we invite your application. This opportunity allows you to shape the future of how students learn. What You Will Be Doing Building and maintaining deep technical expertise in education products to inform strategic direction Creating technical specifications that resolve critical technical decisions while prioritizing simplicity, operational efficiency, and sustainable long-term design Deploying generative AI capabilities to automate and optimize engineering workflows, consistent with the organizational goal of minimizing manual effort What You Won’t Be Doing Functioning solely as a people manager; this role demands both business insight and technical expertise Spending time in meetings, committees, or administrative overhead; automated management systems free you to concentrate on delivering tangible business impact through direct contribution Proposing high-level strategy without actionable implementation plans; your guidance must be grounded in product-level understanding Vice President Of Product Engineering Key Responsibilities Serve as the CTO for one or more education products, owning technical competencies and generating business impact through flawless execution and strategic leadership Basic Requirements 7+ years of experience making critical architecture and design decisions on software projects, including the application of architecture design patterns or major open source technologies 4+ years of experience in roles with complete technical and delivery accountability for a revenue-generating product Experience leveraging AI agents to design, implement, and test software products or features About 2 Hour Learning Education is broken, but 2 Hour Learning is proving it doesn’t have to be. They’re tearing down the outdated one-size-fits-all model and replacing it with AI-driven personalized learning that helps kids master academics in just two hours a day. With students consistently ranking in the top 1-2% nationally and the top 20% achieving an astonishing 6.5x growth, they’re proving that smarter learning is possible. At 2 Hour Learning, it’s talent and performance that matter. They offer a dynamic, on-campus and remote-friendly environment where innovators, educators, and AI specialists can be a part of fixing a broken school system. 2 Hour Learning is reprogramming learning for the AI era. Here’s How They’re Fixing It. There is so much to cover for this exciting role, and space here is limited. Hit the Apply button if you found this interesting and want to learn more. We look forward to meeting you! Working with us This is a full-time (40 hours per week), long-term position. The position is immediately available and requires entering into an independent contractor agreement with Crossover as a Contractor of Record. The compensation level for this role is $200 USD/hour, which equates to $400,000 USD/year assuming 40 hours per week and 50 weeks per year. The payment period is weekly. Consult www.crossover.com/help-and-faqs for more details on this topic. Crossover Job Code: LJ-5590-AE-Dubai-VicePresidento.025

data.title: Vice President, Operations APAC-IMEA
data.jd_text: Role Overview The VP – Operations, APAC & IMEA plays a critical leadership role in shaping the operational strategy and driving operational excellence for APAC & IMEA - Rest of World (RoW) markets. This role is accountable for business growth, operational performance (including efficiency gains, waste elimination, best practice implementation, and cost of goods management), customer satisfaction, and team development. The position combines strategic leadership with hands-on operational management to ensure Brink’s remains a benchmark for excellence across diverse markets. Responsibilities Own the P&L for assigned RoW markets, driving revenue growth, profitability, and cost efficiency. Develop and execute long-term operational strategies aligned with Brink’s global transformation agenda. Build strong partnerships with senior leadership, functional teams, and clients to deliver integrated solutions. Lead transformation initiatives including standardization, automation, and process improvement. Page 1 Establish and track operational scorecards, KPIs, and metrics that measure and drive excellence. Champion a culture of continuous improvement and Lean methodologies to optimize performance. Identify and pursue opportunities for service expansion, efficiency, and innovation. Act as a subject matter expert in operational models, route optimization, and service delivery. Build, mentor, and retain high-performing teams with a focus on diversity, inclusion, and development. Foster a culture of accountability, safety, and engagement across all levels of the business. Ensure operational practices deliver exceptional service quality and client satisfaction. Develop and maintain strong relationships with external stakeholders including regulators, clients, and strategic partners. Minimum Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in operations or a related field. 15+ years of progressive operations experience in areas such as customer service, logistics, go to-market, performance & efficiency, network optimization, and transformation. At least 5 years at a senior operational leadership level with proven senior leadership and transformational experience in multi-country operations. Strong financial acumen with demonstrated success in managing P&L, budgeting, and forecasting. Experience with Lean/continuous improvement methodologies. Desired Qualifications Advanced degree (MBA or similar). Experience in secure logistics, transportation, financial services, or related industries. Ability and willingness to travel extensively across RoW markets. Skills & Experience Expertise in operational transformation and process optimization. Ability to manage complex organizations and navigate ambiguity in global markets. Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills with the ability to influence at all levels. Proven ability to build, mentor, and retain high-performing teams. Exposure to unionized work environments is an advantage.

data.title: Vice President, Mid Corporate
data.jd_text: Job Purpose: The Vice President – Micro, Small, & Medium Enterprises is responsible for assisting the Senior Vice President – Mid Corporate, in developing and executing initiatives related to the growth, financing, and management of the Mid Corporate portfolio. The role includes managing the client relationship life cycle, delivering financial solutions, and ensuring alignment with the strategic objectives of the Mid Corporate department. This position supports the SVP in growing the bank’s Mid Corporate market share, strengthening client relationships, and executing strategic projects and transactions. Key Roles and Responsibilities: Support the SVP in setting clear goals and strategies for the Mid Corporate department to achieve growth targets and objectives. Lead and mentor Relationship Managers, guiding them to achieve team objectives while enhancing their professional development. Manage team performance by assigning tasks, conducting regular performance reviews, and providing feedback to optimize performance and development. Coordinate closely with the SVP to implement the bank's overall strategy and ensure all initiatives align with departmental goals. Provide strategic advice and feedback to the SVP, helping refine and adjust plans based on performance, market conditions, and client feedback. Ensure team members are well-equipped through training programs and maintain a high level of professionalism and expertise. Operational Responsibilities Work closely with the SVP to expand the Mid Corporate banking portfolio, focusing on client acquisition, cross-selling, and revenue growth. Assist in developing a structured client relationship management strategy, ensuring top-tier client satisfaction and service delivery. Spearhead project financing initiatives for Mid Corporate, ensuring that all applications comply with internal and regulatory requirements. Manage the portfolio of Mid Corporate clients by analysing their business needs and structuring financial solutions that meet those requirements while ensuring risk management and compliance. Oversee the preparation of credit proposals and financing applications, ensuring they meet the standards set by the bank’s risk management framework. Monitor financial performance of the Mid Corporate portfolio, working closely with the SVP to ensure revenue and growth targets are met while maintaining a controlled risk profile. Support the SVP in developing strategic initiatives aimed at improving business processes, increasing operational efficiency, and enhancing the client experience. Ensure seamless communication and collaboration with internal stakeholders such as risk management, legal, and finance to manage the end-to-end transaction process. Manage all documentation and due diligence processes for financial transactions, ensuring compliance with legal and regulatory frameworks. Proactively identify and report on early warning signs for any underperforming clients or at-risk transactions to the SVP. Represent the department at internal and external meetings, building strong relationships with key clients and stakeholders as a representative of the SVP. Drive cross-selling initiatives by working with other business units within the bank to ensure that Mid Corporate clients have access to the full range of banking products and services.

data.title: COO
data.jd_text: We are a fast-growing decentralized infrastructure provider sitting at the heart of the Web3 evolution. As we transition from a lean engineering-heavy team to a global powerhouse, we need a Chief Operating Officer to build the engine that powers our growth. You won't just be managing—you will be architecting. You will oversee the "Holy Trinity" of back-office excellence: Operations, Finance, and Legal , ensuring our internal systems are as robust and scalable as the decentralized networks we build. Your Core Responsibilities Design and implement the operational frameworks required to transition the company through significant growth phases and support a rapidly expanding global team. Oversee financial planning, budgeting, and Web3-specific treasury management, balancing fiat and digital asset stability. Navigate the complex global regulatory landscape of decentralized infrastructure, managing external counsel to ensure long-term institutional stability. Build a world-class HR function that attracts top-tier Web3 talent while maintaining a high-performance, remote-first culture. Translate the CEO’s vision into actionable operational roadmaps, ensuring key milestones are met across all non-engineering departments. Who You Are You have a proven track record of leading a startup through high-growth periods and maturing the organizational structure. You understand the unique operational and legal hurdles of the decentralized world, from DAO structures to node provider requirements. You are comfortable pivoting from high-level financial strategy to granular discussions on international employment law. You prioritize automated, scalable workflows over manual processes to ensure efficiency as the company grows. Requirements 7+ years of senior leadership experience (COO, VP Ops, or similar) in a high-growth technology environment. Direct experience in the Web3/Crypto industry is highly preferred. Proven success in managing diverse functions across Finance, Legal, and HR. Experience with international entity structuring and global remote hiring. Exceptional communication skills with the ability to lead a distributed, global team.

data.title: Head Strategic Business Development
data.jd_text: Job Location - Riyadh The Head Strategic Corporate Business Development is responsible for identifying, developing, and executing cross-business growth opportunities that create new revenue streams and enhance synergies across the company’s business units. The role focuses on corporate-level strategic initiatives , new business platforms, partnerships, and investments that support long-term growth and diversification. It works closely with executive leadership to drive expansion into new sectors, services, and markets. This is a senior, executive-track position with exposure to board-level decision making. Core Mandate Create new revenue lines across the group. Identify and implement cross-business synergies . Develop new strategic platforms and partnerships. Support executive leadership on corporate growth initiatives . Key Responsibilities 1) Corporate Growth Initiatives Identify new business opportunities aligned with the company’s long-term vision. Develop and launch new revenue platforms across multiple business units. Lead feasibility and commercial assessments of new corporate initiatives. Translate strategic priorities into executable business opportunities. 2) Cross-Business Synergy Development Identify opportunities to create synergies between existing business units. Develop integrated solutions that combine capabilities across divisions. Coordinate with business unit leaders to implement synergy initiatives. Track and report synergy value creation. 3) New Business Line & Platform Development Identify emerging sectors, technologies, and service models. Develop commercial models for new business lines. Lead the creation of new corporate platforms or verticals. Support the launch and early-stage growth of new ventures. 4) Strategic Partnerships & Alliances Identify and engage with global partners, technology providers, and investors. Structure strategic alliances, joint ventures, or co-development models. Support executive-level negotiations and relationship management. Ensure partnerships generate measurable revenue and strategic value. 5) Strategic Investments & M&A Identify acquisition and investment opportunities aligned with corporate growth platforms. Develop business cases, financial models, and valuation analyses. Support due diligence and transaction structuring. Assist executive leadership in investment decisions. 6) Executive-Level Business Development Support CEO and senior leadership in developing strategic client relationships. Identify long-term programs with key government and strategic clients. Position the company as a strategic partner rather than a transactional contractor. 7) Corporate Opportunity Pipeline Build and maintain a pipeline of corporate-level strategic opportunities. Prioritize initiatives based on strategic fit and financial returns. Present opportunities to executive leadership and the board. Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Revenue generated from new corporate business lines. Value of cross-business synergy initiatives implemented. Number and value of new strategic platforms launched. Partnerships or joint ventures executed. ROI/IRR of new corporate initiatives. Contribution of new segments to total company revenue. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Finance, Business, or related field. MBA or equivalent postgraduate degree preferred. Experience 10–18 years of experience in: Corporate business development, Strategic planning and investment roles in Infrastructure, energy, industrial, or EPC sectors Proven experience in: Launching new business lines, Cross-business or corporate initiatives and Strategic partnerships or investments Key Competencies Corporate-level strategic thinking. Cross-business integration and synergy development. New business platform creation. Strong commercial and financial acumen. Executive-level stakeholder management. Investment analysis and business case development. Leadership Attributes Entrepreneurial and growth-oriented mindset. Strong executive presence and credibility. High ownership and accountability. Ability to work across multiple business units. Results-driven with strong execution focus.

data.title: Chief Financial Officer
data.jd_text: Job Summary: The Chief Financial Officer (CFO) will play a central role in shaping the organization’s strategic direction, strengthening its financial foundation, and driving disciplined growth across all business verticals and service lines. The role requires a senior financial leader with deep experience in technology‑centric organizations operating across the US and UAE/GCC markets, with proven capability in international finance, complex commercial structures, and high‑value digital and intellectual property assets. The CFO will oversee global finance, capital strategy, corporate structuring, procurement, compliance, and performance management, while serving as a key advisor to the CEO and Board. Job Responsibilities: Strategic Financial Leadership Define and execute the long-term financial strategy aligned with organizational goals. Provide financial insights and recommendations to the CEO and Board for strategic decision-making. Establish strong financial governance across all global entities. Capital Strategy, Fundraising & External Relations Lead equity/debt fundraising initiatives and optimize capital structure. Manage relationships with investors, banks, and financial institutions. Oversee financial modeling, due diligence, and investor communications. Ensure transparency and credibility with external stakeholders. Acquisition & Divestiture of Intangible Assets Lead evaluation, negotiation, acquisition, and disposal of high-value intangible assets including software, IP, licenses, and technology platforms. Define valuation, capitalization, amortization, and impairment frameworks. Sponsor and oversee financial aspects of technology asset transactions. Financial Planning, Analytics & Performance Management Direct budgeting, forecasting, scenario planning, and long-term financial modeling. Provide performance visibility across SaaS, products, consulting, and managed services. Implement advanced FP&A tools, dashboards, and reporting systems. Global Accounting, Tax, Compliance & Audit Oversee global accounting, statutory reporting, transfer pricing, and tax strategy. Ensure compliance with IFRS/GAAP and local regulations. Maintain strong internal controls and audit readiness. Standardize financial processes across international operations. Treasury, Liquidity & Working Capital Management Manage global cash flow, treasury operations, and currency risk. Develop working capital strategies for long-cycle and project-based revenue models. Implement treasury policies to ensure financial stability and scalability. Commercial Governance & Deal Structuring Provide financial oversight for enterprise contracts and multi-year transformation programs. Guide pricing strategies, commercial models, and project financials. Partner with product and sales teams on scalable revenue models. Corporate Structuring, M&A & Strategic Investments Design and optimize corporate entity structures across regions. Evaluate acquisitions, partnerships, and strategic investments. Lead financial due diligence, integration, and synergy tracking. Support diversification and growth initiatives. Procurement & Supply Chain Leadership Oversee global procurement strategy and vendor governance. Establish sourcing policies, approval frameworks, and cost controls. Manage procurement of technology, infrastructure, licenses, and services. Lead supplier performance management and contract negotiations. Ensure alignment of procurement with working capital and financial objectives. Strengthen supply chain processes supporting multinational operations. Digital Finance, Systems & Automation Drive ERP optimization, automation, and digitization of finance processes. Implement scalable financial systems and analytics platforms. Promote data-driven decision-making across the organization. Required Skills: Strong strategic and commercial financial leadership. Expertise in global finance operations and multi-entity structures. Advanced knowledge of intangible asset valuation and financial treatment. Strong experience in fundraising, investor relations, and capital strategy. Deep understanding of project-based accounting and SaaS / services revenue models. Excellent negotiation, analytical, and decision-making skills. Strong leadership and stakeholder management capability. Knowledge of IFRS / GAAP, taxation, transfer pricing, and compliance. Experience with ERP and financial systems (SAP, Oracle, Dynamics 365, etc.). Required Experience: Mandatory minimum 15+ years of progressive financial leadership experience. Mandatory experience working in both US and UAE/GCC markets, with strong understanding of regulatory, tax, commercial, and financial operating environments in these regions. Proven experience in technology, software, IT services, or digital transformation organizations. Experience managing multi‑country finance operations and complex multi‑entity structures. Demonstrated track record in capital raising, investor relations, M&A, and complex transactions

data.title: Chief Operating Officer
data.jd_text: Our client is a regional logistics and supply chain services provider with operations spanning warehousing, transportation, and last-mile delivery across the UAE and GCC. The organisation is entering a scale-up phase with increased operational complexity and regional demand. The Role The Chief Operating Officer will have full responsibility for day-to-day operations across all logistics verticals. This role will drive operational performance, cost optimisation, and service quality across multi-site operations. A key mandate will be standardising processes, strengthening operational governance, and improving margin control. The COO will oversee large operational teams and ensure alignment between commercial commitments and delivery capability. The role will work closely with the CEO on expansion strategy and capacity planning. Technology enablement and automation will be central to the operational roadmap. Candidate Profile The ideal candidate will have extensive senior operations leadership experience within logistics, supply chain, or transportation environments. Strong exposure to multi-site, high-volume operations is essential. Experience operating in the UAE or GCC markets is highly preferred. The candidate must demonstrate execution discipline, leadership maturity, and strong people management skills. A track record of scaling operations while maintaining service quality is critical. Commercial awareness and cross-functional influence are required.

data.title: Chief of Staff
data.jd_text: Company Description The company offers an all-in-one platform designed to help businesses efficiently manage essential operations such as payments, expenses, travel, and insurance. It provides tools to streamline and automate multiple processes, enabling operational excellence for its clients. Backed by the International Holding Company (IHC), the platform integrates innovation and reliability to deliver smart business solutions. The Chief of Staff will support executive leadership in strategy development, decision-making, and the execution of key initiatives. Responsibilities include managing cross-functional projects, streamlining internal operations, and ensuring alignment across the leadership team. The role also involves acting as a trusted advisor, providing data-driven insights, and representing the executive team in high-level meetings and communications. Qualifications Strong leadership, strategic planning, and decision-making skills Proficiency in project management, process optimization, and operational planning Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate with diverse stakeholders Analytical and problem-solving skills, with a data-driven approach to decision-making Ability to manage multiple priorities and maintain a high degree of organization Experience working in a fast-paced, high-growth environment is a plus Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Management, or a related field; MBA or similar advanced degree preferred Prior experience in a Chief of Staff role or a similar leadership support position is advantageous

data.title: Project Head
data.jd_text: I am partnering with a Dubai-based real estate development company to hire a Head of Projects 🚧 This is a strategic leadership role responsible for end-to-end project delivery across a growing portfolio, from design and engineering through to construction and handover. The role reports directly to the CEO and works closely with the Board, ensuring projects are delivered on time, within scope, and to budget . Key Responsibilities: Lead project strategy and planning , translating business objectives into executable programs. Oversee design management (architectural, structural, MEP, safety, and electrical) and ensure projects are value-engineered and fully coordinated. Manage construction delivery , contractors, procurement, and vendor relationships. Drive commercial and contract management , including tendering, bid evaluation, negotiations, and FIDIC-aligned contracts. Monitor portfolio performance , budgets, and timelines, implementing corrective actions as needed. Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing project management team . Ensure compliance with company standards, regulatory requirements, and quality benchmarks. Provide timely reporting and dashboards to the CEO and Board. Requirements: 10+ years’ experience in real estate project development and management , including leadership of complex projects in the UAE or GCC. Proven experience in construction, engineering, and commercial contract management (FIDIC exposure preferred). PMP certification required . Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills . Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering, Architecture, Construction Management, or related field (Master’s preferred). Please note, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

data.title: Financial Partnership Development Senior Manager
data.jd_text:
About Thunes

Thunes is the Smart Superhighway for money movement around the world. Thunes’ proprietary Direct Global Network allows Members to make payments in real-time in over 130 countries and more than 80 currencies.

Thunes’ Network connects directly to over 7 billion mobile wallets and bank accounts worldwide, via more than 350 different payment methods, such as GCash, M-Pesa, Airtel, MTN, Orange, JazzCash, Easypaisa, AliPay, WeChat Pay and many more.

Members of Thunes’ Direct Global Network include gig economy giants like Uber and Deliveroo, super-apps like Grab and WeChat, MTOs, fintechs, PSPs and banks. Thunes’ Direct Global Network differentiates itself through its worldwide reach, in-house Smart Treasury Management Platform and Fortress Compliance Infrastructure, ensuring Members of the Network receive unrivalled speed, control, visibility, protection and cost efficiencies when making real-time payments globally.

Headquartered in Singapore, Thunes has offices in 12 locations, including Barcelona, Beijing, Dubai, London, Manila, Nairobi, Paris, Riyadh, San Francisco, Sao Paulo and Shanghai. For more information, visit: https://www.thunes.com/

Context of the role

Thunes is looking for a dynamic, high impact leader to drive the growth, health, and innovation of our Direct Global Network across MENA. The Network Development Manager is technically and financially savvy, creative, with strong personal vision, extremely strong domain expertise, and great people skills.

Key Responsibilities

As a key member of the Network team, you will be asked to wear many hats, including developing country and regional go-to-market strategies, cultivating network partners at all levels of the payments and financial services ecosystem, and developing a well-functioning payments network in MENA that drives volume across our global network, including:

Strategic partnership management & execution: Strategically plan, execute, and manage end-to-end relationships with critical banking and network partners (FIs). This includes strategic road map development in the region and managing key accountsCommercial growth & network competitiveness: Continually drive revenue through cross and up-sell opportunities, in close collaboration with Go-To-Market and Account Management teams. Ensure the Thunes network is the most commercially attractive in the market, from transaction cost to FX, and work with FI partners to drive payment flows through joint go-to-market initiatives.Operational excellence & stakeholder coordination: Act as an owner to ensure all aspects of partnerships are working seamlessly. Work with internal and external stakeholders (including Risk, Compliance, Product, Engineering, and Treasury) to ensure the Thunes network is operationally excellent, coordinate on partner strategy, and advance a holistic network strategy.Market knowledge: Understand and clearly articulate the payment-related nuances among the different markets in-scope (e.g. UAE vs Jordan etc.


Professional Experience/Qualifications

Package And Benefits

Great compensation package, including performance‑based commissionMedical insurance for you and your familyLife insuranceMobile allowanceEmployee wellbeing & support programLearning & Development budgetWork‑life balance (flexibility and hybrid working policy)Our team is global: you will work with 65+ different nationalities spread across 5 continents
