data.jd_text: Job Description Client Integration VP About The Role The role of a Client Integration Vice President (VP) involves leading/overseeing client integrations, conversions and strategic projects. The Integration VP is responsible for the management of virtual project teams tasked with project managing client integrations/conversions, services delivery changes and sales support. The VP is responsible for managing a group of Assistant Vice Presidents (AVP), training and development of staff, role out of global best practice and standardization across the global Integration team. The core skills required are having experience in leading teams for successful delivery of projects within a project management framework, demonstrating effective communication skills, having client-facing experience and understanding the financial services business. The VP will be heavily involved in securing new business as well as manage existing client relationships and onboardings, will have a lead role in global or local initiatives interacting with other Citco global offices and technology groups The integration team is the central point of contact for the client both technical and support during onboarding. The role involves dealing with the senior management of all the organizations that interact with Citco Fund Services. Responsibilities Sales Preparation: Participate in marketing and sales pitches; effectively demonstrate and communicate Citco’s workflows, best practices and technologies Request for proposal/information assistance Direct source of information to Business Development Stakeholder in the development of new clients/business AA / SLA negotiation Manage requested Proof of Concepts for sales pitches Project Manage External New client launches/conversions New Business Existing Client (NBEC) launches Onboarding of Ancillary services Coordinate with all relevant teams to ensure internal workflows will meet client needs and they capture any unique sets of requirements. Coordinate with both internal and external stakeholders to ensure the project scope is documented, agreed and completed at planning phases. Major SLA changes Project Manage Internal Actively participate in departmental and company-wide projects. Process Improvements Oversee and manage strategic initiatives within the Client Integration team Identify issues/concerns and recommend company-wide solutions for any/all high risk projects Administrative Reporting Tracking of projects, progress, status and deadlines MIS Reporting Actively manage operational risk – monthly reporting on status reports and key development issues to external clients and executive management team. Ensure strong emphasis on escalation and complaints management within the team and within the department. Ensure fluid execution and strategy for work forecasting and allocation Management In partnership with the Senior Vice Presidents, oversee the resolution of complex client issues. Management of virtual teams Pipeline/Project/Resourcing Management Oversight, Recruitment, Training and Development. Review departmental procedures and policies and help ensure adherence to these procedures Qualifications Degree qualified or related professional qualification Experiences 5+ years of project management experience 3+ years of client services within the asset management industry 3+ years of people and resource management experience 7+ years in the financial services industry Proven interest in the financial markets and hedge fund industry Proven Project Management Experience Proven Business Analysis Experience Proven knowledge of Listed and OTC products Proficient in Microsoft Office applications Ability to handle several projects and different stakeholders simultaneously, and prioritize work based on deadlines A ‘team player’ who can work well within the function to deliver a quality service Strong sense of ownership and accountability of work product Creative and innovative Continuous improvement Desirable Accounting experience (Desirable) Middle Office experience (Desirable) Project Manager Qualifications (Desirable) Relationship Management Experience (Desirable)

data.title: Vice President, Financial Crime Prevention
data.company: General Commercial Gaming Regulatory Authority - GCGRA
data.location: Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text: Job Description As the VP, Financial Crime Prevention in GCGRA, your primary responsibility is to oversee and manage all aspects of financial crime prevention within the gaming industry. You will play a critical role in safeguarding the integrity of the gaming sector by implementing robust anti-money laundering (AML), counter-terrorist financing (CTF), and other financial crime prevention measures. Your expertise in financial crime prevention, regulatory compliance, and risk management will be essential in maintaining a secure and transparent gaming environment. Responsibilities Develop and implement a comprehensive financial crime prevention strategy aligned with the regulatory objectives and priorities. Establish policies, procedures, and controls to prevent money laundering, fraud, and other financial crimes within the gaming industry. Ensure compliance with anti-money laundering (AML) and counter-terrorist financing (CTF) regulations and requirements. Develop and maintain robust AML/CTF programs specific to the gaming industry, including customer due diligence (CDD), transaction monitoring, and suspicious activity reporting (SAR) processes. Implement risk management strategies and controls to mitigate identified risks effectively and conduct risk assessments to identify vulnerabilities. Prepare comprehensive reports, maintain accurate records and documentation on financial crime prevention activities, including risk assessments, compliance findings, and recommendations for improvements. Monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of AML/CTF controls and recommend enhancements as necessary. Stay updated on emerging financial crime trends, typologies, and regulatory developments to proactively address potential threats. Develop and deliver training programs to enhance the awareness and knowledge of financial crime prevention among gaming operators, licensees, and regulatory staff, and promote a strong culture of compliance and ethics within GCGRA and across the industry. Provide guidance and support to gaming operators and licensees regarding their AML/CTF obligations and best practices. Develop guidance to the bylaws – keeping up to date on federal and international laws. Work alongside other functions providing technical expertise and support where required. Have multiple reporting requirements into various national committees and government entities. Qualifications Extensive experience in financial crime prevention, regulatory compliance, or related roles within the gaming industry or a regulatory agency. In-depth knowledge of anti-money laundering (AML) and counter-terrorist financing (CTF) frameworks, regulations, and industry best practices Bachelor's or Master's degree in a relevant field such as finance, law, or criminology. Familiarity with risk assessment methodologies, control frameworks, and financial crime prevention technologies. Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills. Effective leadership and team management abilities. Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills to engage with diverse stakeholders. Professional certifications related to AML/CTF or financial crime prevention (e.g., CAMS, CFCS) are desirable. About Us The General Commercial Gaming Regulatory Authority (GCGRA) is the federal executive agency responsible for regulating and overseeing commercial gaming in the United Arab Emirates. We aim to drive sustainable growth by cultivating world-class commercial gaming operations and implementing efficient regulation, grounded in the principles of integrity, innovation, and responsible practices. Established by Federal Law by Decree and headquartered in Abu Dhabi, the GCGRA is the executive authority that holds exclusive jurisdiction to regulate, license, and supervise all commercial gaming activities and facilities in the UAE.

The role requires strong judgment, executive presence, and the ability to balance strategic advisory with hands on ownership of delivery, escalation management, and internal coordination across product, engineering, and commercial teams. BlackRock Solutions across the Middle East Demonstrate understanding of the Aladdin platform credibly with sophisticated institutional clients, reinforcing BlackRock’s differentiated value proposition Prior experience in consulting, project management, or client relationship management roles within financial technology Demonstrated ability to deliver large, complex programs across multiple stakeh

data.title: Head - Year - Mamoura British Academy (AY 26/27)
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text: Job Description Aldar Academies are currently seeking an outstanding Teacher - Primary with responsibility for Head of Year for Mamoura British Academy in Abu Dhabi commencing August 2026. Mamoura British Academy is a British curriculum, mixed gender school until Year 8 and a girl’s only school from Year 9 onwards. It is located in a state of the art building in the heart of Abu Dhabi. We strive for excellence in all that we do through our high expectations and commitment to ensuring that we provide high quality learning opportunities for our students. Our vision is to be ‘a sustainable and innovative learning community, where we nurture all learners through our bespoke Mamoura British Academy DNA .’ This Mamoura Learner, Teacher and Values DNA is at the heart of the school community. This is an exciting opportunity to join the highly successful Aldar family of schools and to help shape the future of Abu Dhabi’s leading school group. The successful candidates will be closely aligned with our core values and ethos and possess a genuine understanding and appreciation of the values of the region. Responsibilities Qualities and attributes: A supportive and approachable team player who can collaborate effectively with others. A good communicator at all levels. A commitment to developing positive relationships with all members of the school community. A desire to be part of a school that strives for excellence in all that we do. Passion to instil awe and wonder to the learning of young students. Leadership: Contribute to the whole school vision and the strategic direction of the school. Lead team and planning meetings to ensure that the team is well informed, organised and successful. Ensure that the learning opportunities for the children are innovative, purposeful and challenging across all curriculum areas. Promote the pastoral care and well-being of children within the year group by ensuring all systems and procedures are followed. Effectively line manage a team of teachers and teaching assistants to ensure they are performing successfully against the UAE Professional Teacher Standards. Contribute to the school’s Monitoring, Evaluating and Reporting processes and quality assure all aspects of provision within the year group to ensure we are consistently promoting high standards and expectations. Contribute to the school’s Professional Development cycle helping to upskill and share good practice amongst fellow teachers. Actively participate in the school’s bespoke Middle Leader training programme. Teaching and learning: To be able to teach all areas of the Primary English National Curriculum. Organising the classroom, learning resources and creating displays to encourage a positive learning environment and support the development of the Mamoura British Academy USP in this area. Planning, preparing and presenting lessons that cater for the needs of the whole ability range within the class. Providing support and interventions for students with EAL or who have additional learning needs. Motivating pupils with enthusiastic, imaginative presentation. Evidencing children’s work and learning in line with the Mamoura British Academy Marking and Feedback policy. Being reflective of lessons taught and student progress, and adjusting planning, curriculum and continuous provision to reflect this. Meeting with other professionals such as speech and language therapists, occupational therapists and educational psychologists, if required. Ensuring that the Mamoura British Academy Teacher and Learner DNA language and attributes are being embedded throughout the curriculum. Contribute to the development of teaching and learning across the school by sharing best practice, participating in school CPD and T&L initiatives. To be able to demonstrate effective use of technology through innovative and creative lesson delivery and continuous provision. Assessment and data: Taking responsibility for the attainment and progress of a class of children to ensure that they reach above expected expectations. Meeting the requirements for the assessment and recording of pupils' development using Learning Ladders. Using data provided by assessments to influence further planning and interventions. Providing regular feedback to parents and carers on a student’s progress at Parent Teacher Conferences and other meetings. To plan, prepare and contribute to termly Pupil Progress meetings. Behaviour management and duties: To follow the Mamoura British Academy behaviour management policy and promote the TRACK Values to set high expectations. Manage classes effectively, using approaches which are appropriate to pupils’ needs in order to inspire, motivate and challenge pupils. Maintain good relationships with pupils and parents and follow the school’s procedures to manage and challenge any rare occurrences of unwanted behaviour. Be a positive role model and demonstrate consistently the positive attitudes, values and behaviour which are expected of pupils

data.title: Head - Business Continuity Management
data.location: Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text: Embark on a journey where your unique contributions are celebrated, and your professional growth is embraced. At ADCB, we nurture a diverse, inclusive community where every voice is valued. About the business area - Group Risk Management ADCB prioritises a disciplined approach to risk, recognising its fundamental importance to the Bank’s long-term organisational and financial resilience. Group Risk Management oversees the implementation of ADCB's risk objectives, identifying and addressing gaps in the bank's risk infrastructure/framework. Their responsibilities include nurturing the independence of the risk function, establishing provisioning policies, and introducing changes to energise risk awareness among front office personnel and decision-makers. Continuously tuning the risk organisation in line with market best practices, they manage ADCB's portfolio and associated risks to international standards, while establishing a clear risk culture across all areas of operation. In this role, your key responsibilities include : To embed ADCB’s enterprise-wide Business Continuity Management (BCM) Framework to ensure the Bank’s Operational Risks (ORs) are managed within the agreed appetite Implement changes to the business continuity framework, policy and standard operating procedures in order to facilitate effective risk management on an enterprise level Propose and implement the enterprise-wide BCM strategy by initiating business impact analyses in order to determine areas of risk and degree of exposure Formalise the BCM planning with the business to ensure that standards meet ISO 22301 requirements Provide support and guidance to business unit heads, Business Operational Risk Manager and Business Continuity Coordinators in order to assist them with interpretation and implementation of policy and framework requirements Create Bank-wide responses to a crisis incident to ensure they are in line with the action plans and tasking Monitor progress and problem solving to ensure all are followed through to satisfactory conclusion Coordinate implementation teams working in incident and crisis management mode in order to focus their tasks and forward planning next steps Monitor documentation and decision log to ensure they are maintained for future legal or regulatory purposes and ensure Bank-wide preparedness for crisis Ensure all BCM-related training needs are identified and appropriate learning interventions are delivered across the Bank to increase awareness on BCM to employees and to promote a risk management and compliance culture Create BCM metrics and interpret the data in order to measure and monitor performance Formalise and execute Bank-wide business continuity plans followed by periodic tests and audits in order to validate the plans and assist business in ensuring their recovery objectives are met Review all new products, processes, procedures (old and new) and vendors in order to assess the BCM risks associated with each and recommend remedial action Conduct periodic BCM identification and assessment exercises to ensure that all risks facing the Bank have been identified and addressed Deliver management reports on the status of BCM in order to keep internal stakeholders appraised of the status of BCM Manage self and team in line with ADCB’s people management policies, procedures, processes and practices to ensure adherence and to maximise own and employee contribution to business performance Manage the effective achievement of the team’s objectives through setting individual objectives, managing performance, developing the team and providing formal and informal feedback to maximise overall performance, engagement and motivation Recommend and implement improvements to departmental policies, procedures and processes covering all areas of activity so that all relevant procedural requirements are fulfilled while ensuring that ADCB delivers best-in-class services, products and innovation Identify opportunities to contribute to organisational and departmental change initiatives, programmes and projects taking into account best practice and standards in the business environment Demonstrate Our Promise and apply the ADCB Service Standards to deliver the Bank’s required levels of service in all internal and external customer interactions The ideal candidate should have the following experience At least 9 years of experience of which recent 5 years of experience should be in Business Continuity Management, Governance and Control or Operational Risk, Risk Management in a Financial Institution Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics or Business Administration or any other related field Deep experience in BCM, operational resilience, and regulated environments. Ability to operate at both strategic and hands on execution levels. Familiarity with global BCM regulatory standards. Experience managing large scale BCM implementations. Digital awareness is important. Certifications such as CBCI, MBCI, etc., are wel

data.title: Principal Solution Implementation Manager
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text: At Anaplan, we are a team of innovators focused on optimizing business decision-making through our leading AI-infused scenario planning and analysis platform so our customers can outpace their competition and the market. What unites Anaplanners across teams and geographies is our collective commitment to our customers’ success and to our Winning Culture. Our customers rank among the who’s who in the Fortune 50. Coca-Cola, LinkedIn, Adobe, LVMH and Bayer are just a few of the 2,400+ global companies who rely on our best-in-class platform. Our Winning Culture is the engine that drives our teams of innovators. We champion diversity of thought and ideas, we behave like leaders regardless of title, we are committed to achieving ambitious goals, and we love celebrating our wins – big and small. Supported by operating principles of being strategy-led, values-based and disciplined in execution, you’ll be inspired, connected, developed and rewarded here. Everything that makes you unique is welcome; join us and let’s build what’s next - together! Strategic Consulting Meets Expert Delivery: Lead Anaplan's Enterprise Implementations Are you a seasoned consultant with a passion for seeing strategy through to flawless execution? We are looking for a Principal Solution Implementation Manager to lead Anaplan’s most complex and strategic customer engagements across the Middle East. This role is a unique hybrid of high-end consulting and hands-on program leadership. You will be the senior-most delivery advisor for our most important enterprise accounts, acting as the bridge between the customer’s strategic vision and the technical reality of implementation. You won’t just manage projects; you’ll serve as a trusted partner to C-level stakeholders, guiding them through large-scale business transformation. Based in our Abu Dhabi office with hybrid flexibility, you will be instrumental in shaping the success of iconic brands by delivering sophisticated, high-impact planning solutions. Your Impact Act as the lead strategic advisor for a portfolio of enterprise-level Anaplan deployments, translating complex business challenges into scalable solutions. Provide expert counsel to customer executives on governance, change management, project scoping, and long-term value realisation. Build and maintain C-suite relationships, operating as a trusted, non-sales partner focused entirely on the customer’s success. Orchestrate the end-to-end program, steering internal teams, partner consultants, and customer stakeholders toward delivery excellence. Command complex programs, mastering scope, risk, and budget to ensure predictable, high-quality outcomes. Intervene and recover at-risk projects, applying your advisory and leadership skills to navigate challenges and bring deployments back on track. Your Qualifications A background in management consulting, technology consulting, or a client-facing professional services role is essential. Multiple years of experience leading large-scale enterprise program delivery and execution, ideally from within a consultancy or a SaaS professional services organisation. ERP or EPM expertise in at least one of the following: Supply Chain, Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A), or Workforce Planning. Demonstrated ability to operate as a strategic advisor, with outstanding influencing and negotiation skills at the executive level. A track record of successfully delivering complex, multi-year initiatives against tight timelines and budgets. A resilient, adaptable, and proactive mindset, with a passion for solving complex business problems. Fluent communication skills in English and Arabic Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity, Inclusion and Belonging (DEIB) We believe attracting and retaining the best talent and fostering an inclusive culture strengthens our business. DEIB improves our workforce, enhances trust with our partners and customers, and drives business success. Build your career in a place where diversity, equity, inclusion and belonging aren’t just words on paper – this is what drives our innovation, it’s how we connect, and it contributes to what makes us a market leader. We believe in a hiring and working environment where all people are respected and valued, regardless of gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, religion, ethnicity, age, neurodiversity, disability status, citizenship, or any other aspect which makes people unique. We hire you for who you are, and we want you to bring your authentic self to work every day! We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, perform essential job functions, and receive equitable benefits and all privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation. Fraud Recruitment Disclaimer It has come to our attention that fraudulent and fictitious job opportunities are being circulated on the Internet. Prospective candidates are being contacted

data.title: Regional CEO
data.jd_text: Job Description: The Regional CEO will serve as the strategic leader responsible for overseeing the operational, financial, and cultural success of the organization across multiple territories. This executive will be tasked with developing and executing comprehensive strategies that align with the overall vision of the organization, while fostering innovation and sustainable growth. The Regional CEO will leverage deep market insights to identify opportunities for expansion, ensuring competitive positioning within the industry. With a keen focus on building high-performance teams, the incumbent will lead efforts in transformation and change management, driving engagement and accountability at all levels. The ideal candidate will exemplify exemplary leadership qualities, superior communication skills, and a strong track record in stakeholder management, with the ability to cultivate relationships with partners, clients, and employees to enhance organizational reputation and profitability. Job Requirements: A minimum of 15 years of executive leadership experience, with at least 5 years in a CEO or equivalent C-suite role within a multinational organization. Proven track record in driving revenue growth and strategic vision implementation in complex, dynamic market environments. Demonstrated expertise in financial management, including forecasting, budgeting, and financial analysis, with a history of optimizing resource allocation to bolster profitability. Extensive experience in leading transformational initiatives and cultural change within diverse regions, ensuring alignment with corporate values and objectives. Exceptional stakeholder management skills with an ability to influence and engage a wide range of audiences, from internal teams to clients and board members. Advanced understanding of regulatory frameworks and compliance requirements pertinent to operations across various jurisdictions. History of establishing and sustaining high-performance teams, with a clear focus on talent development, employee engagement, and organizational effectiveness. Strategic thinker with a data-driven approach to decision making, adept at leveraging analytics to forecast trends and inform business strategies. Outstanding negotiation and conflict-resolution skills, with a proven ability to drive consensus and collaboration among diverse groups. Job Responsibilities: Develop and implement strategic plans for the region, ensuring alignment with the organization’s overall mission and goals. Oversee all operational activities, creating an organizational structure that maximizes efficiency and effectiveness across all functions. Build and lead a strong executive team, fostering a culture of excellence, accountability, and innovative thinking. Spearhead market analysis initiatives to identify growth opportunities and adjust strategies accordingly to maintain competitive advantage. Manage relationships with key stakeholders, including board members, partners, and regulatory bodies, ensuring transparency and alignment of interests. Establish and monitor performance metrics, driving accountability within the organization and ensuring achievement of financial and operational targets. Lead crisis management efforts, effectively communicating with the organization and external stakeholders during times of uncertainty. Champion sustainable development and corporate social responsibility initiatives that align with the organization’s values and enhance community engagement. Execute talent management strategies that attract, retain, and nurture high-caliber executives and teams throughout the region. Required Skills: Strategic Leadership: Ability to formulate and communicate a clear strategic vision that inspires and motivates others. Financial Acumen: Strong understanding of financial statements, metrics, and principles to drive informed decision-making. Change Management: Skilled in leading organizational change efforts, utilizing frameworks and methodologies that facilitate smooth transitions. Communication Excellence: Outstanding verbal and written communication skills with the ability to tailor message delivery to various audiences. Relationship Building: Capacity to cultivate and maintain effective relationships with diverse stakeholders at all levels. Negotiation Skills: Advanced proficiency in negotiation strategies that lead to mutually beneficial outcomes. Analytical Thinking: Aptitude for leveraging data analytics to assess performance and inform strategic direction. Cultural Competence: Deep understanding of cultural dynamics and their implications on business operations across different regions. Conflict Resolution: Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to resolve disputes and drive collaboration among differing viewpoints.

data.title: Vice President MMS META
data.jd_text: Job Description Summary We are the makers of possible BD is one of the largest global medical technology companies in the world. Advancing the world of health™ is our Purpose, and it’s no small feat. It takes the imagination and passion of all of us—from design and engineering to the manufacturing and marketing of our billions of MedTech products per year—to look at the impossible and find transformative solutions that turn dreams into possibilities. Why join us? A career at BD means learning and working alongside inspirational leaders and colleagues who are equally passionate and committed to fostering an inclusive, growth-centered, and rewarding culture. You will have the opportunity to help shape the trajectory of BD while leaving a legacy at the same time. To find purpose in the possibilities, we need people who can see the bigger picture, who understand the human story that underpins everything we do. We welcome people with the imagination and drive to help us reinvent the future of health. At BD, you’ll discover a culture in which you can learn, grow and thrive. And find satisfaction in doing your part to make the world a better place. Become a maker of possible with us! Our vision for Medication Management Solutions at BD A key and growing part of our business is Medication Management Solutions (MMS), which offers integrated solutions that touch all stages of the patient journey, with the goal of reducing errors and inefficiencies and increasing patient safety. Whether you join from a clinical, technical or innovation background, a career with us will help deliver a new healthcare environment and give a better experience for patients and those who provide them care. Overview Job Description The Vice President MMS META is a supervisory role reporting directly to the President META with close alignment to the MMS WW President. This role is responsible for leading, developing and implementing Marketing, Sales, and Support strategies for the MMS Business Unit for the META market. The VP responsibilities will include setting and driving the three-year strategic, operational and financial plan for MMS, setting high-reaching financial targets and achieving profitable growth, above market growth, conducting new market / opportunity analysis and building and engaging a highly effective team. The VP will be a member of the META Leadership Team and of the Global MMS Leadership Team Key Responsibilities Develop and maintain a solid understanding of the META market including future trends and the competitive landscape. Develop a strategic understanding / relationships with key customers. Effectively engage network of key influencers to drive strategic health care policy directions Responsible for driving the annual MMS META operating plan including meeting sales, gross profit and key financial metrics. Collaborate with Global Platform Leaders and Country General Managers to define new opportunities for entry into BD’s product development funnel and ensure commercial excellence for new product launches. Work closely with peer regional VPs to support country organizations to implement strategic priorities as defined under the regional Commercial Excellence program. Drive rigorous discipline in sales excellence and adherence to SFDC. Partners with leaders across platforms, regions, R&D, manufacturing, quality, regulatory, and finance to understand, develop and implement the strategic agenda through operational planning, process development and implementation of key projects. Supervise regional product pricing corridors, guidelines and policies for products sold in the META markets, in close alignment with Country Business Leaders to ensure regional pricing consistency and local competitiveness. Develop and implement the interpersonal capability plan in support of the strategy, crafting a highly customer centric organization demonstrated via personal leadership. Responsible for strengthening associate and leadership engagement in the MMS META organization. Identify acquisition targets in the region that can potentially have an immediate, transparent impact on BD's profit margin and growth rate in the region. Experiences Meaningful experience in Sales and Marketing, including proven P&L management experience, preferably in the medical devices' market. Proficiency in marketing, selling and new business development skills. data.title: Transition Portfolio Manager, Vice President, State Street Investment Management
data.jd_text:
Who We Are Looking For

We are seeking candidates with 10+ years of experience to join our Transition Management (TM) team in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. In this role, you’ll work within State Street Investment Management’s global TM business, delivering transition and interim exposure management solutions to institutional investors worldwide. You’ll support and manage complex transitions across multiple asset classes, including equities, fixed income, FX, and exchange‑traded derivatives, serving clients domiciled in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia while contributing to the firm’s broader global transition management platform, including interim exposure management and reporting and analytics.

This role can be performed in a hybrid model, requiring at least 4 days of work in the office.

Why this role is important to us

State Street Investment Management (SSIM) is a global asset manager partnering with some of the world’s most sophisticated investors. Backed by State Street’s scale and stability, SSIM offers employees the opportunity to work on complex, high‑impact investment solutions in a collaborative, forward‑looking environment—where expertise, partnership, and long‑term thinking truly matter

This role will enable local delivery of our industry-leading TM service to clients domiciled in the KSA, and enhance our global coverage model by adding TM analysts in the Middle East. Expanding our global presence aligns with our continued commitment to providing the highest level of service to our clients.

What You Will Be Responsible For

As a Transition Portfolio Manager, you will:

Utilize your in-depth understanding of the transition life cycle (pre-trade, implementation, and post-trade aspects) to lead portfolio restructures for KSA-domiciled clients including complex multi-manager, multi-asset class events.Support KSA-domiciled clients and our global transition desk with pre- and post-trade analysis and execution reporting; planning and project management including communication across parties (clients, custodians, record-keepers, investment managers and consultants); portfolio management and order generation; collaboration with trading desk on trade strategy and instructions; and monitoring of daily middle office support functions (trade communications, reconciliations, corporate actions).Cover trade reconciliation process between internal systems for accuracy and completeness as needed prior to sending to middle office and custodians to ensure timely settlement of trades.Represent transition and interim exposure management products as a subject matter expert on multi-asset agency trading, exposure management, and hedging strategies.Apply your expertise on benchmarking and measurement of transition and trade execution performance through Transaction Cost Analysis (TCA)Support sales and business development initiatives and contribute to thought leadership on transition, exposure management and trading topicsAssess internal business operations (trading strategies, performance, technology, data management, marketing, research) and develop solutions to enhance product positioningLead and contribute to strategic projects in partnership with other groups / businesses within State Street as well as external vendorsAssist in evolving and industrializing our suite of trading, hedging and exposure analysis tools – including ongoing enhancement of pre- and post-trade analytics and interim exposure management platform – working with our Exposure Solutions team, reporting and analytics specialists, and external vendors


What We Value

The following skills will help you succeed in this role:

Portfolio management, trading and/or operational experience, preferably across multiple asset classesFamiliarity with Charles River Development, Axioma, or other portfolio and order management systems / toolsClient-facing experience leading complex transitions or portfolio implementations; strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skillsTechnical proficiency, with experience using Bloomberg and other market-data sources for analytical purposesSelf-starter with ability to work and problem solve independently under tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment, all while maintaining a critical focus on detailsStrong project management and organizational skills; pro-active in seeking out the next project to help the team


Education & Preferred Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, mathematics, economics, finance or similar10+ years of experience in a portfolio management, trading, consulting, analytical or operational roleMBA, graduate degree in a quantitative discipline, or CFA designation preferred


Additional Requirements

CMA registration requiredWillingness to travel up to 10% of the timeExperience with TCA, AI, programming languages, modeling in Excel / VBA and Python, data visualization software, and database infrastructures a plus


About State Street

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to

data.title: EMEA Corporate Tax Manager - Vice President
data.jd_text: Job Description Bring your curiosity, leadership, and professionalism to strengthen compliance, support innovation, and help the firm navigate change. As a Vice President in Corporate Tax within the EMEA Corporate Tax team, you will lead day-to-day corporate income tax and VAT affairs for entities in the GCC, supporting compliance, advisory, and strategic initiatives. You will partner closely with the regional tax lead; stakeholders across lines of business, Finance, and Management; and external advisors to assess new business initiatives, reorganizations, and operational changes. You will manage relationships with tax authorities, lead or support audits and enquiries, and anticipate regulatory changes to maintain readiness. You will help define governance expectations, promote consistent controls, and communicate actionable guidance with clarity and influence. This role is based in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, and reports to the tax lead for the MENA region who is based in Dubai. Job Responsibilities Own day-to-day corporate income tax and VAT affairs for legal entities across the region, ensuring timely compliance and high-quality advice. Support assessment and approval of new business initiatives and corporate reorganizations from corporate income tax and VAT perspectives, in partnership with the regional Head of Tax. Manage relationships with tax authorities; lead and support audits, enquiries, and information requests. Engage with lines of business, Finance, and Management to understand operations and developments; identify and advise on key tax issues affecting the region. Coordinate with external advisors and collaborate with the broader EMEA Corporate Tax team to deliver consistent outcomes. Monitor evolving tax rules and obligations in the GCC; translate changes into practical guidance, controls, and implementation plans. Prepare clear analyses, memos, and communications that frame issues, options, and recommendations for stakeholders. Drive process improvements that strengthen compliance, documentation quality, and audit readiness. Maintain governance and risk alignment for corporate taxes and VAT; escalate and remediate issues promptly. Track, prioritize, and deliver multiple projects with accountability and transparency. Required Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills Arabic and English language proficiency. Professional accounting or tax qualification. Demonstrated corporate income tax and VAT experience in the GCC, ideally within financial services. Working knowledge of governance and risk principles applicable to corporate taxes and VAT. Professional proficiency in written and verbal communication. Strong analytical and critical thinking to develop commercially sound solutions. Ability to work independently, manage workload and projects, and meet deadlines. Collaborative mindset and interpersonal skills to build relationships across diverse stakeholders. Proficiency with Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Word. Ability to produce clear documentation and present recommendations to senior stakeholders. Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills In-house or professional services background supporting financial services firms. Familiarity with GCC tax authority practices and systems, including Saudi rules and processes. Experience with cross-border reorganizations and business change approvals. Exposure to ERP or finance systems and data flows that impact tax compliance and reporting. Experience coordinating external advisors and multi-jurisdictional tax projects. Ability to develop process improvements and control enhancements in tax operations. ABOUT US J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first-class business in a first-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. About The Team Our professionals in our Corporate Functions cover a diverse range of areas from finance and risk to human resources and marketing. Our corporate teams are an essential part of our compan

data.title: Chief Financial Officer

This is a critical executive role reporting directly to the CEO, responsible for shaping financial strategy, ensuring robust financial governance, and driving sustainable growth across the organization. to the CEO, Board, and senior leadership on financial performance and growth opportunities Own and manage the full P&L, ensuring strong financial discipline and performance Financial Planning, Budgeting & Reporting Oversee financial planning, forecasting, and budgeting processes (short- and long-term) Ensure accurate, timely, and compliant financial reporting (monthly, quarterly, annual) Develop and monitor key financial performance metrics and dashboards Treasury & Investment Oversight Oversee treasury operations, liquidity management, and funding strategy Develop contingency funding plans in collaboration with treasury leadership Review and recommend investment strategies and portfolio performance Accounting, Controls & Compliance Ensure robust financial controls, accounting policies, and reporting frameworks Oversee general accounting, including AP/AR, payroll, and reconciliations Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements, including zakat and tax obligations data governance Drive continuous improvement in financial processes and efficiency Stakeholder Management Engage with Board members, senior management, and regulatory stakeholders Present financial performance, forecasts, and strategic recommendations Build strong relationships across the organisation to support decision-making About You Experience & Qualifications: MUST BE A SAUDI NATIONAL services, or fintech Strong experience in financial strategy, treasury, budgeting, and reporting Deep understanding of P&L, balance sheet, and cash flow management Proven ability to lead and develop high-performing finance teams Skills & Competencies: Strong leadership and strategic thinking capability Excellent analytical and decision-making skills High level of integrity, professionalism, and accountability Strong communication and stakeholder management skills Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced, evolving environment What Sets You Apart Demonstrated agility, sound judgment, and a forward-thinking mindset A collaborative and humble leadership style Strong focus on delivering value and sustainable outcomes Ability to balance strategic vision with operational execution Why Apply? data.title: Chief Financial Officer
data.jd_text: We are seeking a highly experienced Chief Financial Officer to lead financial strategy, governance, and performance management across a growing, multi entity organisation. This is a senior leadership role with direct interaction with the CEO and executive management, offering the opportunity to shape financial strategy and drive sustainable growth. The ideal candidate will bring strategic financial leadership with multi-unit oversight, advanced financial modelling and performance management skills, a strong focus on governance, compliance, and risk management, and executive level communication and stakeholder management. High ethical standards and integrity are essential. This role offers the chance to lead the finance function at a senior, strategic level, shape financial governance and controls, work with a dynamic leadership team driving transformation, and benefit from competitive compensation with performance based incentives. Please note this position is open to Saudi nationals only. Key Responsibilities: Develop and implement corporate financial strategy aligned with organisational objectives. Lead budgeting, forecasting, and long-term financial planning for multiple business units. Oversee financial control, internal governance, and compliance across the organisation. Monitor P&L performance, identify optimisation opportunities, and enhance profitability. Manage treasury, cash flow, and working capital to support operational and strategic goals. Provide financial analysis and scenario modelling to inform executive decision-making. Lead, mentor, and develop finance, accounting, and treasury teams. Support expansion initiatives, feasibility studies, and investment evaluation. Qualifications & Experience: Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field; MBA preferred. Professional certification (CPA, ACCA, CMA, or equivalent) required. Minimum 15 years of progressive financial leadership experience, ideally in multi-entity organisations. Strong experience in financial control, governance, and risk management. Proven track record in leading executive finance teams and delivering strategic insights.

data.title: Chief Revenue Officer – E-Sports & Mega-Events
data.jd_text: Job Description: The Chief Revenue Officer (CRO) – E-Sports & Mega-Events is a pivotal executive role responsible for establishing comprehensive revenue generation strategies that align with the overarching business objectives of the organization. The CRO will lead the development and execution of innovative business models tailored to capitalize on current and emerging trends in the e-sports and mega-events sectors. This role demands a visionary leader with a proven track record in driving revenue growth, utilizing market insights to inform strategic decisions, and forging impactful partnerships that elevate the organization's presence and profitability within the competitive landscape. The successful candidate will demonstrate exceptional leadership capabilities, able to coordinate cross-functional teams to optimize sales performance, enhance customer engagement, and ensure the effective promotion of our brand at key events. Job Requirements: A minimum of 15 years of executive-level experience in revenue generation and sales strategy within the e-sports, entertainment, or related industries. Proven ability to develop and implement comprehensive revenue strategies that significantly boost financial performance. Exceptional understanding of market dynamics, consumer behavior, and competitive landscape specific to e-sports and mega-events. Demonstrated experience in establishing and nurturing high-value partnerships to enhance revenue streams and brand visibility. Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex data and drive business decisions based on insights. Foster a culture of continuous improvement and innovation within the sales team to meet organizational goals. Job Responsibilities: Design and execute a multi-faceted revenue generation strategy focusing on e-sports and mega-events that aligns with the company’s strategic objectives. Oversee all aspects of revenue operations, including sales forecasting, pipeline management, and budget allocation to maximize profitability. Lead cross-functional collaboration among marketing, product development, and sales teams to ensure cohesive strategies and efficient execution. Build and manage relationships with key stakeholders, partners, and sponsors to create new revenue opportunities and enhance brand partnerships. Monitor industry trends and competitor activities to identify growth opportunities and inform strategic adjustments. Establish performance metrics that drive accountability and continuous improvement within the revenue generation teams. Act as a spokesperson for the organization at industry events, effectively representing the organization’s vision and revenue strategies. Required Skills: Strategic thinking with strong business acumen to identify new revenue opportunities in the dynamic e-sports landscape. Advanced negotiation and communication skills for effective partnership development and stakeholder engagement. Excellent leadership abilities to inspire and influence cross-functional teams towards achieving revenue goals. Proficiency in data analytics and business intelligence tools for informed decision-making and performance tracking. Strong budgetary and financial management skills to ensure allocated resources are utilized effectively for revenue growth. Ability to foster a collaborative team environment that encourages innovative thinking and new approaches to revenue generation. Results-oriented mindset with a passion for achieving and exceeding ambitious business targets in a fast-paced environment.

data.title: Inverto | Business & Operations Senior Manager
data.jd_text: Who We Are At Inverto in Riyadh, we’re defining what’s next in procurement and supply chain—driven by ambition, precision, and impact. With the strength of BCG behind us and a sharp entrepreneurial mindset at our core, we partner with leading global organizations to deliver strategies that create lasting value. Our people are central to that mission. As experts who move with intent and momentum, we continually invest in developing our capabilities, expanding our reach, and scaling our impact. Growth at Inverto is shared growth-opening new opportunities for our clients, our business, and our teams. What You'll Do The Business & Operations Senior Manager drives the effective operationalization of Inverto’s regional growth agenda by leading key commercial, people, financial, and operational processes for the Middle East. Acting as a strategic partner to Inverto leadership and EMESA teams, the role ensures transparency in business development activities, enables strong go-to-market execution, and supports high-quality decision-making through robust performance and financial management. It plays a central role in shaping and sustaining talent and team development, driving a cohesive and high-performing culture, and ensuring optimal staffing and resource allocation. A key aspect of the role is ensuring alignment between BCG core and Inverto-specific requirements, in accordance with Inverto’s global guardrails, and business model. Overall, the role is critical in enabling the Middle East business to operate effectively, grow sustainably, and achieve its strategic objectives. data.title: Testing & Commissioning Section Head (HQ)
data.jd_text: Job Description The Testing & Commissioning Section Head (E2) is responsible for leading and managing all testing, validation, and commissioning activities across large-scale projects, ensuring full compliance with client requirements, operational standards, and regulatory obligations. The role oversees the preparation, review, and execution of commissioning plans, coordinates closely with contractors, engineers, operators, and authorities, and ensures all systems are properly integrated, tested, and ready for handover. With extensive experience, the position also drives risk management, stakeholder coordination, team leadership, and adherence to HSE standards while ensuring timely project completion, proper documentation, and successful transition to operations. Responsibilities Managing the integration and validation activities of the Contractor, together with the validation of all Test and Commissioning plans and test procedures for the program. Lead, direct and supervise a team of discipline Engineers in preparation and execution of commissioning of work Location. Assuming entire responsibility for the completion of the implementation in conformance with the client’s needs and project standards. Plan, organize and supervise works to ensure all works performed within the parameters of the project schedule. Liaison with the Systems Engineering team for engineering support and control. Coordinate with the Operator, Civil Defiance, EXPRO and other third parties related to all necessary testing and commissioning requirements for the project. Ensure all testing and commissioning works are comprehensive and discussed with stakeholders to ensure best implementation. Approval of testing and commissioning works. Participate in negotiations with regulatory agencies in public meetings with the client. Initiate risk assessment of testing and commissioning works, review and endorse risk assessment results and recommend course of action to ensure mitigation of risks. Attend to final inspections on completed projects and initiate Contract Completion Certificates and Handover Certificates Coordinates all activities for the successful Commissioning of the project within project schedules and contractual requirements. Oversees all coordination between Client, Contractor, vendors, Engineering, sub-contractors and any other external or internal agency to ensure the smooth Commissioning of the process plant. Review calibration of testing equipment on site. Reviewing the testing and commissioning plans submitted by the Contractor to ensure compliance with the operational requirements of the project. Writing Testing & Commissioning reports and documentation. Ensure completion of training for maintenance and operative staff, O&M manual, spare parts and as-built drawings. Informs themselves of the relevant Quality, Environmental, Safety and Occupational Health Policies, Manuals and Procedures within Hill International and ensures continued compliance with these requirements while employed by Hill. Perform other duties as assigned by the line manager/supervisor. Qualifications A degree in engineering or scientific discipline. Minimum 20 years’ experience in large-scale projects including having worked as the testing and commissioning manager. Knowledge of all phases of construction operations relating to field activities of engineering disciplines, construction technologies and jobsite management. Experience of implementing a zero accident philosophy. Extensive knowledge of health, safety and environmental policies, processes and procedures. Knowledge of the Project Construction Environmental Control Plan. Excellent knowledge of Water and Wastewater works.

data.title: Quality Assurance Section Head (HQ)
data.jd_text: Job Description The Quality Assurance Section Head (E2) is responsible for establishing, implementing, and monitoring project-wide quality assurance systems to ensure full compliance with corporate policies, international standards, and client requirements. The role involves developing quality plans, conducting audits, identifying trends and non-conformities, and driving corrective actions to ensure that all project deliverables meet defined quality standards. With extensive experience, the position also provides leadership in quality management, supports continuous improvement initiatives, and ensures effective reporting, training, and stakeholder coordination across all project phases. Responsibilities Responsible for monitoring the implementation of the Projects’ Management System in compliance with the Hill Corporate policies, International Standards and Client's requirements. Develops quality assurance plans by conducting hazard analyses and monitoring procedures. Implementation of the Quality System may require the development or modification of corporate procedures. As per project needs, the development of a Project-specific Quality Plan, as a roadmap of the Project Quality Requirements, is recommended. The Quality Assurance Manager monitors the establishment by the service provider of the quality assurance (QA) programs sufficient for the Project. Monitors and audits the quality-related activities set forth in the Quality Plans to confirm that products or services provided are in accordance with the client's requirements. Periodically conducts detailed audits to assess the level of compliance with the QA systems, procedures, and related quality activities. Regularly interacts with the Project Director and Area Manager, and other staff members to assess on-site activities. Identifies and reports to the Project Director and -as per needs- to the Area Manager and the Area Quality Manager on quality issues or trends based on data from a variety of sources. Supports implementation of appropriate corrective action to quality issues. Validates quality processes by establishing product specifications and quality attributes. Provides input to management on projects performance metrics and process or procedural system improvements. Trains and motivates employees to ensure that quality standards are met. Requires the ability to communicate clearly, and interface effectively with a diverse group of professionals. Prepares quality documentation and reports by collecting, analyzing, and summarizing information and trends. Informs themselves of the relevant Quality, Environmental, Safety and Occupational Health Policies, Manuals and Procedures in place within Hill International, and ensures continued compliance with these requirements while employed by Hill. Perform other duties as assigned by the line manager/supervisor. Qualifications Bachelor in engineering/management from an accredited university. Minimum 20 years’ professional experience including as quality manager for large-scale projects. Certified as a Lead Auditor. Broad knowledge of the quality principles/tools and their application to engineering, environmental, procurement, construction, testing, operations, or decommissioning. Knowledge of the application of corrective actions to reconcile nonconforming conditions. Knowledge of regulatory requirements and functions which impact the quality program/plan. Proficiency in verbal and written communications. Proficiency in providing direction and on the job training to assigned personnel. Experience in directing project audit activities
