data.title: Chief Transformation Officer – Public Sector & Ministries
data.jd_text: Job Description: The Chief Transformation Officer (CTO) – Public Sector & Ministries will spearhead the strategic initiatives aimed at modernizing and enhancing the effectiveness and efficiency of public services within governmental and ministerial entities. This C-suite executive will be responsible for the comprehensive transformation agenda, leveraging innovative methodologies and frameworks to foster a culture of agility, collaboration, and outcome-oriented performance. The CTO will partner with key stakeholders, including senior officials and community leaders, to align transformation initiatives with organizational objectives and constituent needs, ensuring a seamless transition from legacy systems to integrated, technology-driven solutions. The role demands exceptional leadership capabilities to cultivate high-performing teams, drive stakeholder engagement, and promote an equitable and inclusive approach throughout the transformation process. The ideal candidate will embody a strong vision for public service excellence, facilitating a mindset shift that redefines the interface between government entities and the public. Job Requirements: Minimum of 15 years of progressive executive experience in public administration, transformation management, or related fields, preferably within the public sector. Proven track record in leading transformative initiatives at a C-suite level, demonstrating substantial impact on organizational performance, service delivery, and operational efficiency. Extensive knowledge of government policies, regulatory frameworks, and public sector dynamics that influence transformation efforts. Strong analytical and strategic thinking skills, with the ability to synthesize complex information into actionable plans. Excellent communication skills, including the ability to articulate a compelling vision and effectively engage diverse stakeholder groups. Demonstrated competence in change management methodologies and the ability to lead organizations through periods of significant transition. Experience with data-driven decision-making, including the capability to leverage analytics and performance metrics to guide transformation initiatives. Strong interpersonal skills that foster collaboration and consensus-building across varied entities and community stakeholders. Proficiency in leading cross-functional teams in a matrix organizational structure, demonstrating adaptability to different working styles and team dynamics. Job Responsibilities: Develop and implement a comprehensive transformation strategy that aligns with the strategic objectives of public sector organizations and ministries. Collaborate with senior leadership to define and articulate the vision for transformation, ensuring that it resonates with internal and external stakeholders. Assess current operational frameworks and identify opportunities for improved processes, technologies, and resource allocations to enhance service delivery. Foster a culture of continuous improvement, innovation, and accountability throughout public sector organizations by championing best practices and emerging technologies. Engage with government officials, community leaders, and external partners to ensure stakeholder alignment on transformation goals and metrics. Monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of transformation initiatives through regular assessments, adjusting strategies as necessary to achieve desired outcomes. Act as a key spokesperson for transformation initiatives, representing the organization at public forums, conferences, and stakeholder meetings. Lead and mentor cross-functional teams, promoting professional development and building leadership capacities within the organization. Champion initiatives that promote equity, diversity, and inclusion throughout all transformation efforts, ensuring diverse perspectives are integrated into decision-making. Prepare and present regular status updates to the board and legislative bodies, highlighting progress, challenges, and strategic adjustments to transformation initiatives. Required Skills: Exceptional leadership skills with a proven ability to influence and inspire, leading large-scale organizational change. Strong expertise in transformation methodologies, including Agile, Lean Six Sigma, and other relevant frameworks. Advanced project management skills, with proficiency in facilitating complex projects from conception through execution and evaluation. High-level negotiation and conflict resolution skills to navigate diverse stakeholder interests and drive consensus. In-depth knowledge of emerging technologies relevant to public service transformation, including AI, data analytics, and digital platforms. Ability to create and sustain collaborative partnerships across government, non-profit, and private sectors for integrated transformation efforts. Financial acumen to manage budgets effectively and ensure resource allocation aligns with strategic priorities. Strong advocacy

data.title: Category Excellence & Customer Development OT Senior Manager
data.jd_text: Overview The Category Excellence Manager is responsible for leading and enhancing the category growth and activation agenda across Modern Trade, E-Commerce, and Away-From-Home (AFH) channels. The role adapts worldwide category management and customer engagements best practices to the KSA market, ensuring proper rollout and adoption. It leads the World Class Customer Engagement (WCCE) model, drives cross-functional alignment across Sales, RGM, Marketing and E-Com, and ensures execution excellence in major commercial events (e.g. World Cup, formula 1, Ramadan). The manager also supports capability building across field and head-office teams to deliver profitable, insight-led category growth. Responsibilities Develop the multi-channel category capability-building strategy, with a detailed annual rollout and scorecard. Partner with E-Commerce and AFH teams to embed category leadership principles and 4P strategies across all trade channels. Lead the development of customer-and event-based toolkits (assortment, content/visibility, pricing & promotions) Co-own national event planning (World Cup. Formula 1, Ramadan, national Day) with Marketing and Sales for unified execution. Ensure regular review and organization of KPIs across MT, E-Com, and AFH performance dashboards. Coach Key Account Managers on profitable account management, leveraging analytics and shopper insights. Collaborate with the RGM and OT Excellence teams to integrate pricing, pack, and promotional effectiveness in trade plans. Document post-initiative learnings and share best practices to strengthen category execution capabilities across teams. Qualifications 8-10 years of FMCG experience in Sales, Category Management, or Trade Marketing, with exposure to E-Commerce and/or AFH. Strong commercial awareness and understanding of channel economics, shopper behavior, and event-based execution. Advanced analytical and problem-solving skills with proficiency in EPOS, Nielsen, Power BI, and digital commerce dashboards. Demonstrated functional expertise and a proven track record of delivering results in complex multi-channel environments. Strong verbal and written communication skills with the ability to lead discussions and influence across all levels. Excellent relationship building and collaboration skills, with internal stakeholders (Sales, RGM, Marketing) and external partners (Retailers, Platforms, Distributors.) Project management capability-able to lead cross functional teams, build critical paths, and deliver initiatives on time. Strong organizational ability to manage a high workload, multiple priorities, and deliver consistent outcomes. Experience in event-based commercial planning (e.g. Ramadan, formula 1, World Cup). Fluent in English and Arabic with excellent presentation and facilitation skills. Strong interpersonal and leadership skills, able to motivate and develop teams while maintaining collaborative collaborative relationship across functions. Results-driven and strategically minded, demonstrating integrity, sound judgment, and discipline planning in execution.

data.title: HEAD, DIGITAL TECHNOLOGY
data.jd_text: Primary Purpose Of The Job Play a pivotal role in QatarEnergy’s digital transformation by leading the delivery of innovative digital solutions that optimize operations, enhance safety, and unlock new business value. As Head of Digital Technology, you will guide a dynamic team in designing and implementing cutting-edge Cloud AI, and analytics initiatives. You will work closely with business leaders, IT, and global technology partners to ensure digital excellence. By championing Agile methodologies, you will accelerate innovation, enable rapid delivery, and foster a culture of continuous improvement Required Experience And Skills Strategic Leadership: 15+ years of progressive experience in Oil & Gas (Operations, Production, Maintenance, Engineering, Drilling & Wells, Reservoir, or Geosciences), with at least 6 years in digital transformation roles. Agile Delivery: Demonstrated experience leading digital teams and programs using Agile methodologies, with a focus on rapid prototyping, iterative delivery, and measurable business outcomes. Digital Innovation: Proven success in leading large-scale digital programs, including Data Analytics, AI, and ML on the cloud within the energy sector. Change Management: Demonstrated ability to drive digital adoption, foster a culture of innovation, and lead cross-functional teams through change. Business Impact: Strong track record of identifying and delivering high-impact digital use cases with measurable ROI. Financial Acumen: Experience managing digital budgets, vendor relationships, and performance metrics. Industry Insight: Up-to-date knowledge of IoT, automation, cybersecurity, and sustainability trends in Oil & Gas. Collaboration & Communication: Exceptional interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence and inspire stakeholders at all levels. Educational Qualifications Master’s degree in Science, Engineering, Computer Science, Oil & Gas, Energy Management, or a related field is preferred. Candidates with a Bachelor’s degree and extensive (15+ years) relevant experience will also be considered. Advanced degrees (Master’s or PhD) are highly desirable. Professional certifications in digital transformation, cloud, AI, Agile, or related technologies are an advantage.

data.title: Asset & Wealth Management - Doha - Vice President - Lead Security Engineering
data.jd_text: Job Description Role Overview The Lead Information Security Officer for Asset Management is a critical leadership position responsible for defining, implementing, and overseeing the comprehensive information security and cybersecurity risk posture specifically within the Asset Management Private business. This role is pivotal in balancing commercial objectives with robust security controls, ensuring the division's resilience against an evolving threat landscape, and protecting client assets and data. This leader will directly manage and provide strategic direction to teams responsible for Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC), Application Security & Advisory, and Product Security functions within Asset Management. Crucially, this role also involves the oversight and guidance of embedded Technology Risk Officers who are assigned to various Asset Management Private business vertical. The objective is to foster a unified and proactive approach to risk management, ensuring regulatory compliance, and enabling secure technological innovation across all Asset Management initiatives. Key Responsibilities Work with internal application development teams that are developing the next generation of critical business applications, enable them to understand Information Security and Business Resiliency control requirements, and advise on the integration of these controls into their applications Collaborate with the global Application Security Risk, Business Continuity, Risk Measurement, and other global Technology Risk teams to develop and integrate best-in-class security and resiliency controls and practices. Communicate the impact of technology risks and the approach to mitigation/acceptance and provide risk assessment and advisory services to technology engineers, and technology and business management. Assess existing applications for design-related security risks and assist teams in determining appropriate remediation steps Provide guidance to engineering leadership and application developers on existing and emerging threats in the web and mobile application space. Drive adoption of embedded application security controls as part of the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC). Provide deep subject matter expertise to application teams in secure application design and development approaches and techniques. Contribute to the technical understanding, adoption and convergence of information security standards, solutions and tools. Work with engineers to develop customized security testing strategy to complement the existing security testing program managed by Technology Risk. Skills And Experience Required Experience: 5+ years of progressive experience in Multi domain Information Security experience such as vendor security, application security, vulnerability management, data loss prevention, data encryption, and infrastructure security. Regulatory & Risk Expertise: Expert knowledge of global financial regulations (e.g., SEC , FINRA , GDPR , CCPA ) and proven experience applying risk management methodologies such as FAIR (Factor Analysis of Information Risk) or similar frameworks. Leadership & Management: Proven ability to build, mentor, and lead high-performing global teams of security professionals. Program Management: Proven track record involving collaboration with engineering, technology, second line risk functions and audit partners to deliver projects and facilitate resolution of audit issues within committed timelines. Communication: Exceptional written and oral communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical risks and solutions clearly to both technical and executive audiences. Risk Management: Expertise in performing risk assessments, identifying gaps in compliance with information security policies, and recommending effective mitigation strategies. Security Standards: Familiarity with leading security standards and frameworks such as NIST, OWASP, SANS Top 20, PCI DSS, and CIS Controls. Technical Depth: Expertise in Technology Risk data analytics (metrics reporting and dashboarding) and Reviewing Software Development Lifecycle best practices e.g., code reviews, vulnerability scan report analysis to advise application development teams on for secure practices and frameworks, and other application security best practices Preferred Qualifications BS or MS degree in Computer Science, Cyber Security, Information Security, or a related technical field. Relevant industry certifications such as CISSP , CISM , CRISC , CISA, or cloud-specific security certifications (e.g., AWS Certified Security – Specialty). Experience with leveraging AI/ML to solve security problems and scale operations. Knowledge of secure coding languages (e.g., Python, Java, Go). About Goldman Sachs At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. H

data.title: Chief Financial Officer
data.jd_text: Role Purpose Lead financial strategy, capital structuring, investment governance, and risk management for a multi-country mining business. Build the finance function from the ground up and support large-scale growth and value creation. Key Responsibilities Define and execute financial strategy, capital structure, and funding roadmap Lead investment evaluation across exploration, development, and production assets Drive capital allocation and project financial governance Structure and secure project financing and investor partnerships Oversee financial planning, budgeting, and performance management Ensure accurate accounting, reporting, and strong internal controls Develop tax strategy and ensure regulatory compliance across jurisdictions Manage treasury, cash flow, commodity hedging, and financial risks Oversee joint ventures, partnerships, and stakeholder reporting Implement enterprise risk management across mining operations Build and scale the finance organization and systems Experience 15–20 years in finance leadership 7–10 years in mining / natural resources Experience in project finance, large capex projects, and cross-border operations Background in CFO, Finance Director, Project Finance, or Mining Advisory roles Education Degree in Finance, Accounting, or Economics Professional qualification (CA / CPA / ACCA / CMA preferred) Core Competencies Strong capital allocation and financial strategy mindset Expertise in mining financials and project economics Ability to manage commodity and market risks Leadership in building teams and managing stakeholders High resilience, governance focus, and strategic thinking Special Requirement Fluency in both Spanish and English. Prior experience in the mining industry is strongly preferred. Balanced professional background with approximately 50% focus on investment strategy and financial analysis, and 50% on P&L management and core finance operations. Industry: Mining & Natural Resources Business: New Mining Vertical (Large Conglomerate) Scope: Multi-country (Exploration → Development → Production → Trading)

data.title: Chief Financial Officer
data.jd_text: Purpose The Chief Financial Officer (CFO) is responsible for the overall financial stewardship of Ooredoo Fintech, ensuring robust financial governance, regulatory compliance, and operational discipline across the organization, in a fast growing, multi country environment. The role supports the company’s continued growth and operational scale by maintaining strong financial controls, reliable reporting, and efficient financial operations. In addition, the CFO will play a key role in driving transformation within the finance function, strengthening processes, systems, and capabilities to support a maturing fintech platform and an evolving regulatory environment. This is a hands-on role suited to a leader with a builder mentality, highly structured, with a strong personal presence and gravitas. Context OFT is an innovative and dynamic scale-up fintech company. We offer the heritage and stability of a hugely successful founding group combined with the unlimited ambition and passion of a talented team building intuitive, secure and convenient money management and payment tools for everyday people. The company operates digital wallet and payment services, including remittances, card transactions, airtime top-ups, and payroll flows. Maintaining customer trust, regulatory licence adherence, and financial resilience is critical. The business currently operates in 4 countries (Qatar, Oman, Tunisia and Maldives) and it is building in 3 additional countries (Iraq, Algeria, Kuwait). The business is the most ambitious fintech business sin the MENA region and is run with a highly centralised operating model, and high reusability of centrally defined process, systems and procedures. Working with us presents a unique and exciting opportunity to make a difference and be part of a revolution where we are leveraging financial technology to provide accessible, empowering tools to consumer and merchants. Role Accountabilities 1. Financial Strategy & Business Partnership Define and lead the financial strategy of the business, ensuring full alignment with corporate objectives, growth ambitions, and regulatory expectations. Act as a trusted strategic advisor to the CEO and Executive Committee, providing forward-looking financial insight to support product innovation, market expansion, and capital deployment decisions Drive long-term financial planning, including capital allocation, funding strategy, and value creation across markets and product lines. 2. Financial Governance, Control & Reporting Establish and maintain a best-in-class financial control environment, ensuring full compliance with IFRS and all applicable local regulatory requirements. Oversee the timely and accurate delivery of management accounts, board reporting, statutory financial statements, and regulatory submissions Ensure audit readiness at all times, with robust governance frameworks, policies, and controls embedded across the organisation. Own the integrity, consistency, and transparency of all financial data and reporting. 3. Finance Transformation, Systems & Scalability Lead the transformation of the finance function into a scalable, technology-enabled, and data-driven organisation. Design and implement robust, end-to-end processes and control frameworks that support growth, multi-country operations, and increasing regulatory complexity. Champion automation, system optimisation, and advanced analytics to improve efficiency, accuracy, and decision-making capability. Partner closely with Product and Technology teams to ensure financial architecture, reconciliation tools, and data flows are fit for scale and future innovation. 4. Financial Operations & Liquidity Management Provide executive oversight of all financial operations, including reconciliations, settlement processes, escrow management, and partner funding flows. Ensure robust safeguarding of customer funds, strict adherence to settlement SLAs, and effective fund segregation practices Lead liquidity management, including cash forecasting, working capital optimisation, and foreign exchange exposure management. Maintain operational resilience across all financial processes, ensuring continuity under stress scenarios. 5. Regulatory Engagement & Compliance Serve as the primary finance interface with regulators, central banks, auditors, and external stakeholders. Ensure full compliance with all licensing conditions, regulatory reporting requirements, and fintech/payment services regulations. Oversee financial aspects of AML/FCC frameworks in collaboration with Risk and Compliance functions. Proactively manage regulatory relationships, anticipating changes and ensuring the organisation remains ahead of evolving requirements. 6. Performance Management & Value Creation Lead all FP&A activities, including budgeting, forecasting, and performance tracking across markets and products. Translate complex financial and transactional data into clear, actionable insights for senior management and the Board. Drive dis
